Lucid Rewards Lucid Level AchievedRewardQuantityTradeabilityDuration10Nightmare Dreamcatcher Voucher3UntradeableExpires on June 17, 2026 at 11:59 PM15Nightmare Dreamcatcher Voucher320Selective Dreamy Equipment Box (Epic)125Nightmare Dreamcatcher Voucher330Mystery Dreamy Crystal Box (Epic)235Nightmare Dreamcatcher Voucher340Mystery Dreamy Crystal Box (Unique)145Nightmare Dreamcatcher Voucher550Nightmare Dreamcatcher Voucher555Selective Dreamy Equipment Box (Unique)160Mystery Dreamy Crystal Box (Unique)265Nightmare Dreamcatcher Voucher570Selective Dreamy Crystal Box (Unique)275Nightmare Dreamcatcher Voucher580Mystery Dreamy Crystal Box (Legendary)185Nightmare Dreamcatcher Voucher1090Selective Dreamy Equipment Box (Legendary)195Nightmare Dreamcatcher Voucher10100Selective Dreamy Crystal Box (Legendary)3 [Back to Overview] Nightmare Forest Event Participation Requirements: A character participating in the Change Burning: Lucid event. You can click ‘Nightmare Forest’ in the Event List to move to the ‘Nightmare Forest: Forest Entrance’ map. When you enter ‘Nightmare Forest’ you will be transformed into Lucid, and you cannot cancel the transformation while inside. You can talk to the ‘Nightmare Light Cluster’ NPC or use the portal in the center of the map to open the Nightmare Forest UI. You can click the ‘Enter’ button to explore inside the forest. You need 1 or more ‘Nightmare Dreamcatchers’ to explore the Nightmare Forest. Nightmare Dream Catchers ‘Nightmare Dreamcatchers’ are a resource required to explore the Nightmare Forest. You can check how many you have through the Nightmare Forest UI. You will be provided 5 ‘Nightmare Dreamcatchers’ per day, and they will be given out even on days you don’t login. You can hold up to 30 ‘Nightmare Dreamcatchers’. If the quantity would exceed this amount, you will not be provided any more. Any undistributed ‘Nightmare Dreamcatchers’ can be purchased laster for 30m mesos each. Nightmare Forest Exploration When you enter Nightmare Forest, you will automatically enter the stage that matches Lucid’s level. Lucid LevelNightmare Forest Stage1~19Stage 120~39Stage 240~59Stage 360~79Stage 480~100Stage 5 Nightmare Forest consists of a total of 2 stages. In ‘Nightmare Forest: Deep Forest’, you must defeat the ‘Forest Predators’ and ‘Forest Minion’ monsters that appear. This will create a portal on the right side of the map, which you can touch with your character to move to the next stage. ※ You cannot enter the next stage until you defeat all of the monsters. ※ You can click the ‘Exit’ button on the left side of the screen to exit back to the ‘Nightmare Forest: Forest Entrance’ map. ※ If you exit partway through, your stage progress will not be saved and your number of ‘Nightmare Dreamcatchers’ will not be deducted. In ‘Nightmare Forest: Deepest Part of the Forest’, you must defeat the ‘Source of the Forest’ monster to clear. 1 ‘Nightmare Dreamcatcher’ will be deducted at this time. When you clear all of the stages, a portal will appear on the right side of the ‘Nightmare Forest: Deepest Part of the Forest’ map. If your character touches the portal, you can retry exploring the Nightmare Forest. ※ However, if you do not have any ‘Nightmare Dreamcatchers’, you will be returned to the ‘Forest Entrance’. When you clear all of the stages, you can return to the ‘Forest Entrance’ by clicking the ‘Exit’ button on the left side of the screen or by talking to the ‘Nightmare Light Cluster’ NPC. Source of the Forest and Event Rewards The ‘Source of the Forest’ is a boss monster that appears in ‘Nightmare Forest: Deepest Part of the Forest’. It has a total of 5 tier. When you attack the ‘Source of the Forest’, it will evolve into the next tier based on the current Nightmare Forest stage and a set probability. The probability of reaching the final ‘Source of the Forest’ tier based on the Nightmare Forest stage is as follows. Nightmare Forest StageNormalRareEpicUniqueLegendaryStage 155.000 %40.000 %4.350 %0.649 %0.001 %Stage 210.000 %55.000 %33.000 %1.998 %0.002 %Stage 3–19.000 %65.000 %15.000 %1.000 %Stage 4–3.000 %55.000 %40.500 %1.500 %Stage 5––44.000 %52.000 %4.000 % When you defeat the ‘Source of the Forest’, you can acquire Dreamy equipment, Dreamy Crystals, and Dreamy Equipment Fragments based on a set probability. Higher tier ‘Source of the Forest’ will drop higher tier items. ※ ‘Dreamy Equipment’ are exclusive equipment that can only be used by Lucid. ※ ‘Dreamy Crystals’ are additional parts that can be equipped to ‘Dreamy Equipment’. ※ ‘Dreamy Equipment Fragments’ are materials required to enhance and craft ‘Dreamy Equipment’. [Back to Overview] Dreamy Equipment Event Participation Requirements: A character participating in the Change Burning: Lucid event. Dreamy Equipment UI You can select ‘Change Burning Management’ in the Event List then press the [Inventory] button or transform into Lucid and use the ‘Lucid Inventory’ skill to open the Lucid exclusive equipment UI. You can manage and equip your ‘Dreamy Equipment’ and ‘Dreamy Crystals’ with the menu consisting of Equipment Enhancement, Equipment Crafting, Equipment Disassembly, Dreamy Crystal Socketing, and Dreamy Crystal Fusion. Dreamy Equipment Dreamy Equipment Types: TypeItem NameStatWeaponDreamy MessengerMagic AttackRingDreamy SourceCritical RatePendantDreamy BoundaryMax HP Dreamy Equipment Tiers: Equipment TierMaximum Enhancement StageNumber of Equippable Dreamy Crystal SlotsNormalCannot be enhancedCannot be equippedRare+12 Stage1Epic2Unique3Legendary4 Dreamy Equipment Enhancement Dreamy equipment can be enhanced up to +12. You can succeed or fail based on set rates. When you enhance equipment, you will use ‘Dreamy Equipment Fragments’ as material. You can acquire Dreamy equipment, Dreamy Crystals, and Dreamy Equipment Fragments from clearing ‘Change Burning: Nightmare Forest’. You can acquire Dreamy Equipment Fragments by disassembling Dreamy equipment. ※ Normal tier equipment cannot be enhanced. Dreamy Equipment Crafting You can use Dreamy Equipment Fragments to craft a specific equipment type and tier of your choice. The higher the crafted equipment’s tier, the more Dreamy Equipment Fragments will be consumed. You can consume an additional amount of Dreamy Equipment Fragments to increase the crafting success rate. You can acquire Dreamy equipment, Dreamy Crystals, and Dreamy Equipment Fragments from clearing ‘Change Burning: Nightmare Forest’. You can acquire Dreamy Equipment Fragments by disassembling Dreamy equipment. Equipment Disassembly You can acquire Dreamy Equipment Fragments by disassembling Dreamy equipment. You can use Dreamy Equipment Fragments to enhance and craft equipment. The number of Dreamy Equipment Fragments acquired will be based on the equipment’s enhancement stage and tier. If you disassemble equipment with Dreamy Crystals equipped, the Dreamy Crystals will automatically be returned to you. Dreamy Crystals Dreamy Crystal Stats: Possible StatsMagic Attack% Magic AttackMax HP% Max HPMasteryCritical RateCritical DamageSkill Cooldown Decrease (Can only get up to -9 from Dreamy Crystal stats, skill cooldowns cannot be decreased lower than 5 seconds) Dreamy Crystal Tiers: Dreamy Crystal stat values are based on the tier. Dreamy Crystal stats are determined based on a set probability. Dreamy Crystal TierNumber of StatsRare1Epic1Unique2Legendary2 Dreamy Crystal Socketing You can socket any tier of crystal on any tier of Dreamy Equipment. You can socket duplicate crystals of the same tier. Dreamy Crystal Fusion You can combine 3 Dreamy Crystals of the same tier to craft a higher tier Dreamy Crystal. The success rate is based on the tier of Dreamy Crystals being combined. You can succeed or fail based on a set probability. When combining Legendary tier crytals, the tier will remain the same and only the stats will change. For Unique and Legendary tier crystals,the same stats can appear. [Back to Overview] Dreamy Trials Event Participation Requirements: A character participating in the Change Burning: Lucid event. Dreamy Trials You can select ‘Dreamy Trials’ in the Event List to check the missions. The Dreamy Trials consist of a total of 5 missions, and you can complete them by defeating specific bosses as the Change Burning: Lucid character. When you complete a mission, you can receive the rewards corresponding to that mission in the Dreamy Trials UI. Boss Missions: Mission NumberMission Details1Defeat Zakum (Chaos)2Defeat Vellum (Chaos)3Defeat Lucid (Hard)4Defeat Helena (Normal)5Defeat Helena (Nightmare) ※ You can select the desired boss in the Dreamy Trials while in a party of 1 or not in a party to move to the entry waiting map. ※ You can freely challenge and re-challenge all of the bosses in any order. ※ The bosses that appear in the Dreamy Trials are event exclusive monsters. They do not count in the ‘weekly boss defeat limit’ and do not drop what the normal bosses drop when defeated. ※ If the character participating in the event World Leaps, the event progress will be maintained and they can proceed with the event in the world they leaped to. ※ Helena will be added in the April update. My Character Rewards: Mission DetailsMy Character RewardTradeabilityDurationZakum (Chaos)Change Lucidroid VoucherCan only be put into storageExpires on June 18, 2026 at 2:00 AMVellum (Chaos)Dream Light Bangle VoucherLucid (Hard)Master of Nightmares Medal VoucherHelena (Normal)Fluffy Helena Doll VoucherLUCID Title VoucherHelena (Nightmare)Dreamy Butterfly Finish Attack Effect Voucher ※ The ‘My Character’ rewards will expire on June 18, 2026 at 2:00 AM. ※ The ‘My Character’ rewards can only be put into storage. ※ The ‘My Character’ rewards can only be claimed 1 time per Maple ID. Lucid Rewards: Mission DetailsLucid RewardTradeabilityDurationZakum (Chaos)Mystery Dreamy Crystal Box (Epic)UntradeableExpires on June 17, 2026 at 11:59 PMVellum (Chaos)Mystery Dreamy Crystal Box (Unique)Lucid (Hard)Selective Dreamy Equipment Box (Unique)Helena (Normal)Selective Dreamy Crystal Box (Unique)Helena (Nightmare)Selective Dreamy Crystal Box (Legendary) ※ The ‘Lucid’ rewards will expire on June 17, 2026 at 11:59 PM. ※ The ‘Lucid’ rewards can only be claimed 1 time per character participating in the event. If you delete the Change Burning: Lucid character then participate on another character, you can claim the Lucid rewards again. [Back to Overview] Dream Shard Shop Event Participation Requirements: A character participating in the Change Burning: Lucid event. ※ The Dream Shard Shop can be used by all characters level 200 or higher on the Maple ID with a character participating in the Change Burning: Lucid event. You can select ‘Lucid Dream’ in the Event List and click the ‘Open Shop’ button in the [Dream Gifts] tab or select ‘Dream Shard Shop’ in the Event List’s coin shop tab to use the Dream Shard Shop. ※ The Dream Shard Shop purchase limits are shared between all characters in the Maple ID. Adversary’s Black Cube (25 coins) [limit of 200] Adversary’s White Additional Cube (40 coins) [limit of 200] Adversary’s Abyss Rebirth Flame (25 coins) [limit of 200] Sol Erda Fragment (15 coins) [limit of 800] Sol Erda (200 coins) [limit of 30] ※ The Adversary’s Black Cube, Adversary’s White Additional Cube, and Adversary’s Abyss Rebirth Flame can only be purchased by characters that have completed the ‘[Genesis Weapon] 2nd Liberation of Power’ quest. They can only be put into storage. ※ The Adversary’s Black Cube and Adversary’s White Additional Cube are items that can only be used on Genesis/Destiny weapons and Astra subweapons. ※ The Adversary’s Abyss Rebirth Flame is an item that can only be used on Genesis/Destiny weapons. ※ The items purchased from the Dream Shard Shop will expire on June 18, 2026 at 2:00 AM (except for the Sol Erda Fragments). ※ The Sol Erda can only by used by characters level 260 or higher that have completed the 6th job advancement. It cannot be used if you hold 20 Sol Erda. ※ The Sol Erda Fragments are untradeable and permanent items. [Back to Overview] Jin’s Mysterious Garden Roll the dice with Jin to collect Growth Fertilizer and acquire a special potion unlike anything else in the world! Event Participation Requirements A level 101 or higher character (or a Zero character that has completed Chapter 2). Event Period March 23, 2026 at 10:00 AM ~ May 13, 2026 at 11:59 PM. You can select the ‘Jin’s Mysterious Garden’ event in the Event List and click the ‘Participate’ button to start the event. Event Progress You can acquire dice by clicking the [Receive Dice] button in the UI. You can use the dice you acquire to move around a board consisting of a total of 40 spaces and collect ‘Growth Fertilizer’. You can purchase various items at the ‘Growth Fertilizer Shop’ with the ‘Growth Fertilizer’ you acquire. You can acquire various rewards based on the cumulative number of dice you roll. Acquiring Dice You can receive 3 dice per day by opening the Jin’s Mysterious Garden UI through the Event List and clicking the [Receive Dice] button. You can receive dice up to 5 times per week, and up to 40 times over the event period. ※ From March 2 at 10:00 AM until March 25 at 11:59 PM, you can only receive dice 1 time. You will be provided 15 dice all at once. You can click the [Use Pass] button to use 3,000 Maple Points and complete 1 ‘Receive Dice’ that you did not previously complete. ※ The Dice Pass can be used up to the number of claims you did not claim as of the previous week, based on the count on Thursday at 12:00 AM. ※ However, the number of Dice Passes available for the last week is limited to the number of incomplete days based on the count at 12:00 AM of the day before. The number of dice held is shared between all characters within the Maple ID. Rolling the Dice You can roll the dice by pressing the [Roll] button. You will roll 3 dice at once. After checking the dice results, you can click the [Select] button for the die you wish to move with. An arrow will indicate which space you will arrive at. If you want to use that die, you can move by pressing the [Move] button. It will move you to the indicated space. When you arrive at a space , you will acquire an item corresponding to the space or activate the effect written on it. After using all 3 rolled dice to move, you can roll the dice again. Items Acquired from Spaces Growth Fertilizer When you land on a space where the Growth Fertilizer item is placed, you will acquire Growth Fertilizer equal to the number written on the space. You can use the acquired Growth Fertilizer to purchase various items at the Growth Fertilizer Shop. If you land on or pass by the START point, you will acquire an additional 400 Growth Fertilizer as a pass bonus. You cannot acquire the pass bonus if you move backwards to land on or pass by the START point. Special Dice When you land on a space marked with a Special Dice symbol on the board, you will acquire a Special Die. After rolling normal dice, you can roll a Special Die for each result of the normal dice. The Special Dice effect is reflected in the selected dice number or amount of Fertilizer acquired on the space you land on. Special Dice Effects: NumberEffect1Dice Number X22Dice Number X33Growth Fertilizer Acquired at Arrival Space X24Dice Number -55Dice Number -106Dice Number X(-3) Space Effects The spaces are repositioned every time you land on or pass the START point. Movement Space When you land on a movement space, you will be moved forward or backward by the number of spaces indicated on the space. The movement space effect will only activate 1 time per lap. If you land on a movement space that has already been used that lap, you will acquire 100 Growth Fertilizers instead. CategoryEffectRocketMove forwards 10 spacesJump RampMove forwards 3 spacesMoleMove backwards 2 spaces, move backwards 3 spaces Question Mark Space When you land on a question mark space, you can choose one of the reward options below. CategoryEffectReward 1Acquire 200 Growth FertilizerReward 2Acquire Growth Fertilizer based on a set rate.Acquire 50: 5%Acquire 100: 45%Acquire 300: 45%Acquire 1000: 5% Monsters Gold Bee and Poison-Spraying Homunculus monsters will appear on the board. There is a set rate for Mysterious Bee monsters to appear in addition. The monsters’ locations will change every time 3 moves are completed. ※ The Mysterious Bee’s location is fixed at its initial appearance location and it will not move. If you land on a space with a monster, it will activate its effect then disappear. If a Gold Bee or Poison-Spraying Homunculus monster disappears, it will reappear when you land on or pass the START space. CategoryEffectGold BeeWhen you land on that platform, provides 2x rewardsPoison-Spraying HomunculusWhen you land on that platform, provides 1/2 rewardsMysterious BeeWhen you land on that platform, provides 3x rewards. Cumulative Rewards You can acquire cumulative rewards based on the total number of dice you roll. ※ Special dice are not included in this number of dice rolled. Cumulative Number of RollsRewardQuantityDurationTradeability15Blooming Flowers with Jin1PermanentCan only be put into storageAdvanced EXP Voucher500Expires on May 14, 2026 at 2:00 AM30Poison-Spraying Homunculus Riding (Permanent) Voucher1Jin Extreme Growth Potion (200~259)145Mysterious Forest Title Voucher1Advanced EXP Voucher50060Memories with Jin Voucher1Jin Extreme Growth Potion (200~259)175Gardener Jinroid Voucher1Advanced EXP Voucher1,00090Memories of the Mysterious Garden Outfit Set Voucher1Jin Extreme Growth Potion (200~259)1 [Back to Overview] Growth Fertilizer Shop A shop filled with growth items! Participate in Jin’s Mysterious Garden and exchange the Growth Fertilizer you acquire for various items! Event Participation Requirements A level 101 or higher character (or a Zero character that has completed Chapter 2) that has participated in the ‘Jin’s Mysterious Garden’ event. Event Period March 23, 2026 at 10:00 AM ~ May 13, 2026 at 11:59 PM. You can press the [Open Shop] button in the ‘Jin’s Mysterious Garden’ event UI or press the ‘Growth Fertilizer Shop’ in the Event List to use the shop. Jin’s Hyper Growth Potion (260~284) (20k coins) [limit of 1] Jin Extreme Growth Potion (200~259) (500 coins) [limit of 20] 100 Advanced EXP Vouchers (50 coins) [limit of 100] 4x Experience Coupon (30 Minutes) (300 coins) [limit of 20] VIP Booster (100 coins) [limit of 50] 3x Experience Coupon (30 Minutes) (100 coins) [limit of 100] ※ The items purchased from the Growth Fertilizer Shop will expire on May 14, 2026 at 2:00 AM. ※ All items can only be put into storage. ※ The Jin Hyper Growth Potion (260~284) can only be used by characters level 260 or higher. ※ When a level 260~284 character uses the Jin Hyper Growth Potion (260~284), they will level up 1 time. Level 285 or higher characters will receive the amount of experience required to level up at level 284. ※ The Jin Extreme Growth Potion (200~259) cannot be used by characters with the Burning effect active. ※ When you use the Jin Extreme Growth Potion (200~259), you will level up 1~10 times based on a set rate. ※ The Jin Extreme Growth Potion (200~259) can only be used by characters level 200 or higher. Level 260 or higher characters will receive the amount of experience required to level up at level 259. Receive your first clear mission rewards! Event Period: March 19, 2026 after the maintenance ~ March 25, 2026 at 11:59 PM. You can select the ‘[The One That Swallowed a God] Clear Rewards’ event in the Event List and click the ‘Participate’ button to receive the following rewards. ※ All rewards can only be claimed 1 time per Maple ID that completed the first clear mission. items.title: Maple Now LAN Virtual Tour!
items.summary: As stated previously, Director Changseop did a live stream yesterday called the Maple Now LAN Virtual Tour! He said it would be a mini-showcase and it was...

kmsBreakdown.summary: As stated previously, Director Changseop did a live stream yesterday called the Maple Now LAN Virtual Tour! He said it would be a mini-showcase and it was definitely that (or even more) with all the stuff he announced. This year’s anniversary will take place over the next three months (March, April, May) and they have prepared lots of content both in-game and in real life (such as the MapleStory theme park)!
highlights: After the Maple Now stream, the test server was opened with some of the changes which will be coming to the live server next week. Recently I’ve only been doing live server posts so I probably will end up continuing that, it’s just easier to take my time over a week+ instead of having to rush to finish the test server as fast as possible then have to do more work for whatever is different in the live server patch a few days later.
The Maple Now was divided into four sections:
Events

General The 23rd anniversary theme is Maple Attack! with Lotte. MapleStory’s cute monsters will take over every corner of the Jamsil neighbourhood in Seoul, and players can meet them in real-life from March to May. The in-game event map Maple Island was designed to look as close as possible to the real-life version. There’s a stereotype where usually events centred around monsters don’t tend to perform very well, but they were happy that players’s initial response to this concept was quite positive this time around so they’re hoping that this anniversary event will be different.

Mascot Parade The main event remains mostly the same with a one-click attendance check. You also must defeat 10,000 monsters per week (up to 100k monsters), but this limit will accumulate over the weeks so even if you start late, you can finish the event all at once. The special part of this event is that every four weeks, you can receive 1 time-limited Luna Petite pet (vac pet). More importantly, when you finish the whole event, you can receive 1 Luna Petite pet that you can keep forever! This will only be available to claim once the summer update begins on June 18 and can be claimed in any world. The reason why they are suddenly giving a vac pet as a reward is that currently, at least one vac pet is basically a requirement when hunting in MapleStory now. But they’re pretty expensive and many players that don’t have one, so they wanted to provide the experience to them. In addition, when coming back to MapleStory after a long time or starting anew, the best time is definitely the summer. But for people coming back to the game now, they can participate in this event, use these vac pets, and work on getting the permanent one for the summer update. Of course the event also has other parts such as temp stats and rewards.

MapleStory & BTS Jin BTS Jin joined MapleStory as an intern in 2022 and will return as a planner. They won’t show all of it off right now, just some of the highlights. Jin’s Mysterious Garden There are special rewards such as the Jin Hyper Growth Potion (gives 1 level up from level 260~284), Jin Extreme Growth Potion (randomly gives 1~10 level ups from level 200~259), and Advanced EXP Vouchers. In April, there will be a Hot Time event held directly by Jin. In May, there will be a new ‘Jin’ event. This event will have a new structure/format with a new type of gameplay. Compared to the previous BTS Jin event, it will have more rewards and be easier to participate in.
keyChanges: After the Maple Now stream, the test server was opened with some of the changes which will be coming to the live server next week. Recently I’ve only been doing live server posts so I probably will end up continuing that, it’s just easier to take my time over a week+ instead of having to rush to finish the test server as fast as possible then have to do more work for whatever is different in the live server patch a few days later.

sections.title: Overview

details.value: After the Maple Now stream, the test server was opened with some of the changes which will be coming to the live server next week. Recently I’ve only been doing live server posts so I probably will end up continuing that, it’s just easier to take my time over a week+ instead of having to rush to finish the test server as fast as possible then have to do more work for whatever is different in the live server patch a few days later.
The Maple Now was divided into four sections:

items: Events
Gameplay Reorganizations
Economy Related Changes
Newly Added Content

sections.summary: After the Maple Now stream, the test server was opened with some of the changes which will be coming to the live server next week. Recently I’ve only been doing live server posts so I probably will end up continuing that, it’s just easier to take my time over a week+ instead of having to rush to finish the test server as fast as possible then have to do more work for whatever is different in the live server patch a few days later.

items: General The 23rd anniversary theme is Maple Attack! with Lotte. MapleStory’s cute monsters will take over every corner of the Jamsil neighbourhood in Seoul, and players can meet them in real-life from March to May. The in-game event map Maple Island was designed to look as close as possible to the real-life version. There’s a stereotype where usually events centred around monsters don’t tend to perform very well, but they were happy that players’s initial response to this concept was quite positive this time around so they’re hoping that this anniversary event will be different.
The 23rd anniversary theme is Maple Attack! with Lotte.
MapleStory’s cute monsters will take over every corner of the Jamsil neighbourhood in Seoul, and players can meet them in real-life from March to May.
The in-game event map Maple Island was designed to look as close as possible to the real-life version.

There’s a stereotype where usually events centred around monsters don’t tend to perform very well, but they were happy that players’s initial response to this concept was quite positive this time around so they’re hoping that this anniversary event will be different.

Mascot Parade The main event remains mostly the same with a one-click attendance check. You also must defeat 10,000 monsters per week (up to 100k monsters), but this limit will accumulate over the weeks so even if you start late, you can finish the event all at once. The special part of this event is that every four weeks, you can receive 1 time-limited Luna Petite pet (vac pet). More importantly, when you finish the whole event, you can receive 1 Luna Petite pet that you can keep forever! This will only be available to claim once the summer update begins on June 18 and can be claimed in any world. The reason why they are suddenly giving a vac pet as a reward is that currently, at least one vac pet is basically a requirement when hunting in MapleStory now. But they’re pretty expensive and many players that don’t have one, so they wanted to provide the experience to them. In addition, when coming back to MapleStory after a long time or starting anew, the best time is definitely the summer. But for people coming back to the game now, they can participate in this event, use these vac pets, and work on getting the permanent one for the summer update. Of course the event also has other parts such as temp stats and rewards.

MapleStory & BTS Jin BTS Jin joined MapleStory as an intern in 2022 and will return as a planner. They won’t show all of it off right now, just some of the highlights. Jin’s Mysterious Garden There are special rewards such as the Jin Hyper Growth Potion (gives 1 level up from level 260~284), Jin Extreme Growth Potion (randomly gives 1~10 level ups from level 200~259), and Advanced EXP Vouchers. In April, there will be a Hot Time event held directly by Jin. In May, there will be a new ‘Jin’ event. This event will have a new structure/format with a new type of gameplay. Compared to the previous BTS Jin event, it will have more rewards and be easier to participate in.

Hyper Burning Booster x Tera Blink It’s just a given at this point.

Change Burning: Lucid This is a new type of event being released for the first time. It’s a new concept for Burning events which are usually based on creating new characters. This one is different in that it is designed to help you grow your existing characters further. You can transform into Lucid, hunt monsters, and complete missions. They did it this way because previously, some people weren’t motivated by events that had special classes like Saitama or Tanjiro because the characters would disappear at the end. But at the same time, there’s a unique fun to playing a new or event class that can level up quickly. Therefore, with this event, you can enjoy playing a new class while still growing your own character. The first character chosen for this event was Lucid. Before her, they thought it would be fun to transform into a monster. They were also thinking of either Black Mage or Will. But to match the Lotte World aspect (Lucid Dream Festa was held there previously for the Dreamer update), they decided on Lucid. Lucid has her own level and stats. There are also weekly and seasonal missions. The main rewards are experience related of course. When you hunt as Lucid, additional monsters will appear like VIP Booster monsters. There are special Cube and Flame items with a blue dot in the corner. These are new ‘Adversary’ Black Cubes, White Additional Cubes, and Abyss Rebirth Flames. They can only be used on Genesis/Destiny weapons or Astra subweapons. They created these so that they can give them out in larger quantities than previous events without a big effect on the market’s economy. They’re planning to give out about 200 of each. Lucid has her own special content called Nightmare Forest, which is like Monster Park. She can also acquire her own Dreamy Equipment and upgrade them with special Dreamy Crystals. Lucid has her own special content called Dreamy Trials. You must defeat various bosses including an exclusive boss Helena (Athena Pierce), who is Lucid’s ‘rival’. Zakum Vellum Lucid Helena Helena (Nightmare)

Offline Events From Lotte World to Seokchon Lake to Lotte World Tower to Lotte World Mall to all of the Jamsil neighbourhood, it will be covered in MapleStory. Starting today, Lotte World will be decorated with MapleStory themed areas and photo zones. Maple Island, the MapleStory theme park, will open officially on April 3. In May, a giant Orange Mushroom balloon and Slimes will appear in Seokchon Lake. The park itself will become Henesys Mushroom Park with special AR enhancements. There will be a pop-up store in Lotte World mall, as well as a collaboration with Uniqlo’s UT line and special food/drinks with MapleStory themes. They have rented out all of Lotte World for April 29, MapleStory’s birthday.

Their main goal was to fix/implement the things that players needed to have fixed by the time they return for the summer update.

The required experience from level 200-260 will be halved. As more and more level 260+ content has been added to help level up, the level 200-260 range became a bit neglected and overly slow. There’s no point in adding extra content in that range now so they decided to just halve the experience required. In Challengers World, there was a big bottleneck starting from Sellas so they wanted to make this entire range easier. It’s not every single level that is halved (e.g. level 200~210 is unchanged) but the further you go, the more it is reduced. Overall it ends up being halved.

Boss Pattern Reorganization Seren In Normal/Hard Mode, the Death Count will be increased to 8. The Sun’s Sword Wave and Sun’s Rage difficulty have been lowered. (The Sun’s Sword Wave is the banana slash, it moves faster now. The Sun’s Rage is the pillars that erupt from the ground, they don’t blind anymore and you can avoid them by standing in between pillars now) Phase 2 Dawn’s difficulty has been improved. (The Nerota’s Power orbs will no longer inflict reverse keys, and their HP has been drastically lowered so they can be defeated. The deer’s knockback attack cooldown has been increased.) Kalos In Easy/Normal Mode, the Death Count will be increased to 8. The interference removal difficulty has been improved. The Watcher’s Rage pre-delay has been increased. (Eye lasers) The Watcher’s Roar (Extreme) difficulty has been improved. (FMA) The Watcher’s Punishment/Watcher’s Judgement stun duration has been adjusted. (Knockback swipe) Kaling Pattern pre-delays have been adjusted, and you can attack Qiongqi/Kaling while they are flying. In Phase 2/3, when the boss is bound, the forced teleport pattern will be removed. In Phase 3, you can only hit the Peril in the area that you are currently in. (Ascents will still hit all 3 Perils regardless of which area you are in) This was done to alleviate the difference between classes that could hit multiple Perils at the same time and those that could not. In Phase 3, the monsters’ HP have been lowered. Limbo You can now use the Purifying Energy while using Ascent skills and Origin skills. In Phase 3, the map’s upper ceiling height has been adjusted. Jupiter Certain pattern effects’ visibility have been improved. All Damage Reflect/Damage Ignore status effects have been removed from bosses. When a boss uses a rush attack and hits a wall, there will no longer be a lingering hitbox where you still take damage even if the rush ends early.

Special Skill Ring Reorganization When they first added the Continuous Ring, they wanted each type of ring to have its own benefits and reasons to use. However, over time, as they added new skills that favoured one over the other, and added the polishing system, it became an issue where players constantly had to swap between the two. They wanted to fix this going forward. He knows that it’s risky to drastically change the value of these items but they wanted to do it as soon as possible to alleviate it before new skills are added in the future. The existing Ring Exchange skill and the reserve skill ring slot will be removed. Instead, there will be the Additional Special Skill Ring system. This extra slot will only appear if you have a Restraint Ring level 4+ or Continuous Ring level 4+ equipped. You can have one ‘active’ ring and one ‘passive’ ring equipped at the same time. All rings will be classified into active or passive, but naturally the goal is just Restraint + Continuous. All Special Skill Rings’ stats/properties will be adjusted. Continuous Ring: The buff reactivation cooldown has been removed. When you use a skill, a 30 second duration refreshable buff will be activated. The boss damage boost (at level 6) has been decreased from 195% to 54%. Restraint Ring: The area range has been increased. You can now reinstall the area. The attack/magic attack boost (at level 6) has been decreased from 95% to 85%. Generally, if you are currently using a level 4 Continuous Ring, the goal is that you will be dealing the same amount of damage by equipping a level 3 Restraint Ring. (or vice versa) They don’t want people swapping rings anymore, so even though the Ring Exchange skill has been removed, they have also added the condition that if you equip a Special Skill Ring, the skill’s maximum cooldown will be applied (so even if you double click to switch your ROR to a WJ, it won’t work because it will be on a 2 minute cooldown). From now on, only Restraint Ring and Continuous Ring can be polished. You can now use 10 Grindstones of Life + 5b mesos to have a 100% chance of polishing a level 4 ring to level 5. You can now use 20 Grindstones of Faith + 20b mesos to have a 100% chance of polishing a level 5 ring to level 6. If you have a level 5+ of any other ring, they will be turned into level 4 rings and you will receive the corresponding amount of Grindstones/mesos based on the 100% success rate materials. Starting from the June update, the feature where you could swap Special Skill Ring polishing levels will be removed because it won’t be needed anymore. In Challengers World, players that chose the Weapon Jump ring will have it replaced with either a Restraint Ring or Continuous Ring.

Training Ground This is a system to make more effective balance patches. Previously, there have been instances where they balanced based on damage cycles that players didn’t actually use which was a mistake due to lack of understanding. To prevent this, they decided to make this system where they can clearly see what players’ rotations are and balance around them. You must be a character with 300m Combat Power or higher to enter (considered a baseline to have a certain amount of knowledge about the job). After entering, you can hit the dummy and use your skills to record a damage cycle. Other players can then recommend your rotation. The cycle with the highest number of recommendations for each job will be considered the ‘actual damage cycle’ and the development team/players will be unified. There are Training Ground-only buffs, which are the only ones you can use. When you start your recording, the buffs will be applied automatically to be standardized between all players. You also need to meet certain conditions for your record to be registered. If you don’t meet them, your replay will not be registered. Use the Origin skill 1 time. Use the Ascent skill 3 times. Use your 2 minute cycle skills 3 times (except utility skills). You can view other players’ replays, and see their detailed information such as: Character equipment. Character settings and stats. Skill usage details. Battle Analysis. When viewing other players’ replays, you can see: Complete recording of their replay. Skill input timing. Key setting information. To recommend a replay, you must have soloed at least Easy Kalos on the same job (to avoid noise and make sure players are knowledgeable about the job before they can recommend a damage rotation). All results will be provided through the Open API as well. If there’s ever a balance patch or new skill or something like that, the records will be reset and you’ll have to do another replay. When this happens, the players in first place in recommendations will be provided Maple Points.

2x Experience Coupons will be removed. All 2x Experience Coupon sources will be changed to provide 3x Experience Coupons.
An option to prioritize displaying your character and monsters above skill effects will be added.

The cooldown and buff duration display will be improved. You can now choose to display the remaining cooldown/duration in seconds or minutes:seconds (e.g. 90 seconds or 1:30). It will display the cooldown timers for 5 seconds and less. This won’t be 100% perfect but they’re still trying to fix it.

The goal is to strike a good balance between the production and consumption of goods. This is to make sure that the production activities and the value of the goods created from them is maintained at a certain level for a certain period of time. It’s a key aspect of MMORPGs.
This time, they will only be touching the consumption related aspects.

When they provide growth related factors that can affect the market, they have to try to minimize the impact from ‘parked players’ (those who reach a certain power level then stop upgrading all gear and simply sell everything they collect for real life money). But it’s difficult to target and reduce rewards only for those kinds of players.

In this process, they have collected and installed lots of logging and made significant changes to their operation policies to be able to catch and restrict these cases of multi-accounters/abnormal production and supply.

They want to make consumption of goods to be more comfortable and meaningful. They will continue to touch both production and consumption going forward, but it won’t be as fast or intense as they did in the past.

Cube and Flame Reorganization All in-game cubes and flames will be changed to have the ‘Memorial’ feature (being able to choose between before and after). This will remove the risk of working on your own items with these cubes. Craftsman’s Cubes will become the Silver Cubes, Meister Cubes will become the Gold Cubes, and Strange Additional Cubes will become Bronze Additional Cubes. Powerful Rebirth Flames will become Blazing Rebirth Flames and Eternal Rebirth Flames will be removed/replaced with Black Rebirth Flames. Boss rewards will also be reorganized (in terms of cubes). They will now drop the following cubes that can only be put into storage, can only be used on level 200 or lower equipment, and will restrict the equipment that they are used on from being traded for 30 days (‘Memento’ feature). Memento Silver Cube Memento Gold Cube Memento Bronze Additional Cube When they do things such as adjust boss crystal prices, there’s always a dilemma where players that are levelling up and trying to get stronger to face higher bosses end up lacking mesos to upgrade their gear. Now, they will be able to use these materials to gradually upgrade their own gear without risks. The 30 day trade restriction is to emphasize that they are meant to be used directly on your own gear, not to sell to other players. These rewards will drop from bosses between Hard Damien to Easy Kaling.
All in-game cubes and flames will be changed to have the ‘Memorial’ feature (being able to choose between before and after). This will remove the risk of working on your own items with these cubes.
Craftsman’s Cubes will become the Silver Cubes, Meister Cubes will become the Gold Cubes, and Strange Additional Cubes will become Bronze Additional Cubes.

Star Force Enhancement Reorganization When you destroy an item when Star Force enhancing it, you can now use additional spares and mesos to restore the trace to the star that it boomed at (up to 22 stars). For example, for level 200 equipment: 15 Stars: 1 equip, 433m 16 Stars: 1 equip, 1.05b mesos 17 Stars: 1 equip, 1.77b mesos 18 Stars: 1 equip, 4.01b mesos 19 Stars: 2 equips, 6.65b mesos 20 Stars: 2 equips, 11.8b mesos 21 Stars: 3 equips, 14.8b mesos 22 Stars: 4 equips, 24.2b mesos Star Catching will be removed and the increased success rate will be added to the base rates.

Auto Enchant You can now automatically enchant potential, bonus stats, and Star Force. You can set a goal and it will keep going automatically until the goal is reached or you run out of mesos or materials. Potential: You can set 4 different possibilities. Bonus Stats: You can set the desired values for the different bonus stats. Star Force: You can set the desired star you want to stop at, as well as if you want to protect it at 15/16/17 stars.

Bonus Stats/Potential Transfer Scrolls The Bonus Stats Transfer Scroll lets you transfer an equipment’s bonus stats to another of the same exact equipment (the original equipment will be destroyed). It costs 49k NX. The Potential Transfer Scroll lets you transfer an equipment’s potential (not additional potential) to another of the same exact equipment (the original equipment will be destroyed). It costs 99k NX. The goal of these two items is to make it easier to work on your own equipment directly and to be able to complete items while avoiding RNG elements. Trading is a key part of MapleStory and even previously, many users could come together to work on a single item. These scrolls can help with that as even if one person rolls something really good but can’t upgrade the rest of the item to its full value, they can use these items to extract those stats and sell them. When making your own items, at some point you’ll hit an RNG wall where you just have to brute force it to finish a part of it. These scrolls are another option to do that without having to resort to RNG. This is a very big change so they will be examining them closely and make additional changes if necessary.

Along the same lines as the above addition, Complete Under Control and Mitra’s Rage can now be traded using the Platinum Karma Scissors 5 times. There’s definitely players that have had the experience of getting a 60% attack Mitra’s Rage which is very good, but then later on, to try and upgrade it to 63% attack, you would have to either cube it yourself or buy a completely new one. Now, you can resell without disappointment.

Sol Janus & Erda Fountain These skills’ damage will be adjusted to have different damage values for each job (just like Sol Hecate). Previously, all jobs had the same damage % but there’s obviously differences in passives for each class which need to be accounted for so they can use the skills effectively with a similar efficiency.

Sol Hecate A feature to change Sol Hecate’s appearance will be added. You can choose between the following: Sol Hecate: Amicus Sol Hecate: Zephyr Sol Hecate: Nebula Sol Hecate: Draco Sol Hecate: Helia Lots of players shared the opinion that Sol Hecate was ugly when it came out. Originally, they wanted to release this skill with these skin options at the same time but didn’t have time. You can customize your Sol Hecate’s appearance and colour to whichever you want. It costs 1b mesos to customize it. They may sell more fancy designs later on but they haven’t confirmed that yet.

details.value: After the Maple Now stream, the test server was opened with some of the changes which will be coming to the live server next week. Recently I’ve only been doing live server posts so I probably will end up continuing that, it’s just easier to take my time over a week+ instead of having to rush to finish the test server as fast as possible then have to do more work for whatever is different in the live server patch a few days later.
The Maple Now was divided into four sections:

items: Events
Gameplay Reorganizations
Economy Related Changes
Newly Added Content

General The 23rd anniversary theme is Maple Attack! with Lotte. MapleStory’s cute monsters will take over every corner of the Jamsil neighbourhood in Seoul, and players can meet them in real-life from March to May. The in-game event map Maple Island was designed to look as close as possible to the real-life version. There’s a stereotype where usually events centred around monsters don’t tend to perform very well, but they were happy that players’s initial response to this concept was quite positive this time around so they’re hoping that this anniversary event will be different.
The 23rd anniversary theme is Maple Attack! with Lotte.
MapleStory’s cute monsters will take over every corner of the Jamsil neighbourhood in Seoul, and players can meet them in real-life from March to May.
The in-game event map Maple Island was designed to look as close as possible to the real-life version.

There’s a stereotype where usually events centred around monsters don’t tend to perform very well, but they were happy that players’s initial response to this concept was quite positive this time around so they’re hoping that this anniversary event will be different.

Mascot Parade The main event remains mostly the same with a one-click attendance check. You also must defeat 10,000 monsters per week (up to 100k monsters), but this limit will accumulate over the weeks so even if you start late, you can finish the event all at once. The special part of this event is that every four weeks, you can receive 1 time-limited Luna Petite pet (vac pet). More importantly, when you finish the whole event, you can receive 1 Luna Petite pet that you can keep forever! This will only be available to claim once the summer update begins on June 18 and can be claimed in any world. The reason why they are suddenly giving a vac pet as a reward is that currently, at least one vac pet is basically a requirement when hunting in MapleStory now. But they’re pretty expensive and many players that don’t have one, so they wanted to provide the experience to them. In addition, when coming back to MapleStory after a long time or starting anew, the best time is definitely the summer. But for people coming back to the game now, they can participate in this event, use these vac pets, and work on getting the permanent one for the summer update. Of course the event also has other parts such as temp stats and rewards.

MapleStory & BTS Jin BTS Jin joined MapleStory as an intern in 2022 and will return as a planner. They won’t show all of it off right now, just some of the highlights. Jin’s Mysterious Garden There are special rewards such as the Jin Hyper Growth Potion (gives 1 level up from level 260~284), Jin Extreme Growth Potion (randomly gives 1~10 level ups from level 200~259), and Advanced EXP Vouchers. In April, there will be a Hot Time event held directly by Jin. In May, there will be a new ‘Jin’ event. This event will have a new structure/format with a new type of gameplay. Compared to the previous BTS Jin event, it will have more rewards and be easier to participate in.

Change Burning: Lucid This is a new type of event being released for the first time. It’s a new concept for Burning events which are usually based on creating new characters. This one is different in that it is designed to help you grow your existing characters further. You can transform into Lucid, hunt monsters, and complete missions. They did it this way because previously, some people weren’t motivated by events that had special classes like Saitama or Tanjiro because the characters would disappear at the end. But at the same time, there’s a unique fun to playing a new or event class that can level up quickly. Therefore, with this event, you can enjoy playing a new class while still growing your own character. The first character chosen for this event was Lucid. Before her, they thought it would be fun to transform into a monster. They were also thinking of either Black Mage or Will. But to match the Lotte World aspect (Lucid Dream Festa was held there previously for the Dreamer update), they decided on Lucid. Lucid has her own level and stats. There are also weekly and seasonal missions. The main rewards are experience related of course. When you hunt as Lucid, additional monsters will appear like VIP Booster monsters. There are special Cube and Flame items with a blue dot in the corner. These are new ‘Adversary’ Black Cubes, White Additional Cubes, and Abyss Rebirth Flames. They can only be used on Genesis/Destiny weapons or Astra subweapons. They created these so that they can give them out in larger quantities than previous events without a big effect on the market’s economy. They’re planning to give out about 200 of each. Lucid has her own special content called Nightmare Forest, which is like Monster Park. She can also acquire her own Dreamy Equipment and upgrade them with special Dreamy Crystals. Lucid has her own special content called Dreamy Trials. You must defeat various bosses including an exclusive boss Helena (Athena Pierce), who is Lucid’s ‘rival’. Zakum Vellum Lucid Helena Helena (Nightmare)

Offline Events From Lotte World to Seokchon Lake to Lotte World Tower to Lotte World Mall to all of the Jamsil neighbourhood, it will be covered in MapleStory. Starting today, Lotte World will be decorated with MapleStory themed areas and photo zones. Maple Island, the MapleStory theme park, will open officially on April 3. In May, a giant Orange Mushroom balloon and Slimes will appear in Seokchon Lake. The park itself will become Henesys Mushroom Park with special AR enhancements. There will be a pop-up store in Lotte World mall, as well as a collaboration with Uniqlo’s UT line and special food/drinks with MapleStory themes. They have rented out all of Lotte World for April 29, MapleStory’s birthday.

Their main goal was to fix/implement the things that players needed to have fixed by the time they return for the summer update.

The required experience from level 200-260 will be halved. As more and more level 260+ content has been added to help level up, the level 200-260 range became a bit neglected and overly slow. There’s no point in adding extra content in that range now so they decided to just halve the experience required. In Challengers World, there was a big bottleneck starting from Sellas so they wanted to make this entire range easier. It’s not every single level that is halved (e.g. level 200~210 is unchanged) but the further you go, the more it is reduced. Overall it ends up being halved.

Boss Pattern Reorganization Seren In Normal/Hard Mode, the Death Count will be increased to 8. The Sun’s Sword Wave and Sun’s Rage difficulty have been lowered. (The Sun’s Sword Wave is the banana slash, it moves faster now. The Sun’s Rage is the pillars that erupt from the ground, they don’t blind anymore and you can avoid them by standing in between pillars now) Phase 2 Dawn’s difficulty has been improved. (The Nerota’s Power orbs will no longer inflict reverse keys, and their HP has been drastically lowered so they can be defeated. The deer’s knockback attack cooldown has been increased.) Kalos In Easy/Normal Mode, the Death Count will be increased to 8. The interference removal difficulty has been improved. The Watcher’s Rage pre-delay has been increased. (Eye lasers) The Watcher’s Roar (Extreme) difficulty has been improved. (FMA) The Watcher’s Punishment/Watcher’s Judgement stun duration has been adjusted. (Knockback swipe) Kaling Pattern pre-delays have been adjusted, and you can attack Qiongqi/Kaling while they are flying. In Phase 2/3, when the boss is bound, the forced teleport pattern will be removed. In Phase 3, you can only hit the Peril in the area that you are currently in. (Ascents will still hit all 3 Perils regardless of which area you are in) This was done to alleviate the difference between classes that could hit multiple Perils at the same time and those that could not. In Phase 3, the monsters’ HP have been lowered. Limbo You can now use the Purifying Energy while using Ascent skills and Origin skills. In Phase 3, the map’s upper ceiling height has been adjusted. Jupiter Certain pattern effects’ visibility have been improved. All Damage Reflect/Damage Ignore status effects have been removed from bosses. When a boss uses a rush attack and hits a wall, there will no longer be a lingering hitbox where you still take damage even if the rush ends early.

Special Skill Ring Reorganization When they first added the Continuous Ring, they wanted each type of ring to have its own benefits and reasons to use. However, over time, as they added new skills that favoured one over the other, and added the polishing system, it became an issue where players constantly had to swap between the two. They wanted to fix this going forward. He knows that it’s risky to drastically change the value of these items but they wanted to do it as soon as possible to alleviate it before new skills are added in the future. The existing Ring Exchange skill and the reserve skill ring slot will be removed. Instead, there will be the Additional Special Skill Ring system. This extra slot will only appear if you have a Restraint Ring level 4+ or Continuous Ring level 4+ equipped. You can have one ‘active’ ring and one ‘passive’ ring equipped at the same time. All rings will be classified into active or passive, but naturally the goal is just Restraint + Continuous. All Special Skill Rings’ stats/properties will be adjusted. Continuous Ring: The buff reactivation cooldown has been removed. When you use a skill, a 30 second duration refreshable buff will be activated. The boss damage boost (at level 6) has been decreased from 195% to 54%. Restraint Ring: The area range has been increased. You can now reinstall the area. The attack/magic attack boost (at level 6) has been decreased from 95% to 85%. Generally, if you are currently using a level 4 Continuous Ring, the goal is that you will be dealing the same amount of damage by equipping a level 3 Restraint Ring. (or vice versa) They don’t want people swapping rings anymore, so even though the Ring Exchange skill has been removed, they have also added the condition that if you equip a Special Skill Ring, the skill’s maximum cooldown will be applied (so even if you double click to switch your ROR to a WJ, it won’t work because it will be on a 2 minute cooldown). From now on, only Restraint Ring and Continuous Ring can be polished. You can now use 10 Grindstones of Life + 5b mesos to have a 100% chance of polishing a level 4 ring to level 5. You can now use 20 Grindstones of Faith + 20b mesos to have a 100% chance of polishing a level 5 ring to level 6. If you have a level 5+ of any other ring, they will be turned into level 4 rings and you will receive the corresponding amount of Grindstones/mesos based on the 100% success rate materials. Starting from the June update, the feature where you could swap Special Skill Ring polishing levels will be removed because it won’t be needed anymore. In Challengers World, players that chose the Weapon Jump ring will have it replaced with either a Restraint Ring or Continuous Ring.

When they first added the Continuous Ring, they wanted each type of ring to have its own benefits and reasons to use. However, over time, as they added new skills that favoured one over the other, and added the polishing system, it became an issue where players constantly had to swap between the two. They wanted to fix this going forward.
He knows that it’s risky to drastically change the value of these items but they wanted to do it as soon as possible to alleviate it before new skills are added in the future.
The existing Ring Exchange skill and the reserve skill ring slot will be removed.

Instead, there will be the Additional Special Skill Ring system. This extra slot will only appear if you have a Restraint Ring level 4+ or Continuous Ring level 4+ equipped. You can have one ‘active’ ring and one ‘passive’ ring equipped at the same time.
All rings will be classified into active or passive, but naturally the goal is just Restraint + Continuous.

All Special Skill Rings’ stats/properties will be adjusted. Continuous Ring: The buff reactivation cooldown has been removed. When you use a skill, a 30 second duration refreshable buff will be activated. The boss damage boost (at level 6) has been decreased from 195% to 54%. Restraint Ring: The area range has been increased. You can now reinstall the area. The attack/magic attack boost (at level 6) has been decreased from 95% to 85%.

Continuous Ring: The buff reactivation cooldown has been removed. When you use a skill, a 30 second duration refreshable buff will be activated. The boss damage boost (at level 6) has been decreased from 195% to 54%.
Restraint Ring: The area range has been increased. You can now reinstall the area. The attack/magic attack boost (at level 6) has been decreased from 95% to 85%.
Generally, if you are currently using a level 4 Continuous Ring, the goal is that you will be dealing the same amount of damage by equipping a level 3 Restraint Ring. (or vice versa)

They don’t want people swapping rings anymore, so even though the Ring Exchange skill has been removed, they have also added the condition that if you equip a Special Skill Ring, the skill’s maximum cooldown will be applied (so even if you double click to switch your ROR to a WJ, it won’t work because it will be on a 2 minute cooldown).
From now on, only Restraint Ring and Continuous Ring can be polished.

You can now use 10 Grindstones of Life + 5b mesos to have a 100% chance of polishing a level 4 ring to level 5. You can now use 20 Grindstones of Faith + 20b mesos to have a 100% chance of polishing a level 5 ring to level 6.
If you have a level 5+ of any other ring, they will be turned into level 4 rings and you will receive the corresponding amount of Grindstones/mesos based on the 100% success rate materials.

In Challengers World, players that chose the Weapon Jump ring will have it replaced with either a Restraint Ring or Continuous Ring.

Training Ground This is a system to make more effective balance patches. Previously, there have been instances where they balanced based on damage cycles that players didn’t actually use which was a mistake due to lack of understanding. To prevent this, they decided to make this system where they can clearly see what players’ rotations are and balance around them. You must be a character with 300m Combat Power or higher to enter (considered a baseline to have a certain amount of knowledge about the job). After entering, you can hit the dummy and use your skills to record a damage cycle. Other players can then recommend your rotation. The cycle with the highest number of recommendations for each job will be considered the ‘actual damage cycle’ and the development team/players will be unified. There are Training Ground-only buffs, which are the only ones you can use. When you start your recording, the buffs will be applied automatically to be standardized between all players. You also need to meet certain conditions for your record to be registered. If you don’t meet them, your replay will not be registered. Use the Origin skill 1 time. Use the Ascent skill 3 times. Use your 2 minute cycle skills 3 times (except utility skills). You can view other players’ replays, and see their detailed information such as: Character equipment. Character settings and stats. Skill usage details. Battle Analysis. When viewing other players’ replays, you can see: Complete recording of their replay. Skill input timing. Key setting information. To recommend a replay, you must have soloed at least Easy Kalos on the same job (to avoid noise and make sure players are knowledgeable about the job before they can recommend a damage rotation). All results will be provided through the Open API as well. If there’s ever a balance patch or new skill or something like that, the records will be reset and you’ll have to do another replay. When this happens, the players in first place in recommendations will be provided Maple Points.

The goal is to strike a good balance between the production and consumption of goods. This is to make sure that the production activities and the value of the goods created from them is maintained at a certain level for a certain period of time. It’s a key aspect of MMORPGs.
This time, they will only be touching the consumption related aspects.

When they provide growth related factors that can affect the market, they have to try to minimize the impact from ‘parked players’ (those who reach a certain power level then stop upgrading all gear and simply sell everything they collect for real life money). But it’s difficult to target and reduce rewards only for those kinds of players.

In this process, they have collected and installed lots of logging and made significant changes to their operation policies to be able to catch and restrict these cases of multi-accounters/abnormal production and supply.

They want to make consumption of goods to be more comfortable and meaningful. They will continue to touch both production and consumption going forward, but it won’t be as fast or intense as they did in the past.

Cube and Flame Reorganization All in-game cubes and flames will be changed to have the ‘Memorial’ feature (being able to choose between before and after). This will remove the risk of working on your own items with these cubes. Craftsman’s Cubes will become the Silver Cubes, Meister Cubes will become the Gold Cubes, and Strange Additional Cubes will become Bronze Additional Cubes. Powerful Rebirth Flames will become Blazing Rebirth Flames and Eternal Rebirth Flames will be removed/replaced with Black Rebirth Flames. Boss rewards will also be reorganized (in terms of cubes). They will now drop the following cubes that can only be put into storage, can only be used on level 200 or lower equipment, and will restrict the equipment that they are used on from being traded for 30 days (‘Memento’ feature). Memento Silver Cube Memento Gold Cube Memento Bronze Additional Cube When they do things such as adjust boss crystal prices, there’s always a dilemma where players that are levelling up and trying to get stronger to face higher bosses end up lacking mesos to upgrade their gear. Now, they will be able to use these materials to gradually upgrade their own gear without risks. The 30 day trade restriction is to emphasize that they are meant to be used directly on your own gear, not to sell to other players. These rewards will drop from bosses between Hard Damien to Easy Kaling.

Star Force Enhancement Reorganization When you destroy an item when Star Force enhancing it, you can now use additional spares and mesos to restore the trace to the star that it boomed at (up to 22 stars). For example, for level 200 equipment: 15 Stars: 1 equip, 433m 16 Stars: 1 equip, 1.05b mesos 17 Stars: 1 equip, 1.77b mesos 18 Stars: 1 equip, 4.01b mesos 19 Stars: 2 equips, 6.65b mesos 20 Stars: 2 equips, 11.8b mesos 21 Stars: 3 equips, 14.8b mesos 22 Stars: 4 equips, 24.2b mesos Star Catching will be removed and the increased success rate will be added to the base rates.

Auto Enchant You can now automatically enchant potential, bonus stats, and Star Force. You can set a goal and it will keep going automatically until the goal is reached or you run out of mesos or materials. Potential: You can set 4 different possibilities. Bonus Stats: You can set the desired values for the different bonus stats. Star Force: You can set the desired star you want to stop at, as well as if you want to protect it at 15/16/17 stars.

Bonus Stats/Potential Transfer Scrolls The Bonus Stats Transfer Scroll lets you transfer an equipment’s bonus stats to another of the same exact equipment (the original equipment will be destroyed). It costs 49k NX. The Potential Transfer Scroll lets you transfer an equipment’s potential (not additional potential) to another of the same exact equipment (the original equipment will be destroyed). It costs 99k NX. The goal of these two items is to make it easier to work on your own equipment directly and to be able to complete items while avoiding RNG elements. Trading is a key part of MapleStory and even previously, many users could come together to work on a single item. These scrolls can help with that as even if one person rolls something really good but can’t upgrade the rest of the item to its full value, they can use these items to extract those stats and sell them. When making your own items, at some point you’ll hit an RNG wall where you just have to brute force it to finish a part of it. These scrolls are another option to do that without having to resort to RNG. This is a very big change so they will be examining them closely and make additional changes if necessary.

Along the same lines as the above addition, Complete Under Control and Mitra’s Rage can now be traded using the Platinum Karma Scissors 5 times. There’s definitely players that have had the experience of getting a 60% attack Mitra’s Rage which is very good, but then later on, to try and upgrade it to 63% attack, you would have to either cube it yourself or buy a completely new one. Now, you can resell without disappointment.
