kmsBreakdown.fullText: As stated previously, Director Changseop did a live stream yesterday called the Maple Now LAN Virtual Tour! He said it would be a mini-showcase and it was definitely that (or even more) with all the stuff he announced. This year’s anniversary will take place over the next three months (March, April, May) and they have prepared lots of content both in-game and in real life (such as the MapleStory theme park)! After the Maple Now stream, the test server was opened with some of the changes which will be coming to the live server next week. Recently I’ve only been doing live server posts so I probably will end up continuing that, it’s just easier to take my time over a week+ instead of having to rush to finish the test server as fast as possible then have to do more work for whatever is different in the live server patch a few days later. The Maple Now was divided into four sections: Events Gameplay Reorganizations Economy Related Changes Newly Added Content Maple Attack! with Lotte General The 23rd anniversary theme is Maple Attack! with Lotte. MapleStory’s cute monsters will take over every corner of the Jamsil neighbourhood in Seoul, and players can meet them in real-life from March to May. The in-game event map Maple Island was designed to look as close as possible to the real-life version. There’s a stereotype where usually events centred around monsters don’t tend to perform very well, but they were happy that players’s initial response to this concept was quite positive this time around so they’re hoping that this anniversary event will be different. Mascot Parade The main event remains mostly the same with a one-click attendance check. You also must defeat 10,000 monsters per week (up to 100k monsters), but this limit will accumulate over the weeks so even if you start late, you can finish the event all at once. The special part of this event is that every four weeks, you can receive 1 time-limited Luna Petite pet (vac pet). More importantly, when you finish the whole event, you can receive 1 Luna Petite pet that you can keep forever! This will only be available to claim once the summer update begins on June 18 and can be claimed in any world. The reason why they are suddenly giving a vac pet as a reward is that currently, at least one vac pet is basically a requirement when hunting in MapleStory now. But they’re pretty expensive and many players that don’t have one, so they wanted to provide the experience to them. In addition, when coming back to MapleStory after a long time or starting anew, the best time is definitely the summer. But for people coming back to the game now, they can participate in this event, use these vac pets, and work on getting the permanent one for the summer update. Of course the event also has other parts such as temp stats and rewards. MapleStory & BTS Jin BTS Jin joined MapleStory as an intern in 2022 and will return as a planner. They won’t show all of it off right now, just some of the highlights. Jin’s Mysterious Garden There are special rewards such as the Jin Hyper Growth Potion (gives 1 level up from level 260~284), Jin Extreme Growth Potion (randomly gives 1~10 level ups from level 200~259), and Advanced EXP Vouchers. In April, there will be a Hot Time event held directly by Jin. In May, there will be a new ‘Jin’ event. This event will have a new structure/format with a new type of gameplay. Compared to the previous BTS Jin event, it will have more rewards and be easier to participate in. Hyper Burning Booster x Tera Blink It’s just a given at this point. Change Burning: Lucid This is a new type of event being released for the first time. It’s a new concept for Burning events which are usually based on creating new characters. This one is different in that it is designed to help you grow your existing characters further. You can transform into Lucid, hunt monsters, and complete missions. They did it this way because previously, some people weren’t motivated by events that had special classes like Saitama or Tanjiro because the characters would disappear at the end. But at the same time, there’s a unique fun to playing a new or event class that can level up quickly. Therefore, with this event, you can enjoy playing a new class while still growing your own character. The first character chosen for this event was Lucid. Before her, they thought it would be fun to transform into a monster. They were also thinking of either Black Mage or Will. But to match the Lotte World aspect (Lucid Dream Festa was held there previously for the Dreamer update), they decided on Lucid. Lucid has her own level and stats. There are also weekly and seasonal missions. The main rewards are experience related of course. When you hunt as Lucid, additional monsters will appear like VIP Booster monsters. There are special Cube and Flame items with a blue dot in the corner. These are new ‘Adversary’ Black Cubes, White Additional Cubes, and Abyss Rebirth Flames. They can only be used on Genesis/Destiny weapons or Astra subweapons. They created these so that they can give them out in larger quantities than previous events without a big effect on the market’s economy. They’re planning to give out about 200 of each. Lucid has her own special content called Nightmare Forest, which is like Monster Park. She can also acquire her own Dreamy Equipment and upgrade them with special Dreamy Crystals. Lucid has her own special content called Dreamy Trials. You must defeat various bosses including an exclusive boss Helena (Athena Pierce), who is Lucid’s ‘rival’. Zakum Vellum Lucid Helena Helena (Nightmare) Offline Events From Lotte World to Seokchon Lake to Lotte World Tower to Lotte World Mall to all of the Jamsil neighbourhood, it will be covered in MapleStory. Starting today, Lotte World will be decorated with MapleStory themed areas and photo zones. Maple Island, the MapleStory theme park, will open officially on April 3. In May, a giant Orange Mushroom balloon and Slimes will appear in Seokchon Lake. The park itself will become Henesys Mushroom Park with special AR enhancements. There will be a pop-up store in Lotte World mall, as well as a collaboration with Uniqlo’s UT line and special food/drinks with MapleStory themes. They have rented out all of Lotte World for April 29, MapleStory’s birthday. Gameplay Reorganizations Their main goal was to fix/implement the things that players needed to have fixed by the time they return for the summer update. The required experience from level 200-260 will be halved. As more and more level 260+ content has been added to help level up, the level 200-260 range became a bit neglected and overly slow. There’s no point in adding extra content in that range now so they decided to just halve the experience required. In Challengers World, there was a big bottleneck starting from Sellas so they wanted to make this entire range easier. It’s not every single level that is halved (e.g. level 200~210 is unchanged) but the further you go, the more it is reduced. Overall it ends up being halved. 2x Arcane Symbols provided from daily quests and weekly content. 1.5x Authentic Symbols provided from daily quests. Boss Pattern Reorganization Seren In Normal/Hard Mode, the Death Count will be increased to 8. The Sun’s Sword Wave and Sun’s Rage difficulty have been lowered. (The Sun’s Sword Wave is the banana slash, it moves faster now. The Sun’s Rage is the pillars that erupt from the ground, they don’t blind anymore and you can avoid them by standing in between pillars now) Phase 2 Dawn’s difficulty has been improved. (The Nerota’s Power orbs will no longer inflict reverse keys, and their HP has been drastically lowered so they can be defeated. The deer’s knockback attack cooldown has been increased.) Kalos In Easy/Normal Mode, the Death Count will be increased to 8. The interference removal difficulty has been improved. The Watcher’s Rage pre-delay has been increased. (Eye lasers) The Watcher’s Roar (Extreme) difficulty has been improved. (FMA) The Watcher’s Punishment/Watcher’s Judgement stun duration has been adjusted. (Knockback swipe) Kaling Pattern pre-delays have been adjusted, and you can attack Qiongqi/Kaling while they are flying. In Phase 2/3, when the boss is bound, the forced teleport pattern will be removed. In Phase 3, you can only hit the Peril in the area that you are currently in. (Ascents will still hit all 3 Perils regardless of which area you are in) This was done to alleviate the difference between classes that could hit multiple Perils at the same time and those that could not. In Phase 3, the monsters’ HP have been lowered. Limbo You can now use the Purifying Energy while using Ascent skills and Origin skills. In Phase 3, the map’s upper ceiling height has been adjusted. Jupiter Certain pattern effects’ visibility have been improved. All Damage Reflect/Damage Ignore status effects have been removed from bosses. When a boss uses a rush attack and hits a wall, there will no longer be a lingering hitbox where you still take damage even if the rush ends early. Special Skill Ring Reorganization When they first added the Continuous Ring, they wanted each type of ring to have its own benefits and reasons to use. However, over time, as they added new skills that favoured one over the other, and added the polishing system, it became an issue where players constantly had to swap between the two. They wanted to fix this going forward. He knows that it’s risky to drastically change the value of these items but they wanted to do it as soon as possible to alleviate it before new skills are added in the future. The existing Ring Exchange skill and the reserve skill ring slot will be removed. Instead, there will be the Additional Special Skill Ring system. This extra slot will only appear if you have a Restraint Ring level 4+ or Continuous Ring level 4+ equipped. You can have one ‘active’ ring and one ‘passive’ ring equipped at the same time. All rings will be classified into active or passive, but naturally the goal is just Restraint + Continuous. All Special Skill Rings’ stats/properties will be adjusted. Continuous Ring: The buff reactivation cooldown has been removed. When you use a skill, a 30 second duration refreshable buff will be activated. The boss damage boost (at level 6) has been decreased from 195% to 54%. Restraint Ring: The area range has been increased. You can now reinstall the area. The attack/magic attack boost (at level 6) has been decreased from 95% to 85%. Generally, if you are currently using a level 4 Continuous Ring, the goal is that you will be dealing the same amount of damage by equipping a level 3 Restraint Ring. (or vice versa) They don’t want people swapping rings anymore, so even though the Ring Exchange skill has been removed, they have also added the condition that if you equip a Special Skill Ring, the skill’s maximum cooldown will be applied (so even if you double click to switch your ROR to a WJ, it won’t work because it will be on a 2 minute cooldown). From now on, only Restraint Ring and Continuous Ring can be polished. You can now use 10 Grindstones of Life + 5b mesos to have a 100% chance of polishing a level 4 ring to level 5. You can now use 20 Grindstones of Faith + 20b mesos to have a 100% chance of polishing a level 5 ring to level 6. If you have a level 5+ of any other ring, they will be turned into level 4 rings and you will receive the corresponding amount of Grindstones/mesos based on the 100% success rate materials. Starting from the June update, the feature where you could swap Special Skill Ring polishing levels will be removed because it won’t be needed anymore. In Challengers World, players that chose the Weapon Jump ring will have it replaced with either a Restraint Ring or Continuous Ring. Training Ground This is a system to make more effective balance patches. Previously, there have been instances where they balanced based on damage cycles that players didn’t actually use which was a mistake due to lack of understanding. To prevent this, they decided to make this system where they can clearly see what players’ rotations are and balance around them. You must be a character with 300m Combat Power or higher to enter (considered a baseline to have a certain amount of knowledge about the job). After entering, you can hit the dummy and use your skills to record a damage cycle. Other players can then recommend your rotation. The cycle with the highest number of recommendations for each job will be considered the ‘actual damage cycle’ and the development team/players will be unified. There are Training Ground-only buffs, which are the only ones you can use. When you start your recording, the buffs will be applied automatically to be standardized between all players. You also need to meet certain conditions for your record to be registered. If you don’t meet them, your replay will not be registered. Use the Origin skill 1 time. Use the Ascent skill 3 times. Use your 2 minute cycle skills 3 times (except utility skills). You can view other players’ replays, and see their detailed information such as: Character equipment. Character settings and stats. Skill usage details. Battle Analysis. When viewing other players’ replays, you can see: Complete recording of their replay. Skill input timing. Key setting information. To recommend a replay, you must have soloed at least Easy Kalos on the same job (to avoid noise and make sure players are knowledgeable about the job before they can recommend a damage rotation). All results will be provided through the Open API as well. If there’s ever a balance patch or new skill or something like that, the records will be reset and you’ll have to do another replay. When this happens, the players in first place in recommendations will be provided Maple Points. A new bag will be added that holds boss loot, Symbol Vouchers, and personality trait items. 60 slot Ore/Herb/Crafting Material Bags will be added. 2x Experience Coupons will be removed. All 2x Experience Coupon sources will be changed to provide 3x Experience Coupons. An option to prioritize displaying your character and monsters above skill effects will be added. The cooldown and buff duration display will be improved. You can now choose to display the remaining cooldown/duration in seconds or minutes:seconds (e.g. 90 seconds or 1:30). It will display the cooldown timers for 5 seconds and less. This won’t be 100% perfect but they’re still trying to fix it. The following buffs will be added to Practice Mode and Foggy Forest Training Centre buffs. Weather effect VIP Buff (Stats) MVP Superpower Other various gameplay improvements: Union Buff visibility improvements. Daily quests and weekly content Symbol claiming convenience improvements. Soul Enchanters will be sold in general shops. The Pocket item slot unlock conditions will be removed. You can now bulk use Monster Park Day boxes and Eternal equipment pieces. The Maple Guide/Meisterville teleport cooldown will be decreased from 7 seconds to 3 seconds. Custom illustrations will be introduced as permanent content. Economy Reorganization The goal is to strike a good balance between the production and consumption of goods. This is to make sure that the production activities and the value of the goods created from them is maintained at a certain level for a certain period of time. It’s a key aspect of MMORPGs. This time, they will only be touching the consumption related aspects. When they provide growth related factors that can affect the market, they have to try to minimize the impact from ‘parked players’ (those who reach a certain power level then stop upgrading all gear and simply sell everything they collect for real life money). But it’s difficult to target and reduce rewards only for those kinds of players. In this process, they have collected and installed lots of logging and made significant changes to their operation policies to be able to catch and restrict these cases of multi-accounters/abnormal production and supply. They want to make consumption of goods to be more comfortable and meaningful. They will continue to touch both production and consumption going forward, but it won’t be as fast or intense as they did in the past. Cube and Flame Reorganization All in-game cubes and flames will be changed to have the ‘Memorial’ feature (being able to choose between before and after). This will remove the risk of working on your own items with these cubes. Craftsman’s Cubes will become the Silver Cubes, Meister Cubes will become the Gold Cubes, and Strange Additional Cubes will become Bronze Additional Cubes. Powerful Rebirth Flames will become Blazing Rebirth Flames and Eternal Rebirth Flames will be removed/replaced with Black Rebirth Flames. Boss rewards will also be reorganized (in terms of cubes). They will now drop the following cubes that can only be put into storage, can only be used on level 200 or lower equipment, and will restrict the equipment that they are used on from being traded for 30 days (‘Memento’ feature). Memento Silver Cube Memento Gold Cube Memento Bronze Additional Cube When they do things such as adjust boss crystal prices, there’s always a dilemma where players that are levelling up and trying to get stronger to face higher bosses end up lacking mesos to upgrade their gear. Now, they will be able to use these materials to gradually upgrade their own gear without risks. The 30 day trade restriction is to emphasize that they are meant to be used directly on your own gear, not to sell to other players. These rewards will drop from bosses between Hard Damien to Easy Kaling. Star Force Enhancement Reorganization When you destroy an item when Star Force enhancing it, you can now use additional spares and mesos to restore the trace to the star that it boomed at (up to 22 stars). For example, for level 200 equipment: 15 Stars: 1 equip, 433m 16 Stars: 1 equip, 1.05b mesos 17 Stars: 1 equip, 1.77b mesos 18 Stars: 1 equip, 4.01b mesos 19 Stars: 2 equips, 6.65b mesos 20 Stars: 2 equips, 11.8b mesos 21 Stars: 3 equips, 14.8b mesos 22 Stars: 4 equips, 24.2b mesos Star Catching will be removed and the increased success rate will be added to the base rates. Bonus Stats Enhancement Reorganization. You can now reset an items’ bonus stats (flames) with mesos. Auto Enchant You can now automatically enchant potential, bonus stats, and Star Force. You can set a goal and it will keep going automatically until the goal is reached or you run out of mesos or materials. Potential: You can set 4 different possibilities. Bonus Stats: You can set the desired values for the different bonus stats. Star Force: You can set the desired star you want to stop at, as well as if you want to protect it at 15/16/17 stars. Bonus Stats/Potential Transfer Scrolls The Bonus Stats Transfer Scroll lets you transfer an equipment’s bonus stats to another of the same exact equipment (the original equipment will be destroyed). It costs 49k NX. The Potential Transfer Scroll lets you transfer an equipment’s potential (not additional potential) to another of the same exact equipment (the original equipment will be destroyed). It costs 99k NX. The goal of these two items is to make it easier to work on your own equipment directly and to be able to complete items while avoiding RNG elements. Trading is a key part of MapleStory and even previously, many users could come together to work on a single item. These scrolls can help with that as even if one person rolls something really good but can’t upgrade the rest of the item to its full value, they can use these items to extract those stats and sell them. When making your own items, at some point you’ll hit an RNG wall where you just have to brute force it to finish a part of it. These scrolls are another option to do that without having to resort to RNG. This is a very big change so they will be examining them closely and make additional changes if necessary. Along the same lines as the above addition, Complete Under Control and Mitra’s Rage can now be traded using the Platinum Karma Scissors 5 times. There’s definitely players that have had the experience of getting a 60% attack Mitra’s Rage which is very good, but then later on, to try and upgrade it to 63% attack, you would have to either cube it yourself or buy a completely new one. Now, you can resell without disappointment. Newly Added Content World Archive: Arcane River will be added. Sol Janus & Erda Fountain These skills’ damage will be adjusted to have different damage values for each job (just like Sol Hecate). Previously, all jobs had the same damage % but there’s obviously differences in passives for each class which need to be accounted for so they can use the skills effectively with a similar efficiency. Sol Hecate A feature to change Sol Hecate’s appearance will be added. You can choose between the following: Sol Hecate: Amicus Sol Hecate: Zephyr Sol Hecate: Nebula Sol Hecate: Draco Sol Hecate: Helia Lots of players shared the opinion that Sol Hecate was ugly when it came out. Originally, they wanted to release this skill with these skin options at the same time but didn’t have time. You can customize your Sol Hecate’s appearance and colour to whichever you want. It costs 1b mesos to customize it. They may sell more fancy designs later on but they haven’t confirmed that yet. 3rd Common Core The 3rd Common Core will be a powerful transformation of the common job branch 5th job skills. (similar to Mastery Cores) Blitz Shield Arcana Overdrive Evolve Ultimate Dark Sight Pirate Flag Cygnus Phalanx Freud’s Wisdom Call Mastema Resistance Infantry Pantheon Magic Circuit Full Drive Transcendent Another World’s Afterimage Lotus Flower Most of these skills are quite useful but they may be lacking in design or combat performance. This Common Core will enhance them and upgrade their visual aspects to match each separate job. They will be revealed in the April update. Roadmap March 19 Gameplay Reorganizations Level 200~260 experience required halved. Boss pattern reorganization Special Skill Ring reorganization Auto Enchant system Economy Reorganization Enhancement reorganization Boss reward reorganization Training Ground World Archive – Arcane River April 16 3rd Common Core Update Sol Hecate Appearance Change Sol Janus/Erda Fountain Reorganization May 14 Pre-Summer (coming soon) Events 23rd Anniversary Maple Attack – Mascot Parade Change Burning: Lucid MapleStory & BTS Jin (Jin’s Mysterious Garden/Jin’s Hot Time Event/New Jin Event) Hyper Burning Booster Offline Events March 14: MapleStory in LotteWorld April 3: Maple Island Opens April 29: LotteWorld All-Day Rental May 22: LotteWorld Tower/Mall/Seokchon Lake Special Displays

items.summary: The third and final patch of the MapleStory Crown update, featuring the new level 295 area Geardrak and the new end-game boss Jupiter! It also includes the...

kmsBreakdown.summary: The third and final patch of the MapleStory Crown update, featuring the new level 295 area Geardrak and the new end-game boss Jupiter! It also includes the Destiny Weapon 2nd transcendence, the Guild Castle system, and the MapleStory x Frieren: Beyond Journey’s End collaboration.

details.value: When you enter Jupiter, you will be moved to the Shackles of Order and Chaos waiting map. Here, you and your party members can move to the left or right side of the map to decide who will fight Jupiter and who will fight the Alchemic Matter. Up to 2 party members can be on each side.

When the fight starts, you will be placed in the side that you chose previously. You can use the Ether Warp skill with the NPC Chat button to move to the other side (only if there are not already 2 players there).

At the top of the screen below the timer, you’ll see the Rupture Value. This is your personal Death Count in this boss, starting at 0. When you die in this boss, it will increase by 150 (separated) or 200 (combined). There are also other patterns that increase or decrease it. If it reaches 1000, you will fail and be kicked out of the boss.
The timer at the bottom is for the Merge pattern.

The main mechanic of the boss is based on the Magic Sync gauge. When you are hit by either boss, the gauge will move towards that boss. The timer on the bottom is the Objective pattern where Jupiter will check if the gauge is balanced (in the blue area in the middle) every so often, and if not, your Rupture value will go up.

Both bosses share the same HP bar, but they have separate patterns and ‘maps’. You can see your other party members and the other boss on the other side but you can’t interact with them unless you swap sides.

When the Merge timer hits 0, Jupiter and the Alchemic Matter will merge into a single boss. You will face Jupiter of Perfection if the Magic Sync gauge was pushed to the Jupiter side, Jupiter of Balance if the gauge was in the centre, or Jupiter of Destruction if it was pushed to the Alchemic Matter side.
When the timer hits 0 here, the boss will separate again, and the cycle will start over.
[Back to Overview]
[Boss Event] The One That Swallowed a God
Stop Jupiter from obtaining power through the god’s furnace Cronos, and acquire various rewards!
Event Participation Requirements:

items: Level 295 or higher characters that have completed the [Geardrak] area quests.

details.value: You can select the ‘[Boss Event] The One That Swallowed a God’ event in the Event List and click the ‘Participate’ button to start the event.
※ If you are logged in to another account in the same Nexon ID at the same time, you cannot participate in the Clear event.
※ If you participate in the event in an abormal way, your game or content usage may be restricted and you will be excluded from the event participation and rewards.
Regular Clear Mission
You can receive clear rewards through the Event List by defeating Jupiter on any difficulty at least 1 time during the event period.
※ You cannot complete the missions if you are not on a Green PC.
※ You cannot complete the missions by clearing in Practice Mode or Story Mode.
※ After clearing Jupiter (Normal/Hard), you must enter the reward map to satisfy the clear mission requirements.
※ You can participate in the event by clearing the boss in a party of 2 or more as well.
First Clear Mission
You can complete the First Clear Mission by being part of one of the first 3 parties that defeat Hard Jupiter during the event period.
If you complete the First Clear mission, you can acquire the following rewards through the Event List during the separate reward claiming period.
※ After defeating Jupiter (Hard), you must defeat the reward monster ‘Alchemic Matter’s Remnants’ to be recognized as a clear.
※ Your clear will be acknowledged even if you clear in Cross World Boss Content.
※ You cannot participate in the First Clear Mission if you are in Practice Mode or not on a Green PC. Even if you defeat the reward monster, it will not be recognized as a First Clear.
※ The First Clear mission rewards can be claimed after the March 19, 2026 maintenance, and can only be claimed on the Maple ID that completed the mission.
※ If your game usage is restricted due to violations of the operating policy during the event period, you may be excluded from receiving the First Clear mission rewards.
Commemoration Gifts
If Hard Jupiter is defeated during the event period, you can receive commemoration rewards by talking to NPCs of the characters that first cleared it in major towns.
※ Each reward can only be claimed 1 time per Maple ID.
※ When you use the Jupiter Magic Circle Voucher, the item you acquire will be permanent and untradeable.
[Back to Overview]
Destiny 2nd Transcendence
The Adversary’s Destiny Weapon has finally blossomed. To reach a higher level, the sages have offered three trials. Will the Adversary discover their own truths in the ongoing battles?
Participation Requirements:

items: Characters who have completed the ‘[Destiny Weapon] Adversary’s Weapon’ quest and have a Destiny Weapon.

details.value: ※ You can open the Destiny 2nd Transcendence mission UI through the ‘[Destiny Weapon] Adversary’s Faith’ quest.
Decisive Battle Quests

You can complete the Decisive Battle quests by solo clearing boss monsters in Destiny Mode with the corresponding mission requirements as well as collecting the required amount of Adversary’s Resolve.
※ You cannot participate in Decisive Battle quests in cross world boss content.
You can acquire Adversary’s Resolve by defeating certain difficulty of Grandis boss monsters or higher.
※ When you defeat a boss, you will acquire the Adversary’s Resolve divided equally based on the number of party members that entered.
※ You can accumulate up to 15,000 Adversary’s Resolve. You can use the excess Adversary’s Resolve in the next Decisive Battle quest.

After completing all of the Decisive Battle quests, you can transcend your Destiny 1st Transcendence Weapon into the Destiny 2nd Transcendence Weapon through the ‘[Destiny Weapon] Adversary’s Dignity’ quest.

items: The Destiny 2nd Transcendence Weapon will be provided by transferring the stats of your Destiny 1st Transcendence Weapon as follows. Level 250 equipment requirement. All upgrade slots will be consumed with 15% Spell Trace enhancements matching your job. 22 Star Force enhancement The maximum Star Force enhancement limit will be changed to 25 stars. The Destiny 1st Transcendence Weapon’s potential will be transferred. The Destiny 1st Transcendence Weapon’s additional potential will be transferred. The Destiny 1st Transcendence Weapon’s bonus stats will be transferred. The Destiny 1st Transcendence Weapon’s Soul will be transferred. Additional scroll enhancements cannot be made.

details.value: [Back to Overview]
Guild Castle
A hideout just for your guild members, the guild castle! Grow the guild castle with your guild members and create unforgettable memories.
Participation Requirements:

items: A guild that has reached Guild Level 20.
The guild master can create a guild castle by talking to the NPC Benedict in the Guild Headquarters <Hall of Heroes>.

details.value: After the guild castle is created, you can click the Guild Casttle button in the Guild UI to access it.
Guild Castle Growth

items: The castle can grow up to level 20.
As the castle grows, various features will become available.
The castle grows using Energy acquired from guild members participating in guild content.
You can only start acquiring Guild Castle Energy 7 days after you join a guild.

details.value: Crafting Decorations and Placing Decorations

items: Guild members can participate in various content to collect materials that can be used to craft guild decorations.
The Guild Master can craft decorations using the materials through the Crafting UI.
The Guild Master can place crafted decorations in the Guild Castle by using Placement Mode.
Decorations can only be placed in customizable areas, such as the lobby, guild room, greenhouse, and party room.
You can also acquire special decorations to decorate the greenhouse through guild missions performed jointly by the guild.

details.value: ※ Among the guild missions, the boss clear mission can only be done 7 days after joining a guild.
Lobby

items: The guild castle Lobby serves as the centre of the guild castle and connects the various maps inside.
If your guild castle is below level 10, you will have a small Lobby that only contains the Guild Room and Supply Station.
If your guild castle is level 10 or higher, you will have a bigger Lobby that also contains the Party Room, Greenhouse, and Enhancement Altar.

You can use the item display cases to display items, and the guild honor statues to display specific guild members. As your guild castle level increases, you will be able to use more item display cases and statues.

details.value: Supply Station

items: You can use the guild castle’s Crafting skill.
You can talk to the NPC Susan to use the guild shop.
You can access the Crafting UI by talking to the NPC Oliver or by clicking the Crafting button in the guild castle menu.
You can access the guild’s shared storage, the Guild Safes.
The guild master can designate individual guild members’ weekly entry/exit limits and change the safes’ names.
The Guild Safes can only hold consumable, etc, and install items.
As the guild castle’s level increases, more Guild Safes will become available.

details.value: ※ You can access the Supply Station 24 hours after joining a guild.
※ Guild members without access rights cannot use this feature.
※ If a guild is disbanded, the items stored in the guild safes will disappear.
Guild Room

items: This is a custom room that you can decorate with various furniture.

details.value: Party Room

items: This is a custom room that you can decorate with various furniture.
You can talk to the NPC Party Master Pierre to play minigames with your guild members.

details.value: Greenhouse

items: This is a custom room that you can decorate with various furniture.
You can place wood decorations and boss statues acquired from guild missions.

details.value: Enhancement Altar

items: When you use the Enhancement Altar to Star Force enhance an item, your enhancement status will be shared with your guild members.

details.value: ※ The Enhancement Altar is unlocked when your guild castle reaches level 15.
※ The Enhancement Altar can be used up to 30 times per day per guild.
※ The Enhancement Altar can only be used if there are 2 or more guild members in the room.
※ You can only register equipment items that can reach 20 stars or higher, or Superior equipment items.
※ You can only access this feature 24 hours after joining a guild.
※ Guild members without access rights cannot use this feature.
※ The usage time will begin as soon as you open the Enhancement Altar UI. You can use it for up to 30 minutes.
※ If you are inactive for 3 minutes or more, you will be automatically removed.
Guild Castle Minigame – Quiz Us

items: Participation Method: Select <Quiz Us> in the minigame UI to enter the Quiz Room. ※ Up to 200 players can enter the Quiz Room.
Select <Quiz Us> in the minigame UI to enter the Quiz Room.
※ Up to 200 players can enter the Quiz Room.

Gameplay: You can talk to the NPC Party Master Pierre in the Quiz Room to create a quiz. Anyone can do so. ※ You cannot create a quiz if a quiz is already in progress. You can create up to 10 quizzes. The quiz creater must enter each question and answer before starting the quiz. ※ Profanity and certain symbols are not allowed in quiz questions and answers. When a round begins, the question will appear at the top of the screen. Participates can enter the correct answer by using the </answer> command. The round lasts 30 seconds. The first person to enter the answer matching the one provided by the quiz creater will receive 1 point. The creater can use the </hint> command to provide hints. The creator’s hints will be displayed at the bottom right corner for all participants. The participant with the highest score at the end of all the rounds will be crowned the final winner.
You can talk to the NPC Party Master Pierre in the Quiz Room to create a quiz. Anyone can do so.
※ You cannot create a quiz if a quiz is already in progress.
You can create up to 10 quizzes. The quiz creater must enter each question and answer before starting the quiz.

The round lasts 30 seconds. The first person to enter the answer matching the one provided by the quiz creater will receive 1 point.
The creater can use the </hint> command to provide hints. The creator’s hints will be displayed at the bottom right corner for all participants.
The participant with the highest score at the end of all the rounds will be crowned the final winner.

details.value: Guild Castle Minigame – Sky Sovereigns

items: Participation Method: Select <Sky Sovereigns> in the minigame UI to enter the Quiz Room. ※ You cannot enter if a game is already in progress or if the waiting room already has 20 players The first character that enters the waiting UI will be selected as that game’s leader. The leader can change the map. Participants can freely change teams by clicking on the empty slots. When all participants are ready, the leader can start the game.
Select <Sky Sovereigns> in the minigame UI to enter the Quiz Room.
※ You cannot enter if a game is already in progress or if the waiting room already has 20 players
The first character that enters the waiting UI will be selected as that game’s leader. The leader can change the map.
Participants can freely change teams by clicking on the empty slots.
When all participants are ready, the leader can start the game.

Gameplay Up to 20 players can enter a single game. ※ All buff effects will be removed upon entering. ※ If you are inactive for 10 seconds after entering, you will receive a warning. If you receive 2 warnings, you will be kicked from the game. Sky Sovereigns has a 3 minute time limit. Your character’s nametag, HP bar, and skill colours will be based on your team colour. When jumping, you can press the jump key again to do a Double Jump. You can use the ‘Hammer Swing!’ skill. You can hold the key down to charge the hammer. You can release it to swing the hammer, dealing damage, stunning, and knocking back multiple enemies. When the energy is filled, the stun duration and knockback distance are increased. Items will appear throughout the map, and you can acquire them to use item skills. Bomb Throw!: When used, throw a bomb in front of you to damage and stun multiple enemies. High Barrier: When used, you become immune to damage and stuns for a certain period of time. However, you cannot ignore death from the electricity currents. You can change hotkeys by clicking on the skill icons. During the game, you can press the ‘Tab’ key to check the current score for all characters. You can acquire individual points by killing enemies or destroying Golden Hammers. Enemy Kills: 1 point Golden Hammer Destroyed: 5 points At the end of the game, the RED and BLUE teams’ invidual scores are added up and the team with the higher score wins.

items: The regular guild skills will be changed as follows. Guild Lv. 1 Guild’s Know-How I: 30 attack/magic attack, 10% damage reduction. Guild Lv. 5 Guild’s Know-How II: 6 attack/magic attack. Special Power: 30 Arcane Force, 15 Star Force. I’ll Go To You: Teleport to a designated guild member’s location, 1 hour cooldown. Guild Lv. 10 Guild’s Know-How III: 12% normal monster damage. Merchant: 4% discount when buying items from a shop. However, this discount does not apply if the resell price is 70% or more of the selling price. Guild Lv. 15 Guild’s Know-How IV: 4 attack/magic attack. Master of Scroll Enhancements: When scroll enhancing using Spell Traces or special scrolls, increases success rate by +4%. Guild Lv. 20 Guild’s Know-How V: 40 all stats, 2000 HP. Not Afraid of Death: Death penalty’s duration is decreased by 30%. Guild Lv. 25 Guild’s Know-How VI: 5 attack/magic attack. Craftsman of Scroll Enhancements: When a scroll enhancement fails, there is a 4% chance to not consume a scroll slot. Guild Lv. 30 Sharenian’s Demon Riding: When you ride Sharenian’s Demon, 190 speed and 120 jump.

sections.title: Monsters

details.value: Radiant Malefic Star (Normal)’s HP has been decreased by about 30%.
Developer Notes:
On the February 5th Maple Now, many players provided feedback about Radiant Malefic Star (Normal)’s boss difficulty setting, so we conducted an internal review.

After reviewing, we confirmed that even considering the differences in mechanics between Kaling and Radiant Malefic Star, Radiant Malefic Star (Normal)’s HP was excessively higher than Kaling’s. Therefore, we will adjust Radiant Malefic Star (Normal)’s HP to fit better in the official server update.
We apologize again for not being able to sufficiently verify numerical consistency and we will do our best to provide you with better service in the future.
The hitboxes for the monsters that appear when using VIP Boosters and Express Boosters have been increased by about 52%.
[Back to Overview]

details.value: The Maple Home preset feature has been added.

items: This feature lets you save and load your currently decorated Maple Home theme, building design, furniture, and platforms.
A total of 3 preset slots are provided.
You can set a name for each preset.

details.value: ※ Presets can be saved per world.
※ Names that violate game policies may result in sanctions.
※ When a preset is loaded, for items placed in a display case, they will be loaded based on the items that are currently displayed and not the items that were there at the time the preset was saved.
※ If a Maple Home theme that was saved in a preset has been extracted and you no longer have it, you cannot load that preset.

sections.summary: Supplementary work is being done to alleviate lag issues in certain content.

items: Fixed an issue where the ‘[Maple Union] Champion’s Path’ quest could be started by characters in a Union raid lower than Grand Master I rank.

details.value: [Back to Overview]

items: Fixed an issue where the ‘Book of Luminous’ achievement would not be refreshed on characters that already met the requirements.

details.value: [Back to Overview]

items: Fixed an issue in Practice Mode where even if you selected the Mashoor’s Gift Weather Effect item, you could use it through the Buff Sequence.
Fixed an issue where in certain situations, even if you used certain buff items, the durations would not be refreshed.
Fixed an issue where the Snowflame Riding (Permanent) Voucher tooltip would display ‘unowned’ even if you had it.
Fixed an issue where the Name Engraver and Name Remover items could not be used on Astra subweapons.
Fixed an issue where emoticons would appear awkwardly if you had a chat bubble ring with animated effects equipped.
Fixed an issue where the preview when naming the Malefic Staroid looked awkward.
Fixed an issue where in specific situations, the Intense Power Crystals’ sale details’ number of players defeated with would be displayed awkwardly.

details.value: [Back to Overview]

items: Fixed an issue in the First Adversary (Easy/Normal/Hard/Extreme) phase 2 where character chat bubbles were covered by the element effects.
Fixed an issue in Kai (Normal/Hard) where absolute bind effects were not applied during the Groggy pattern.
Fixed an issue in Kai (Normal/Hard) where entering phase 2 would sometimes cause you to exit even if you had time remaining.
Fixed an issue in Kai (Normal/Hard) where defeating the reward monster would sometimes cause you to exit even if you had time remaining.

details.value: [Back to Overview]

items: Fixed an issue in the ‘Where Earth Sleeps’ map where certain portals’ locations were awkward.
Fixed an issue in the ‘Labyrinth of Suffering Deepest Core 1’ and ‘Labyrinth of Suffering Interior 2’ maps where certain platforms were awkward.
Fixed an issue in the ‘Monster Park Carcion Stage 3: Entangling Forest’ map where the portal images were awkward.
Fixed an issue in the ‘Arteria Supply Depot’ map where certain objects were awkward.
Fixed an issue in the ‘Beach Rocky Area 1’ map where certain ropes were awkward.
Fixed an issue in Champion Raid: Dragon Island where if Mercedes linked skills, the character would not switch even if the control duration ran out.
Fixed an issue in Champion Raid content maps where channel names were displayed awkwardly.

details.value: [Back to Overview]

items: Fixed an issue with the Maple Guide UI’s Special Content tab where clicking certain button images moved awkwardly when clicked.
Fixed an issue with the Union Artifact’s artifact mission UI where the ‘Complete the Nightmare Paradise quests’ artifact special mission was displayed in an awkward order.
Fixed an issue with the Soul Collection where Suu/Annihilation Weapon Suu’s normal illustrations’ display coordinates were awkward.
Fixed an issue where if a character died with a Soul Weapon equipped, the Soul Weapon UI could not be opened until the Soul Gauge reset.

Fixed an issue where if you had the Union’s Wealth Stage 2 effect applied then used the Buff Sequence’s ‘use unapplied buffs’ feature, the Union’s Wealth Stage 3 would be applied.
Fixed an issue where you could move intermittently while the minigame UI was open.
Fixed an issue with the item trade UI where the decorative tab’s item list was displayed awkwardly.
Fixed an issue in the cross world boss content <Dimensional Battlefield> where the cross world boss UI’s Find Party button did not work properly.
Fixed an issue with the Fast Login UI where if the displayed character was deleted, the UI would display ‘retrieving information’ rather than ‘no character set for fast login’.
Fixed an issue with Monster Collection where double-clicking Arma or Arma’s Subordinate monsters would not display the world map.
Fixed an issue with item trades where in certain situations, the amount of mesos acquired through the trade was not displayed properly.
Fixed an issue with the Maple Guide UI’s per-level content tab and quick move section where even if you dragged the scroll button to the bottom, there would still be a small amount of scroll space.
Fixed an issue in the guid skill UI where Noblesse guild skills did not show that they could be registered in a Buff Sequence.
Fixed an issue where achievements for cumulative number of daily quest completions did not count when completing Arcane River/Grandis daily quests with the Symbol Quick Pass UI.

Events

sections.title: Accelerated Hunting! Haste BEYOND

details.value: Receive abundant experience benefits and items with the returning Haste event!
Event Participation Requirements:

items: A level 200 or higher character (or a Zero character that has completed story quest Chapter 2).

details.value: Event Period:

items: February 12, 2026 after the maintenance ~ March 18, 2026 at 11:59 PM.

details.value: You can select the ‘Accelerated Hunting! Haste BEYOND’ event in the Event List and click the ‘Participate’ button to start the event.
After starting the event, you can check the Haste BEYOND field benefits and special mission rewards through the Event List.
※ The field benefits and special mission rewards can be checked and claimed until March 18, 2026 at 11:59 PM.
Event Period Special Benefits
If you login during the event period, you will receive the ‘Haste BEYOND’ skill.
※ The ‘Haste BEYOND’ skill will be applied until March 18, 2026 at 11:59 PM.
Every time you defeat a certain number of monsters within your level range, you will receive Haste BEYOND field benefit effects.
※ Monsters within your level range refers to monsters -20~+20 of your character level.
※ The event will only progress if you defeat monsters within your level range while you are in a party of 1 or not in a party.
※ Haste Treasure Boxes will appear when you defeat 10,000 monsters within your level range on a level 200 or higher character in instanced maps.
※ Haste Treasure Boxes will give a gem that gives a lot of experience based on the Treasure Box’s rank.
※ Haste Treasure Boxes are not included in Treasure Hunter achievement counts.
※ You can discover up to 3 Haste Treasure Boxes per day per Maple ID.
※ The Haste BEYOND field benefits will be applied until March 18, 2026 at 11:59 PM.
Haste Fever Time
After participating in the event, you can use the Haste Fever Time through the special UI that appears when you are in a map with monsters within your level range or through the Haste UI.
When you use the Haste Fever Time, Haste Inferno monsters that give lots of experience will be summoned for 5 minutes.
When you defeat the Haste Inferno monsters, there is a low chance of acquiring the Cassandra’s Crystal Ball item that helps you defeat monsters within your level range.
※ The Haste Fever Time can be used 1 time per day per Maple ID.
※ The Haste Fever Time can only be used on level 200 or higher characters that have completed the ‘[5th Job] Another Power, Arcane Force’ quest.
※ Haste Inferno monsters are not affected by any additional experience effects.
※ When you acquire the Cassandra’s Crystal Ball item, the Cassandra’s Crystal Ball skill will be activated and attack nearby enemies.
※ The Cassandra’s Crystal Ball skill can stack up to 5 times, and the attack range will be increased/attack cycle will be decreased per stack.
※ When you acquire the Cassandra’s Crystal Ball item, the skill duration will be increased by 10 seconds, and can be increased by up to 90 seconds.
Special Missions
You can complete missions by defeating a certain number of monsters within your level range.
※ Monsters within your level range refers to monsters -20~+20 of your character level.
※ The event will only progress if you defeat monsters within your level range while you are in a party of 1 or not in a party.
※ The special mission progress record is shared between all characters within the Maple ID.
※ When you complete each mission, the cumulative number of monsters defeated will not be reset.
You can receive rewards for each completed mission through the Haste UI.
※ All reward items can only be claimed 1 time per Maple ID.
※ The ‘Flame Wolf Action Damage Skin (Unit)’ can be extracted with a Damage Skin Extractor.
※ When you use the ‘I’m Quite Hasted Title Voucher’, you will receive the ‘I’m Quite Hasted’ title that is permament. The title’s stats will expire 30 days after you use the voucher.
※ The ‘I’m Quite Hasted’ title’s stats are as follows.

items: 50 all stats
20 attack/magic attack
30% boss damage
30% defense ignore
2,500 HP/MP

details.value: [Back to Overview]

sections.summary: Receive abundant experience benefits and items with the returning Haste event!

sections.title: MapleStory Winter Event [Night of Falling Illusions]

details.value: [Attendance Check Event] Fantasia Finale
To conclude Tallahart’s illusion magic festival, listen to Riche’s request and receive gifts!
Event Participation Requirements:

items: A level 101 or higher character (or a Zero character that has completed story quest Chapter 2) that has participated in the ‘Night of Falling Illusions’ event.

details.value: Event Period:

items: March 5, 2026 at 10:00 AM ~ March 18, 2026 at 11:59 PM.

details.value: You can select the ‘Night of Falling Illusions’ event in the Event List and click the ‘Participate’ button to start this event with the other Night of Falling Illusions event. Or you can select the ‘[Attendance Check] Fantasia Finale’ event.
※ The <Fantasia Finale> participation records are shared between all characters within the Maple ID.
Daily Attendance Check

Every day, you can defeat 1,000 monsters within your level range and press the ‘Attendance Check’ button to complete the daily attendance check and receive rewards. You can complete it up to 10 times during the event period.

You can click the <Use> button in the top-right corner of the UI to use the Finale Pass to complete 1 attendance check you did not previously complete. It can be used up to the number of missed days as of the previous day, and costs 3,000 Maple Points per use.
※ The number of monsters defeated record is shared between all characters within the Maple ID.
※ The event will only progress if you defeat monsters within your level range while you are in a party of 1 or not in a party.
Attendance Check Rewards
When you complete an attendancec check, you can press the <Receive> button to receive the corresponding reward. You can press the <Receive All Rewards> button to claim all rewards at once.
※ The attendance check rewards can be claimed 1 time per Maple ID.
※ All reward items can only be put into storage.
※ All reward items can be claimed until March 18, 2026 at 11:59 PM, and will expire on March 19, 2026 at 2:00 AM.
※ The ‘Illusion’s Ellanos Voucher’ can only be used after creating a Maple Home. The furniture acquired from the voucher is an item that can be applied in Maple Home.
[Back to Overview]

sections.summary: To conclude Tallahart’s illusion magic festival, listen to Riche’s request and receive gifts!

sections.title: Frontier Pass

details.value: Cassandra’s support knows no bounds! Level up your Frontier Pass and receive special gifts!
Event Participation Requirements:

items: Level 101 or higher characters created (or a Zero character that has completed story quest Chapter 2) after November 20, 2025 at 12:00 AM.

details.value: Event Period:

items: February 12, 2026 after the maintenance ~ April 15, 2025 at 11:59 PM.

details.value: You can select the ‘Frontier Pass’ event in the Event List and click the ‘Participate’ button to start the event.
※ The Frontier Pass event can only be participated in 1 time per person.
※ You cannot change the character or Maple ID that participates in the Frontier Pass event.
※ Even if you delete the character that participates in the Frontier Pass event, you cannot participate on another character.
Frontier Pass Level and Weekly Missions
The Fronter Pass can go up to level 30. You can reach the next level by acquiring 480 Pass Points.
You can acquire Frontier Pass Points by completing weekly missions.
You can acquire up to 2,000 Frontier Pass Points per week.
※ The Frontier Pass missions can only be progressed on the character that is participating in the event.
※ The weekly Frontier Pass point acquisition and weekly mission progress are reset every Thursday at midnight.
※ Weekly bosses defeated/Symbol daily quests/Monster Park and Monster Park Extreme cleared before participating in the Frontier Pass event will not be reflected in the Frontier Pass weekly missions.
You can buy Frontier Pass Points based on the amount of Points you did not acquire from the previous week. It costs 1000 Maple Points per 100 Pass Points.
Pass Level Achievement Rewards
If you buy the Premium Pass, you can claim the Special Support Items.
※ All reward items can only be acquired on the character participating in the event, and can only be claimed 1 time.
※ When you use the Selective Symbol Voucher, you can choose between 5 Selective Arcane Symbol Vouchers or 2 Selective Authentic Symbol Vouchers.
※ The VIP Sauna Entry Tickets can only be put into storage. All other items are untradeable.
※ All reward items can only be claimed until April 15, 2026 at 11:59 PM.
※ The Sol Erda Fragments are permanent items. All other items will expire on April 16, 2026 at 2:00 AM.
Premium Pass
The Frontier Premium Pass product will be sold.

items: Sale Duration: February 12, 2026 after the maintenance ~ April 15, 2026 at 11:00 PM.
Price: 30,000 NX
If you buy the Premium Pass, you can claim the Special Support Items. You will also receive the following benefits. +200% normal monster damage +20% additional experience acquisition
+200% normal monster damage
+20% additional experience acquisition

details.value: ※ The Frontier Premium Pass will expire on April 15, 2026 at 11:59 PM.
※ Regardless of the purchase date, the Premium Pass effects will be applied until April 15, 2026 at 11:59 PM.
※ The ‘Frontier Premium Pass’ can only be purchased 1 time per person.
※ The ‘Frontier Premium Pass’ can only be purchased on the character participating in the event.
※ If you buy the Premium Pass, the benefits will be maintained until the Frontier Pass event ends.

sections.title: Momentum Pass

details.value: Cassandra’s support is speeding up! Level up the Momentum Pass and receive rewards!
Event Participation Requirements:

items: A level 280 or higher character (or a Zero character that has completed story quest Chapter 2).

details.value: Event Period:

items: February 12, 2026 after the maintenance ~ March 18, 2026 at 11:59 PM.

details.value: You can select the ‘Momentum Pass’ event in the Event List and click the ‘Participate’ button to start the event.
※ You can only participate in this event on one character per person.
※ The character and Maple ID participating in this event cannot be changed afterwards.
※ Even if you delete the character that participates in the Momentum Pass event, you cannot participate in the event on another character.
Momentum Pass Levels and Weekly Missions
The Momentum Pass levels range from level 0~10. You can reach the next level by acquiring 720 Momentum Pass Points.
You can acquire Momentum Pass Points by completing weekly missions.
You can acquire up to 2,000 Momentum Pass Points per week.
When you have acquired all of the Momentum Pass Points you can for thet week, all remaining uncompleted missions will be completed.
※ The missions can only be progressed on the character that is participating in the event.
※ The weekly Momentum Pass point acquisition and weekly mission progress are reset every Thursday at midnight.
※ Symbol daily quests, Monster Park, Monster Park Extreme, and Epic Dungeon missions completed before participating in the event are not included in the weekly mission progress.
You can buy Momentum Pass Points based on the amount of Points you did not acquire from the previous week.
※ 100 Momentum Pass Points costs 1,000 Maple Points.
Pass Level Achievement Rewards
If you buy the Prime Momentum Pass, you can claim the Prime Momentum Rewards.
※ All rewards can be claimed during the event period until March 18, 2026 at 11:59 PM.
※ All rewards will expire on March 19, 2026 at 2:00 AM.
※ All rewards except for the VIP Sauna Entry Tickets are untradeable.
※ The VIP Sauna Entry Tickets can only be put into storage.
Prime Momentum Pass
The Prime Momentum Pass will be available for purchase.

items: Sale Period: February 12, 2026 after the maintenance ~ March 18, 2026 at 11:00 PM
Price: 49,800 NX (cannot be purchased with Maple Points or Mileage)

items: February 12, 2026 after the maintenance ~ March 18, 2026 at 11:59 PM.

details.value: During the event period, you can leap your characters to your desired world through the NPC Isaga in Henesys. You can select the ‘Challengers World Early Leap’ event in the Event List and click the ‘Participate’ button to move to Henesys.
※ Worlds You can Leap To: All worlds.
※ You can only move to the first world you select through the Challengers World Early Leap and End Leap.
※ You can move a total of 5 characters to other worlds through the Challengers World Early Leap and End Leap.

items: Dim Illusion Memories, Bright Illusion Memories, Complete Illusion Memories, Challengers Coins, Advanced Challengers Coins, and Premium Coins acquired in Challengers World will not be moved with the World Leap.
Challengers World Bronze/Silver/Gold/Platinum rewards that can be acquired from Challengers World missions can only be claimed in Challengers World.
Rewards that can be acquired from the Challengers Pass can only be claimed in Challengers World.
The Challengers Pass: Special Support buff will be removed.
The Challengers EXP Duo’s designated Challengers World character will be removed and you will no longer acquire Duo Points until the end of the usage period.

The Tallahart Fantasia’s Illusion Magic Enhancement’s levels will not be moved. Your acquired Memory Traces and Reminiscense Traces will be maintained, so after the World Leap, you can set the levels again your destination world.

If you World Leap without receiving Hyper Burning MAX level achievement rewards, you will receive them based on the world you move to. Please claim the enhanced level achievement rewards in Challengers World before you leap.
The Mystic Seal event’s equipped Seal Core stats will be removed.
Characters with active Meso Market transactions cannot proceed with World Leap.
When World Leaping from Challengers World, items in the cash storage, storage, and Maple Auction will not be moved.
Custom Backgrounds acquired using Voucher items will not be moved.

details.value: ※ Custom Backgrounds acquired using Voucher items can be extracted into Vouchers through the NPC Isaga’s ‘Extract Custom Background’ option. The acquired Voucher can only be put into storage and is permanent.

items: If you leap without checking the results of a custom illustration request, the request will be cancelled. Please confirm the request before leaping.
The Tera Burning effect applied when a character is created in Challengers World will not be transferred through World Leap.

details.value: ※ The Tera Burning effect will be removed when the character is transferred, and the ‘Tera Burning Gift Giveaway!’ quest will no longer be available.

items: The Strange Cube, Karma Meister Cube, Karma Strange Additional Cube, and Karma Star Force 17 Star Enhancement Scroll (Level 160) purchased from the Challengers Shop are Challengers World-only items and you cannot World Leap while holding them.
The Selective Challengers Level 4 Special Skill Ring Voucher and the Special Skill Ring acquired from using it are Challengers World-only items and you cannot World Leap while holding them.
The Unstable Time Fragments are exclusive to Challengers World and you cannot leap while you have them in your inventory.
If a character that has cleared an Epic Dungeon uses World Leap, you cannot claim the cleared Epic Dungeon’s bonus rewards.
If there are no empty character slots in the world you are moving to, slots will be added before the Leap.
However, if you already have 50 characters in the destination world, you cannot Leap to that world.

details.value: When you enter Jupiter, you will be moved to the Shackles of Order and Chaos waiting map. Here, you and your party members can move to the left or right side of the map to decide who will fight Jupiter and who will fight the Alchemic Matter. Up to 2 party members can be on each side.

When the fight starts, you will be placed in the side that you chose previously. You can use the Ether Warp skill with the NPC Chat button to move to the other side (only if there are not already 2 players there).

At the top of the screen below the timer, you’ll see the Rupture Value. This is your personal Death Count in this boss, starting at 0. When you die in this boss, it will increase by 150 (separated) or 200 (combined). There are also other patterns that increase or decrease it. If it reaches 1000, you will fail and be kicked out of the boss.
The timer at the bottom is for the Merge pattern.

The main mechanic of the boss is based on the Magic Sync gauge. When you are hit by either boss, the gauge will move towards that boss. The timer on the bottom is the Objective pattern where Jupiter will check if the gauge is balanced (in the blue area in the middle) every so often, and if not, your Rupture value will go up.

Both bosses share the same HP bar, but they have separate patterns and ‘maps’. You can see your other party members and the other boss on the other side but you can’t interact with them unless you swap sides.

When the Merge timer hits 0, Jupiter and the Alchemic Matter will merge into a single boss. You will face Jupiter of Perfection if the Magic Sync gauge was pushed to the Jupiter side, Jupiter of Balance if the gauge was in the centre, or Jupiter of Destruction if it was pushed to the Alchemic Matter side.
When the timer hits 0 here, the boss will separate again, and the cycle will start over.
[Back to Overview]
[Boss Event] The One That Swallowed a God
Stop Jupiter from obtaining power through the god’s furnace Cronos, and acquire various rewards!

details.value: You can select the ‘[Boss Event] The One That Swallowed a God’ event in the Event List and click the ‘Participate’ button to start the event.
※ If you are logged in to another account in the same Nexon ID at the same time, you cannot participate in the Clear event.
※ If you participate in the event in an abormal way, your game or content usage may be restricted and you will be excluded from the event participation and rewards.
Regular Clear Mission
You can receive clear rewards through the Event List by defeating Jupiter on any difficulty at least 1 time during the event period.
※ You cannot complete the missions if you are not on a Green PC.
※ You cannot complete the missions by clearing in Practice Mode or Story Mode.
※ After clearing Jupiter (Normal/Hard), you must enter the reward map to satisfy the clear mission requirements.
※ You can participate in the event by clearing the boss in a party of 2 or more as well.
First Clear Mission
You can complete the First Clear Mission by being part of one of the first 3 parties that defeat Hard Jupiter during the event period.
If you complete the First Clear mission, you can acquire the following rewards through the Event List during the separate reward claiming period.
※ After defeating Jupiter (Hard), you must defeat the reward monster ‘Alchemic Matter’s Remnants’ to be recognized as a clear.
※ Your clear will be acknowledged even if you clear in Cross World Boss Content.
※ You cannot participate in the First Clear Mission if you are in Practice Mode or not on a Green PC. Even if you defeat the reward monster, it will not be recognized as a First Clear.
※ The First Clear mission rewards can be claimed after the March 19, 2026 maintenance, and can only be claimed on the Maple ID that completed the mission.
※ If your game usage is restricted due to violations of the operating policy during the event period, you may be excluded from receiving the First Clear mission rewards.
Commemoration Gifts
If Hard Jupiter is defeated during the event period, you can receive commemoration rewards by talking to NPCs of the characters that first cleared it in major towns.
※ Each reward can only be claimed 1 time per Maple ID.
※ When you use the Jupiter Magic Circle Voucher, the item you acquire will be permanent and untradeable.
[Back to Overview]
Destiny 2nd Transcendence
The Adversary’s Destiny Weapon has finally blossomed. To reach a higher level, the sages have offered three trials. Will the Adversary discover their own truths in the ongoing battles?

Decisive Battle Quests

You can complete the Decisive Battle quests by solo clearing boss monsters in Destiny Mode with the corresponding mission requirements as well as collecting the required amount of Adversary’s Resolve.
※ You cannot participate in Decisive Battle quests in cross world boss content.
You can acquire Adversary’s Resolve by defeating certain difficulty of Grandis boss monsters or higher.
※ When you defeat a boss, you will acquire the Adversary’s Resolve divided equally based on the number of party members that entered.
※ You can accumulate up to 15,000 Adversary’s Resolve. You can use the excess Adversary’s Resolve in the next Decisive Battle quest.

After completing all of the Decisive Battle quests, you can transcend your Destiny 1st Transcendence Weapon into the Destiny 2nd Transcendence Weapon through the ‘[Destiny Weapon] Adversary’s Dignity’ quest.

items: The Destiny 2nd Transcendence Weapon will be provided by transferring the stats of your Destiny 1st Transcendence Weapon as follows. Level 250 equipment requirement. All upgrade slots will be consumed with 15% Spell Trace enhancements matching your job. 22 Star Force enhancement The maximum Star Force enhancement limit will be changed to 25 stars. The Destiny 1st Transcendence Weapon’s potential will be transferred. The Destiny 1st Transcendence Weapon’s additional potential will be transferred. The Destiny 1st Transcendence Weapon’s bonus stats will be transferred. The Destiny 1st Transcendence Weapon’s Soul will be transferred. Additional scroll enhancements cannot be made.

After completing the Geardrak area quests, you can complete the daily quest. When you complete it, you will receive 10 Grand Authentic Symbol: Geardrak Vouchers.

details.value: [Back to Overview]
Guild Castle
A hideout just for your guild members, the guild castle! Grow the guild castle with your guild members and create unforgettable memories.
Participation Requirements:

items: A guild that has reached Guild Level 20.
The guild master can create a guild castle by talking to the NPC Benedict in the Guild Headquarters <Hall of Heroes>.

details.value: After the guild castle is created, you can click the Guild Casttle button in the Guild UI to access it.
Guild Castle Growth

items: The castle can grow up to level 20.
As the castle grows, various features will become available.
The castle grows using Energy acquired from guild members participating in guild content.
You can only start acquiring Guild Castle Energy 7 days after you join a guild.

details.value: Crafting Decorations and Placing Decorations

items: Guild members can participate in various content to collect materials that can be used to craft guild decorations.
The Guild Master can craft decorations using the materials through the Crafting UI.
The Guild Master can place crafted decorations in the Guild Castle by using Placement Mode.
Decorations can only be placed in customizable areas, such as the lobby, guild room, greenhouse, and party room.
You can also acquire special decorations to decorate the greenhouse through guild missions performed jointly by the guild.

details.value: ※ Among the guild missions, the boss clear mission can only be done 7 days after joining a guild.
Lobby

items: The guild castle Lobby serves as the centre of the guild castle and connects the various maps inside.
If your guild castle is below level 10, you will have a small Lobby that only contains the Guild Room and Supply Station.
If your guild castle is level 10 or higher, you will have a bigger Lobby that also contains the Party Room, Greenhouse, and Enhancement Altar.

You can use the item display cases to display items, and the guild honor statues to display specific guild members. As your guild castle level increases, you will be able to use more item display cases and statues.

details.value: Supply Station

items: You can use the guild castle’s Crafting skill.
You can talk to the NPC Susan to use the guild shop.
You can access the Crafting UI by talking to the NPC Oliver or by clicking the Crafting button in the guild castle menu.
You can access the guild’s shared storage, the Guild Safes.
The guild master can designate individual guild members’ weekly entry/exit limits and change the safes’ names.
The Guild Safes can only hold consumable, etc, and install items.
As the guild castle’s level increases, more Guild Safes will become available.

details.value: ※ You can access the Supply Station 24 hours after joining a guild.
※ Guild members without access rights cannot use this feature.
※ If a guild is disbanded, the items stored in the guild safes will disappear.
Guild Room

items: This is a custom room that you can decorate with various furniture.

details.value: Party Room

items: This is a custom room that you can decorate with various furniture.
You can talk to the NPC Party Master Pierre to play minigames with your guild members.

details.value: Greenhouse

items: This is a custom room that you can decorate with various furniture.
You can place wood decorations and boss statues acquired from guild missions.

details.value: Enhancement Altar

items: When you use the Enhancement Altar to Star Force enhance an item, your enhancement status will be shared with your guild members.

details.value: ※ The Enhancement Altar is unlocked when your guild castle reaches level 15.
※ The Enhancement Altar can be used up to 30 times per day per guild.
※ The Enhancement Altar can only be used if there are 2 or more guild members in the room.
※ You can only register equipment items that can reach 20 stars or higher, or Superior equipment items.
※ You can only access this feature 24 hours after joining a guild.
※ Guild members without access rights cannot use this feature.
※ The usage time will begin as soon as you open the Enhancement Altar UI. You can use it for up to 30 minutes.
※ If you are inactive for 3 minutes or more, you will be automatically removed.
Guild Castle Minigame – Quiz Us

items: Participation Method: Select <Quiz Us> in the minigame UI to enter the Quiz Room. ※ Up to 200 players can enter the Quiz Room.
Select <Quiz Us> in the minigame UI to enter the Quiz Room.
※ Up to 200 players can enter the Quiz Room.

Gameplay: You can talk to the NPC Party Master Pierre in the Quiz Room to create a quiz. Anyone can do so. ※ You cannot create a quiz if a quiz is already in progress. You can create up to 10 quizzes. The quiz creater must enter each question and answer before starting the quiz. ※ Profanity and certain symbols are not allowed in quiz questions and answers. When a round begins, the question will appear at the top of the screen. Participates can enter the correct answer by using the </answer> command. The round lasts 30 seconds. The first person to enter the answer matching the one provided by the quiz creater will receive 1 point. The creater can use the </hint> command to provide hints. The creator’s hints will be displayed at the bottom right corner for all participants. The participant with the highest score at the end of all the rounds will be crowned the final winner.
You can talk to the NPC Party Master Pierre in the Quiz Room to create a quiz. Anyone can do so.
※ You cannot create a quiz if a quiz is already in progress.
You can create up to 10 quizzes. The quiz creater must enter each question and answer before starting the quiz.
※ Profanity and certain symbols are not allowed in quiz questions and answers.
When a round begins, the question will appear at the top of the screen. Participates can enter the correct answer by using the </answer> command.
The round lasts 30 seconds. The first person to enter the answer matching the one provided by the quiz creater will receive 1 point.
The creater can use the </hint> command to provide hints. The creator’s hints will be displayed at the bottom right corner for all participants.
The participant with the highest score at the end of all the rounds will be crowned the final winner.

details.value: Guild Castle Minigame – Sky Sovereigns

items: Participation Method: Select <Sky Sovereigns> in the minigame UI to enter the Quiz Room. ※ You cannot enter if a game is already in progress or if the waiting room already has 20 players The first character that enters the waiting UI will be selected as that game’s leader. The leader can change the map. Participants can freely change teams by clicking on the empty slots. When all participants are ready, the leader can start the game.
Select <Sky Sovereigns> in the minigame UI to enter the Quiz Room.
※ You cannot enter if a game is already in progress or if the waiting room already has 20 players
The first character that enters the waiting UI will be selected as that game’s leader. The leader can change the map.
Participants can freely change teams by clicking on the empty slots.
When all participants are ready, the leader can start the game.

Gameplay Up to 20 players can enter a single game. ※ All buff effects will be removed upon entering. ※ If you are inactive for 10 seconds after entering, you will receive a warning. If you receive 2 warnings, you will be kicked from the game. Sky Sovereigns has a 3 minute time limit. Your character’s nametag, HP bar, and skill colours will be based on your team colour. When jumping, you can press the jump key again to do a Double Jump. You can use the ‘Hammer Swing!’ skill. You can hold the key down to charge the hammer. You can release it to swing the hammer, dealing damage, stunning, and knocking back multiple enemies. When the energy is filled, the stun duration and knockback distance are increased. Items will appear throughout the map, and you can acquire them to use item skills. Bomb Throw!: When used, throw a bomb in front of you to damage and stun multiple enemies. High Barrier: When used, you become immune to damage and stuns for a certain period of time. However, you cannot ignore death from the electricity currents. You can change hotkeys by clicking on the skill icons. During the game, you can press the ‘Tab’ key to check the current score for all characters. You can acquire individual points by killing enemies or destroying Golden Hammers. Enemy Kills: 1 point Golden Hammer Destroyed: 5 points At the end of the game, the RED and BLUE teams’ invidual scores are added up and the team with the higher score wins.
Up to 20 players can enter a single game.

Guild Skills

items: The regular guild skills will be changed as follows. Guild Lv. 1 Guild’s Know-How I: 30 attack/magic attack, 10% damage reduction. Adventure Together: 5% additional experience from killing normal monsters that are level 101~200. Eyes on Small Change: Acquire an additional 8 mesos when you loot mesos while hunting. Guild Lv. 5 Guild’s Know-How II: 6 attack/magic attack. Special Power: 30 Arcane Force, 15 Star Force. I’ll Go To You: Teleport to a designated guild member’s location, 1 hour cooldown. Guild Lv. 10 Guild’s Know-How III: 12% normal monster damage. Merchant: 4% discount when buying items from a shop. However, this discount does not apply if the resell price is 70% or more of the selling price. Guild Lv. 15 Guild’s Know-How IV: 4 attack/magic attack. Master of Scroll Enhancements: When scroll enhancing using Spell Traces or special scrolls, increases success rate by +4%. Guild Lv. 20 Guild’s Know-How V: 40 all stats, 2000 HP. Not Afraid of Death: Death penalty’s duration is decreased by 30%. Guild Lv. 25 Guild’s Know-How VI: 5 attack/magic attack. Craftsman of Scroll Enhancements: When a scroll enhancement fails, there is a 4% chance to not consume a scroll slot. Guild Lv. 30 Sharenian’s Demon Riding: When you ride Sharenian’s Demon, 190 speed and 120 jump.

items: Fixed an issue with the Maple Guide UI’s Special Content tab where clicking certain button images moved awkwardly when clicked.
Fixed an issue with the Union Artifact’s artifact mission UI where the ‘Complete the Nightmare Paradise quests’ artifact special mission was displayed in an awkward order.
Fixed an issue with the Soul Collection where Suu/Annihilation Weapon Suu’s normal illustrations’ display coordinates were awkward.
Fixed an issue where if a character died with a Soul Weapon equipped, the Soul Weapon UI could not be opened until the Soul Gauge reset.
Fixed an issue with the Buff Manager where if you had a 2x Experience Coupon item, the Union’s Experience item would be incorrectly displayed as owned.
Fixed an issue where if you had the Union’s Wealth Stage 2 effect applied then used the Buff Sequence’s ‘use unapplied buffs’ feature, the Union’s Wealth Stage 3 would be applied.

Fixed an issue with the item trade UI where the decorative tab’s item list was displayed awkwardly.

Fixed an issue with Monster Collection where double-clicking Arma or Arma’s Subordinate monsters would not display the world map.
Fixed an issue with item trades where in certain situations, the amount of mesos acquired through the trade was not displayed properly.

Fixed an issue in the guid skill UI where Noblesse guild skills did not show that they could be registered in a Buff Sequence.
