details.value: [Back to Overview]
Receive abundant experience benefits and items with the returning Haste event!
Event Participation Requirements:

items: A level 200 or higher character (or a Zero character that has completed story quest Chapter 2).

details.value: Event Period:

items: February 12, 2026 after the maintenance ~ March 18, 2026 at 11:59 PM.

details.value: You can select the ‘Accelerated Hunting! Haste BEYOND’ event in the Event List and click the ‘Participate’ button to start the event.
After starting the event, you can check the Haste BEYOND field benefits and special mission rewards through the Event List.
※ The field benefits and special mission rewards can be checked and claimed until March 18, 2026 at 11:59 PM.
Event Period Special Benefits
If you login during the event period, you will receive the ‘Haste BEYOND’ skill.
※ The ‘Haste BEYOND’ skill will be applied until March 18, 2026 at 11:59 PM.
Every time you defeat a certain number of monsters within your level range, you will receive Haste BEYOND field benefit effects.
※ Monsters within your level range refers to monsters -20~+20 of your character level.
※ The event will only progress if you defeat monsters within your level range while you are in a party of 1 or not in a party.
※ Haste Treasure Boxes will appear when you defeat 10,000 monsters within your level range on a level 200 or higher character in instanced maps.
※ Haste Treasure Boxes will give a gem that gives a lot of experience based on the Treasure Box’s rank.
※ Haste Treasure Boxes are not included in Treasure Hunter achievement counts.
※ You can discover up to 3 Haste Treasure Boxes per day per Maple ID.
※ The Haste BEYOND field benefits will be applied until March 18, 2026 at 11:59 PM.
Haste Fever Time
After participating in the event, you can use the Haste Fever Time through the special UI that appears when you are in a map with monsters within your level range or through the Haste UI.
When you use the Haste Fever Time, Haste Inferno monsters that give lots of experience will be summoned for 5 minutes.
When you defeat the Haste Inferno monsters, there is a low chance of acquiring the Cassandra’s Crystal Ball item that helps you defeat monsters within your level range.
※ The Haste Fever Time can be used 1 time per day per Maple ID.
※ The Haste Fever Time can only be used on level 200 or higher characters that have completed the ‘[5th Job] Another Power, Arcane Force’ quest.
※ Haste Inferno monsters are not affected by any additional experience effects.
※ When you acquire the Cassandra’s Crystal Ball item, the Cassandra’s Crystal Ball skill will be activated and attack nearby enemies.
※ The Cassandra’s Crystal Ball skill can stack up to 5 times, and the attack range will be increased/attack cycle will be decreased per stack.
※ When you acquire the Cassandra’s Crystal Ball item, the skill duration will be increased by 10 seconds, and can be increased by up to 90 seconds.
Special Missions
You can complete missions by defeating a certain number of monsters within your level range.
※ Monsters within your level range refers to monsters -20~+20 of your character level.
※ The event will only progress if you defeat monsters within your level range while you are in a party of 1 or not in a party.
※ The special mission progress record is shared between all characters within the Maple ID.
※ When you complete each mission, the cumulative number of monsters defeated will not be reset.
You can receive rewards for each completed mission through the Haste UI.
※ All reward items can only be claimed 1 time per Maple ID.
※ The ‘Flame Wolf Action Damage Skin (Unit)’ can be extracted with a Damage Skin Extractor.
※ When you use the ‘I’m Quite Hasted Title Voucher’, you will receive the ‘I’m Quite Hasted’ title that is permament. The title’s stats will expire 30 days after you use the voucher.
※ The ‘I’m Quite Hasted’ title’s stats are as follows.

items: 50 all stats
20 attack/magic attack
30% boss damage
30% defense ignore
2,500 HP/MP

sections.title: MapleStory Winter Event [Night of Falling Illusions]

details.value: [Attendance Check Event] Fantasia Finale
To conclude Tallahart’s illusion magic festival, listen to Riche’s request and receive gifts!
Event Participation Requirements:

items: A level 101 or higher character (or a Zero character that has completed story quest Chapter 2) that has participated in the ‘Night of Falling Illusions’ event.

details.value: Event Period:

items: March 5, 2026 at 10:00 AM ~ March 18, 2026 at 11:59 PM.

details.value: You can select the ‘Night of Falling Illusions’ event in the Event List and click the ‘Participate’ button to start this event with the other Night of Falling Illusions event. Or you can select the ‘[Attendance Check] Fantasia Finale’ event.
※ The <Fantasia Finale> participation records are shared between all characters within the Maple ID.
Daily Attendance Check

Every day, you can defeat 1,000 monsters within your level range and press the ‘Attendance Check’ button to complete the daily attendance check and receive rewards. You can complete it up to 10 times during the event period.

You can click the <Use> button in the top-right corner of the UI to use the Finale Pass to complete 1 attendance check you did not previously complete. It can be used up to the number of missed days as of the previous day, and costs 3,000 Maple Points per use.
※ The number of monsters defeated record is shared between all characters within the Maple ID.
※ The event will only progress if you defeat monsters within your level range while you are in a party of 1 or not in a party.
Attendance Check Rewards
When you complete an attendancec check, you can press the <Receive> button to receive the corresponding reward. You can press the <Receive All Rewards> button to claim all rewards at once.
※ The attendance check rewards can be claimed 1 time per Maple ID.
※ All reward items can only be put into storage.
※ All reward items can be claimed until March 18, 2026 at 11:59 PM, and will expire on March 19, 2026 at 2:00 AM.
※ The ‘Illusion’s Ellanos Voucher’ can only be used after creating a Maple Home. The furniture acquired from the voucher is an item that can be applied in Maple Home.
[Back to Overview]

Event Participation Requirements:

items: Level 101 or higher characters created (or a Zero character that has completed story quest Chapter 2) after November 20, 2025 at 12:00 AM.

details.value: Event Period:

items: February 12, 2026 after the maintenance ~ April 15, 2025 at 11:59 PM.

details.value: You can select the ‘Frontier Pass’ event in the Event List and click the ‘Participate’ button to start the event.
※ The Frontier Pass event can only be participated in 1 time per person.
※ You cannot change the character or Maple ID that participates in the Frontier Pass event.
※ Even if you delete the character that participates in the Frontier Pass event, you cannot participate on another character.
Frontier Pass Level and Weekly Missions
The Fronter Pass can go up to level 30. You can reach the next level by acquiring 480 Pass Points.
You can acquire Frontier Pass Points by completing weekly missions.
You can acquire up to 2,000 Frontier Pass Points per week.
※ The Frontier Pass missions can only be progressed on the character that is participating in the event.
※ The weekly Frontier Pass point acquisition and weekly mission progress are reset every Thursday at midnight.
※ Weekly bosses defeated/Symbol daily quests/Monster Park and Monster Park Extreme cleared before participating in the Frontier Pass event will not be reflected in the Frontier Pass weekly missions.
You can buy Frontier Pass Points based on the amount of Points you did not acquire from the previous week. It costs 1000 Maple Points per 100 Pass Points.
Pass Level Achievement Rewards
If you buy the Premium Pass, you can claim the Special Support Items.
※ All reward items can only be acquired on the character participating in the event, and can only be claimed 1 time.
※ When you use the Selective Symbol Voucher, you can choose between 5 Selective Arcane Symbol Vouchers or 2 Selective Authentic Symbol Vouchers.
※ The VIP Sauna Entry Tickets can only be put into storage. All other items are untradeable.
※ All reward items can only be claimed until April 15, 2026 at 11:59 PM.
※ The Sol Erda Fragments are permanent items. All other items will expire on April 16, 2026 at 2:00 AM.
Premium Pass
The Frontier Premium Pass product will be sold.

items: Sale Duration: February 12, 2026 after the maintenance ~ April 15, 2026 at 11:00 PM.

Momentum Pass Levels and Weekly Missions

items: Dim Illusion Memories, Bright Illusion Memories, Complete Illusion Memories, Challengers Coins, Advanced Challengers Coins, and Premium Coins acquired in Challengers World will not be moved with the World Leap.

The Selective Challengers Level 4 Special Skill Ring Voucher and the Special Skill Ring acquired from using it are Challengers World-only items and you cannot World Leap while holding them.
The Unstable Time Fragments are exclusive to Challengers World and you cannot leap while you have them in your inventory.
If a character that has cleared an Epic Dungeon uses World Leap, you cannot claim the cleared Epic Dungeon’s bonus rewards.
If there are no empty character slots in the world you are moving to, slots will be added before the Leap.
However, if you already have 50 characters in the destination world, you cannot Leap to that world.

details.value: [Back to Overview]
Radiant Malefic Star (Normal)’s HP has been decreased by about 30%.
Developer Notes:
On the February 5th Maple Now, many players provided feedback about Radiant Malefic Star (Normal)’s boss difficulty setting, so we conducted an internal review.

After reviewing, we confirmed that even considering the differences in mechanics between Kaling and Radiant Malefic Star, Radiant Malefic Star (Normal)’s HP was excessively higher than Kaling’s. Therefore, we will adjust Radiant Malefic Star (Normal)’s HP to fit better in the official server update.
We apologize again for not being able to sufficiently verify numerical consistency and we will do our best to provide you with better service in the future.
The hitboxes for the monsters that appear when using VIP Boosters and Express Boosters have been increased by about 52%.

details.value: The Santa Beard item tooltip will now include that it may look awkward when worn with certain items.
The Entrancing Fantasy’s Snippets dropped by Radiant Malefic Star (Hard Mode) have been changed to drop 6 bundles of 3 instead of 18 bundles of 1.
If you try to use a portal while a VIP Booster/HEXA Booster/Express Booster effect is being applied, a warning window will now be displayed.
When extracting a ‘Proof of a Brilliant Hero’ item through the Mage Association NPC Owl, the extraction will now only be done after inputting a confirmation message.
[Back to Overview]
The item trade UI and storage UI have been improved so you can drag-and-drop or right-click to register, store, or take out items.
The Buff Manager’s scrolling has been improved to be smoother.
Erion’s Fragment Voucher acquisition information has been added to the Maple Guide’s Boss Contents section.
Certain boss tiers have been changed in the Maple Guide’s Boss Contents section.
In the Guild UI’s list of guild members, online guild members will now be displayed at the top, then for offline guild members, they will be listed in order of join date.
[Back to Overview]
The Maple Home preset feature has been added.

items: This feature lets you save and load your currently decorated Maple Home theme, building design, furniture, and platforms.
A total of 3 preset slots are provided.
You can set a name for each preset.

details.value: ※ Presets can be saved per world.
※ Names that violate game policies may result in sanctions.
※ When a preset is loaded, for items placed in a display case, they will be loaded based on the items that are currently displayed and not the items that were there at the time the preset was saved.
※ If a Maple Home theme that was saved in a preset has been extracted and you no longer have it, you cannot load that preset.
[Back to Overview]
For equipment that do not allow custom illustrations to be requested if they are equipped, a message has been added to the item tooltips so you can know ahead of time.
Custom illustration requests will now be allowed when the following items are equipped.

items: You can see the list of items here.

details.value: The Moving Easel NPC’s chat bubble dialogue has been changed.
[Back to Overview]

items: Fixed an issue in Practice Mode where even if you selected the Mashoor’s Gift Weather Effect item, you could use it through the Buff Sequence.
Fixed an issue where in certain situations, even if you used certain buff items, the durations would not be refreshed.
Fixed an issue where the Snowflame Riding (Permanent) Voucher tooltip would display ‘unowned’ even if you had it.
Fixed an issue where the Name Engraver and Name Remover items could not be used on Astra subweapons.
Fixed an issue where emoticons would appear awkwardly if you had a chat bubble ring with animated effects equipped.
Fixed an issue where the preview when naming the Malefic Staroid looked awkward.
Fixed an issue where in specific situations, the Intense Power Crystals’ sale details’ number of players defeated with would be displayed awkwardly.

details.value: [Back to Overview]

items: Fixed an issue where certain items’ tooltip text looked blurry.
Fixed an issue where the amount of space required to install the game was displayed incorrectly.

details.value: [Back to Overview]
Events
Voice Chat is now available.
[Back to Overview]
Supplementary work is being done to alleviate lag issues in certain content.
[Back to Overview]
Error Fixes

items: Fixed an issue where if you played remotely using Nexon Link on a mobile network with an unstable environment, sometimes the controls would not work.

kmsBreakdown.fullText: The third and final patch of the MapleStory Crown update, featuring the new level 295 area Geardrak and the new end-game boss Jupiter! It also includes the Destiny Weapon 2nd transcendence, the Guild Castle system, and the MapleStory x Frieren: Beyond Journey’s End collaboration. Overview New Boss: Jupiter Information [Boss Event] The One That Swallowed a God Destiny 2nd Transcendence New Area <Geardrak> Guild Castle Guild Reorganization Cash Skill Changes Common Hero Paladin Dark Knight Arch Mage (Fire/Poison) Arch Mage (Ice/Lightning) Bishop Bowmaster Marksman Pathfinder Night Lord Shadower Dual Blade Viper Captain Cannon Shooter Mikhail Soul Master Flame Wizard Wind Breaker Night Walker Striker Evan Mercedes Luminous Phantom Blaster Battle Mage Xenon Mechanic Demon Slayer Demon Avenger Kaiser Kain Cadena Zero Adele Illium Khali Ark Lara Hoyoung HEXA Core Improvements Field Items NPCs Monsters Maps UI Events Maple Home Custom Illustrations Mac Version Beta Service Content Error Fixes Quests Achievements Items Monsters Maps UI Events Nexon Link Mac Version Beta Service Events Accelerated Hunting! Haste BEYOND MapleStory Winter Event [Night of Falling Illusions] [Attendance Check Event] Fantasia Finale Frontier Pass Momentum Pass Challengers World 2nd Early Leap Tell Me Your New Year’s Wish [Boss Clear Event] Call of Deception Solo Clear Rewards Sunday Maple New Boss: Jupiter Participation Requirements Level 295 or higher characters that have completed the ‘[Geardrak] Epilogue’ quest. Participation Method You can enter through Geardrak: Blast Furnace’s Gate. ※ Hard Mode will be available after February 13 at 7:00 PM. ※ The Hard Mode challenge time is subject to change. Any changes will be announced in a separate notice. You can acquire the following items by defeating Jupiter. Original Sin of Pride (Hard Mode only) Level 250 Face Accessory Part of the Brilliant Boss set Jupiteroid Twisted Yearning Fragment Yearning Eternal Armor Box (Hard Mode only) Jupiter’s Soul Shard ※ When you use the Yearning Eternal Armor Box, you can choose one of the Eternal Gloves, Shoes, or Cape. Jupiter’s Souls have been added. Jupiter’s Soul Magnificent Jupiter’s Soul Jupiter’s Soul Skills have been added. Negative Reaction[Master Level: 2]With the Soul Weapon’s power, Jupiter deals a powerful strike on enemies in front of you.[Activation Command: ↓↓↑↑ + Attack key] [Level 2]Consumes 200 Soul. Deals 1200% damage 4 times on up to 15 enemies.Cooldown: 30 seconds. Order and ChaosCall Jupiter’s soul. When you use the Soul Skill, either Jupiter of Perfection or Jupiter of Destruction will be summoned. Each Jupiter has a different attack pattern.[Activation Command: ↓↓↑↑ + Attack key] [Level 2]Consumes 250 Soul. For 120 seconds, summon Jupiter that periodically deals 2200% damage on up to 12 enemies.The Soul Weapon summon’s damage is calculated differently than the character’s.Cooldown: 150 seconds. Jupiter’s Soul Collector illustrations have been added. Normal: For the glory of the gods.Hidden: Let’s see who wins! Jupiter related achievements have been added. [Back to Overview] Information The Crossroads of Control and Disobedience The Arena of Discipline and Command The Arena of Irregularity and Chaos The Arena of Rules and Rupture The Arena of Discipline and Command (Union) The Arena of Irregularity and Chaos (Union) A Desolate and Lonely Furnace Jupiter can only be entered in a party of 1~3 players. Normal Mode: Level: 295 HP: 10.266Q Authentic Force: 810 Power Crystal Price: 1.7b Hard Mode: Level: 295 HP: 49.4Q Authentic Force: 810 Power Crystal Price: 5.1b When you enter Jupiter, you will be moved to the Shackles of Order and Chaos waiting map. Here, you and your party members can move to the left or right side of the map to decide who will fight Jupiter and who will fight the Alchemic Matter. Up to 2 party members can be on each side. When the fight starts, you will be placed in the side that you chose previously. You can use the Ether Warp skill with the NPC Chat button to move to the other side (only if there are not already 2 players there). At the top of the screen below the timer, you’ll see the Rupture Value. This is your personal Death Count in this boss, starting at 0. When you die in this boss, it will increase by 150 (separated) or 200 (combined). There are also other patterns that increase or decrease it. If it reaches 1000, you will fail and be kicked out of the boss. The timer at the bottom is for the Merge pattern. The main mechanic of the boss is based on the Magic Sync gauge. When you are hit by either boss, the gauge will move towards that boss. The timer on the bottom is the Objective pattern where Jupiter will check if the gauge is balanced (in the blue area in the middle) every so often, and if not, your Rupture value will go up. Both bosses share the same HP bar, but they have separate patterns and ‘maps’. You can see your other party members and the other boss on the other side but you can’t interact with them unless you swap sides. When the Merge timer hits 0, Jupiter and the Alchemic Matter will merge into a single boss. You will face Jupiter of Perfection if the Magic Sync gauge was pushed to the Jupiter side, Jupiter of Balance if the gauge was in the centre, or Jupiter of Destruction if it was pushed to the Alchemic Matter side. When the timer hits 0 here, the boss will separate again, and the cycle will start over. [Back to Overview] [Boss Event] The One That Swallowed a God Stop Jupiter from obtaining power through the god’s furnace Cronos, and acquire various rewards! Event Participation Requirements: Level 295 or higher characters that have completed the [Geardrak] area quests. Event Period: February 12, 2026 after the maintenance ~ March 18, 2026 at 11:59 PM. ※ Jupiter Hard Mode will be available after February 13 at 7:00 PM. Event Reward Claim Period: Regular Clear Mission: February 12, 2026 after the maintenance ~ March 19, 2026 at 2:00 AM. First Clear Mission: March 19, 2026 at 7:00 PM ~ March 25, 2026 at 11:59 PM. You can select the ‘[Boss Event] The One That Swallowed a God’ event in the Event List and click the ‘Participate’ button to start the event. ※ If you are logged in to another account in the same Nexon ID at the same time, you cannot participate in the Clear event. ※ If you participate in the event in an abormal way, your game or content usage may be restricted and you will be excluded from the event participation and rewards. Regular Clear Mission You can receive clear rewards through the Event List by defeating Jupiter on any difficulty at least 1 time during the event period. Acquisition ConditionRewardQuantityTradeabilityDurationClear Jupiter MissionMiniature Cronos Chair1Can only be put into storagePermanent ※ You cannot complete the missions if you are not on a Green PC. ※ You cannot complete the missions by clearing in Practice Mode or Story Mode. ※ After clearing Jupiter (Normal/Hard), you must enter the reward map to satisfy the clear mission requirements. ※ You can participate in the event by clearing the boss in a party of 2 or more as well. First Clear Mission You can complete the First Clear Mission by being part of one of the first 3 parties that defeat Hard Jupiter during the event period. If you complete the First Clear mission, you can acquire the following rewards through the Event List during the separate reward claiming period. RewardQuantityTradeabilityDurationFull Stop of Gluttony1UntradeablePermanentEntangled Balance Appearance Effect Voucher1Can only be put into storage7 DaysGod’s Furnace Custom Background Voucher1Balance Nametag/Chat Bubble Ring Voucher1 ※ After defeating Jupiter (Hard), you must defeat the reward monster ‘Alchemic Matter’s Remnants’ to be recognized as a clear. ※ Your clear will be acknowledged even if you clear in Cross World Boss Content. ※ You cannot participate in the First Clear Mission if you are in Practice Mode or not on a Green PC. Even if you defeat the reward monster, it will not be recognized as a First Clear. ※ The First Clear mission rewards can be claimed after the March 19, 2026 maintenance, and can only be claimed on the Maple ID that completed the mission. ※ If your game usage is restricted due to violations of the operating policy during the event period, you may be excluded from receiving the First Clear mission rewards. Commemoration Gifts If Hard Jupiter is defeated during the event period, you can receive commemoration rewards by talking to NPCs of the characters that first cleared it in major towns. RewardQuantityTradeabilityDuration4x Experience Coupon (30 Minutes)2Can only be put into storageExpires on March 19, 2026 at 2:00 AMVIP Sauna Entry Ticket4Advanced EXP Voucher1000Jupiter Magic Circle Voucher1 ※ Each reward can only be claimed 1 time per Maple ID. ※ When you use the Jupiter Magic Circle Voucher, the item you acquire will be permanent and untradeable. [Back to Overview] Destiny 2nd Transcendence The Adversary’s Destiny Weapon has finally blossomed. To reach a higher level, the sages have offered three trials. Will the Adversary discover their own truths in the ongoing battles? Participation Requirements: Characters who have completed the ‘[Destiny Weapon] Adversary’s Weapon’ quest and have a Destiny Weapon. Destiny Weapon related quests have been added. [Destiny Weapon] New Realm [Destiny Weapon] Adversary’s Faith [Destiny Weapon] Decisive Battle, First Adversary [Destiny Weapon] Decisive Battle, Apostle Limbo [Destiny Weapon] Decisive Battle, Apostle Baldrix [Destiny Weapon] Adversary’s Dignity ※ You can open the Destiny 2nd Transcendence mission UI through the ‘[Destiny Weapon] Adversary’s Faith’ quest. Decisive Battle Quests You can complete the Decisive Battle quests by solo clearing boss monsters in Destiny Mode with the corresponding mission requirements as well as collecting the required amount of Adversary’s Resolve. ※ You cannot participate in Decisive Battle quests in cross world boss content. Decisive Battle QuestDestiny ModeAdversary’s Resolve RequiredDecisive Battle, First Adversary– Solo Hard Mode– 20% final damage reduction – When hit, the Power of Order reduction is increased by 50%10,000Decisive Battle, Apostle Limbo– Solo Hard Mode– Erosion value starts at 80012,500Decisive Battle, Baldrix– Solo Hard Mode– When hit, damage taken is increased by 30%15,000 You can acquire Adversary’s Resolve by defeating certain difficulty of Grandis boss monsters or higher. BossAdversary’s ResolveHard Seren6Normal Kalos10Normal First Adversary15Normal Radiant Malefic Star20Normal Kaling20Chaos Kalos70Extreme Seren80Normal Limbo120Hard First Adversary120Normal Baldrix150Normal Jupiter160Hard Kaling160Hard Limbo360Hard Radiant Malefic Star380Extreme Kalos400Hard Baldrix450Extreme First Adversary500Hard Jupiter500Extreme Kaling1,200 ※ When you defeat a boss, you will acquire the Adversary’s Resolve divided equally based on the number of party members that entered. ※ You can accumulate up to 15,000 Adversary’s Resolve. You can use the excess Adversary’s Resolve in the next Decisive Battle quest. After completing all of the Decisive Battle quests, you can transcend your Destiny 1st Transcendence Weapon into the Destiny 2nd Transcendence Weapon through the ‘[Destiny Weapon] Adversary’s Dignity’ quest. The Destiny 2nd Transcendence Weapon will be provided by transferring the stats of your Destiny 1st Transcendence Weapon as follows. Level 250 equipment requirement. All upgrade slots will be consumed with 15% Spell Trace enhancements matching your job. 22 Star Force enhancement The maximum Star Force enhancement limit will be changed to 25 stars. The Destiny 1st Transcendence Weapon’s potential will be transferred. The Destiny 1st Transcendence Weapon’s additional potential will be transferred. The Destiny 1st Transcendence Weapon’s bonus stats will be transferred. The Destiny 1st Transcendence Weapon’s Soul will be transferred. Additional scroll enhancements cannot be made. When you equip the Destiny 2nd Transcendence Weapon, you can use the following 3 skills. Transcendence: Decisive Battle’s Will[Master Level: 1]With an decisive will to never give up, transcend your limits once more.On/Off skill. [Level 1]Permanently increases final damage by 10%, boss damage by 15%, and defense ignore by 15%.When the skill is used, manifest your decisive will. Transcendence: Unbreakable Resolve[Master Level: 1]With an unbreakable resolve, gain the power to withstand any attack.This skill is not affected by cooldown reset or buff duration increasing effects. If it hits an enemy using Damage Reflect, you will not take damage.You can use Transcendence: Unbreakable Resolve while using other skills. [Level 1]Manifest your unbreakable resolve to become invincible for 10 seconds.You can use the skill again to instantly end it.Cooldown: 120 seconds. Transcendence: First Legacy[Master Level: 1]Internalize the will inherited from the past and awaken the ultimate fighting spirit.This skill is not affected by cooldown reset or buff duration increasing effects. Can be registered in a Skill Sequence. [Level 1]Awaken the ultimate fighting spirit, increasing attack/magic attack by 10% for 60 seconds.Cooldown: 120 seconds. Zeros will acquire the Destiny Essence which can be used to enhance the Destiny 1st Transcendence Lapis/Lazuli into the Destiny 2nd Transcendence Lapis/Lazuli. Each character can only hold 1 Destiny Weapon, and it cannot be traded. If you lose your Destiny 1st Transcendence Weapon during the quests, you must complete the Genesis Weapon quests again from the beginning. If you lose your Destiny 2nd Transcendence Weapon after completing the quests, you must complete the Genesis Weapon quests again from the beginning then complete the Destiny Weapon quests again. Destiny Weapon Shop After completing the ‘[Destiny Weapon] Adversary’s Dignity’ quest, you can buy the Destiny 2nd Transcendence Weapon Box from the Ark of Resolve NPC in Shangri-La: Sinners’ Paradise. You can buy the Destiny 2nd Transcendence Weapon Box for 10b mesos. You can only use it if you do not have a Destiny 2nd Transcendence Weapon and you do have its trace. For Zero, if their weapon is destroyed while Star Force enhancing it and turned into a trace, they will equip the Lapis/Lazuli Type 7 weapon. You can use the Destiny Essence to enhance the weapon into the Destiny 2nd Transcendence Weapon. ※ Zero will receive the Destiny Essence from the Destiny 2nd Transcendence Weapon Box. ※ If Zero uses the Destiny Essence, all of the Lapis/Lazuli Type 7’s enhancements will be lost and the weapon will be transformed into the Destiny 2nd Transcendence Weapon with Unique potential and Epic additional potential. Destiny Weapon related achievements have been added. [Destiny Weapon] Decisive Battle, First Adversary [Destiny Weapon] Decisive Battle, Apostle Limbo [Destiny Weapon] Decisive Battle, Apostle Baldrix [Back to Overview] New Area <Geardrak> Primacy asks the Adversary for help to find Gob, who they have lost contact with and can’t be reached. The Adversary accompanies them to explore the mysterious desert discovered by Primacy. Will they be able to find Gob? Why did he disappear? Participation Requirements: Level 295 or higher characters who have completed the 5th job advancement and the ‘[Tallahart] For the Coming Battle’ quest. Participation Method: Accept the ‘[Geardrak] Missing’ quest through the Lightbulb icon on the left side of the screen. The following quests have been added to the Geardrak area. [Geardrak] Missing [Geardrak] In the Middle of the Desert [Geardrak] Robot Sleeping in the Wilderness [Geardrak] To the 2nd Underground Floor [Geardrak] To the 3rd Underground Floor [Geardrak] To the 4th Underground Floor [Geardrak] Breakthrough [Geardrak] Location Tracking [Geardrak] Pursuit [Geardrak] Search [Geardrak] Where Cronos Sleeps [Geardrak] Rescue [Geardrak] Epilogue [Daily Quest] Geardrak Cronos’ Remnants Collection ※ When you complete the daily quest, you will receive experience (once per day per world) and 10 Grand Authentic Symbol: Geardrak Vouchers. If you have the Astra subweapon quest in progress, you will receive 65 Erion’s Fragments. The following NPCs have been added to the Geardrak area. Gob Tiny Security Robot Z Kelly Ailene Gob’s Storage Potion Making Robot Nineheart The Furnace That Swallowed the Gods Secrets Hidden Under the Sand The Gaze of the Abandoned Robots The Ghosts of the Sleepless Gods The following maps have been added to the Geardrak area. Where Cronos Sleeps Abandoned Station Sunset Wilderness Furnace Entrance Underground 1F Underground 2F Section 1 Underground 2F Section 2 Underground 3F Section 1 Underground 3F Section 2 Underground 4F Robot Storage 1 Robot Storage 2 Robot Storage 3 Robot Storage 4 Robot Storage 5 Robot Storage 6 Robot Storage 7 Robot Storage 8 Gob’s Workshop 1 Gob’s Workshop 2 Gob’s Workshop 3 Gob’s Workshop 4 Gob’s Workshop 5 Gob’s Workshop 6 Gob’s Workshop 7 Gob’s Workshop 8 The following monsters have been added to the Geardrak area. Patrol Type Security Robot Attack Type Security Robot Normal Type Weapon Robot Enhanced Type Weapon Robot Cronos’ Remnant Cronos’ Resentment Surveillance Robot The Geardrak area’s Elite Boss has been added. Giant Weapon Robot The Grand Authentic Symbol has been added to the Geardrak area. When you complete all of the Geardrak area quests, you will acquire 1 Grand Authentic Symbol: Geardrak. The Grand Authentic Symbol: Geardrak gives 10% additional experience, 5% meso acquisition rate, and 5% item drop rate at level 1. When it reaches level 11, it will give 50% additional experience, 15% meso acquisition rate, and 15% item drop rate, as well as +20% damage when attacking Jupiter. After completing the Geardrak area quests, you can complete the daily quest. When you complete it, you will receive 10 Grand Authentic Symbol: Geardrak Vouchers. Geardrak has been added to the Symbol Quick Pass. After completing the Geardrak area quests, you can use 1,000 Maple Points to instantly complete the Geardrak daily quest. The Geardrak area achievements have been added. The Results of an Incomplete Fusion [Area Exploration] Geardrak Exploration The Geardrak area artifact missions have been added. Complete the Geardrak area quests. Grow the Grand Authentic Symbol: Geardrak to level 11. The Geardrak area medal has been added. Requiem of the Gods [Back to Overview] Guild Castle A hideout just for your guild members, the guild castle! Grow the guild castle with your guild members and create unforgettable memories. Participation Requirements: A guild that has reached Guild Level 20. The guild master can create a guild castle by talking to the NPC Benedict in the Guild Headquarters <Hall of Heroes>. After the guild castle is created, you can click the Guild Casttle button in the Guild UI to access it. Guild Castle Growth The castle can grow up to level 20. As the castle grows, various features will become available. The castle grows using Energy acquired from guild members participating in guild content. You can only start acquiring Guild Castle Energy 7 days after you join a guild. Guild ContentEnergy AcquiredGuild Attendance Check 1 time.10Complete Sharenian’s Underground Culvert 1 time.65Complete Flag Race 1 time.651 Weekly Mission Point.30 Crafting Decorations and Placing Decorations Guild members can participate in various content to collect materials that can be used to craft guild decorations. Guild Castle Decoration MaterialsAcquisition MethodBreath of the ArtisanAcquired at a certain rate when defeating certain difficulty boss monsters or higher.Unknown Smithing StonePurchased from the Supply Station’s NPC Susan.Blessing PowderChallenge Sharenian’s Underground Culvert or complete Flag Race.Maple Tree LumberAcquired at a certain rate when defeating monsters within your level range. The Guild Master can craft decorations using the materials through the Crafting UI. The Guild Master can place crafted decorations in the Guild Castle by using Placement Mode. Decorations can only be placed in customizable areas, such as the lobby, guild room, greenhouse, and party room. You can also acquire special decorations to decorate the greenhouse through guild missions performed jointly by the guild. ※ Among the guild missions, the boss clear mission can only be done 7 days after joining a guild. Lobby The guild castle Lobby serves as the centre of the guild castle and connects the various maps inside. If your guild castle is below level 10, you will have a small Lobby that only contains the Guild Room and Supply Station. If your guild castle is level 10 or higher, you will have a bigger Lobby that also contains the Party Room, Greenhouse, and Enhancement Altar. You can use the item display cases to display items, and the guild honor statues to display specific guild members. As your guild castle level increases, you will be able to use more item display cases and statues. Supply Station You can use the guild castle’s Crafting skill. You can talk to the NPC Susan to use the guild shop. You can access the Crafting UI by talking to the NPC Oliver or by clicking the Crafting button in the guild castle menu. You can access the guild’s shared storage, the Guild Safes. The guild master can designate individual guild members’ weekly entry/exit limits and change the safes’ names. The Guild Safes can only hold consumable, etc, and install items. As the guild castle’s level increases, more Guild Safes will become available. ※ You can access the Supply Station 24 hours after joining a guild. ※ Guild members without access rights cannot use this feature. ※ If a guild is disbanded, the items stored in the guild safes will disappear. Guild Room This is a custom room that you can decorate with various furniture. Party Room This is a custom room that you can decorate with various furniture. You can talk to the NPC Party Master Pierre to play minigames with your guild members. Greenhouse This is a custom room that you can decorate with various furniture. You can place wood decorations and boss statues acquired from guild missions. Enhancement Altar When you use the Enhancement Altar to Star Force enhance an item, your enhancement status will be shared with your guild members. ※ The Enhancement Altar is unlocked when your guild castle reaches level 15. ※ The Enhancement Altar can be used up to 30 times per day per guild. ※ The Enhancement Altar can only be used if there are 2 or more guild members in the room. ※ You can only register equipment items that can reach 20 stars or higher, or Superior equipment items. ※ You can only access this feature 24 hours after joining a guild. ※ Guild members without access rights cannot use this feature. ※ The usage time will begin as soon as you open the Enhancement Altar UI. You can use it for up to 30 minutes. ※ If you are inactive for 3 minutes or more, you will be automatically removed. Guild Castle Minigame – Quiz Us Participation Method: Select <Quiz Us> in the minigame UI to enter the Quiz Room. ※ Up to 200 players can enter the Quiz Room. Gameplay: You can talk to the NPC Party Master Pierre in the Quiz Room to create a quiz. Anyone can do so. ※ You cannot create a quiz if a quiz is already in progress. You can create up to 10 quizzes. The quiz creater must enter each question and answer before starting the quiz. ※ Profanity and certain symbols are not allowed in quiz questions and answers. When a round begins, the question will appear at the top of the screen. Participates can enter the correct answer by using the </answer> command. The round lasts 30 seconds. The first person to enter the answer matching the one provided by the quiz creater will receive 1 point. The creater can use the </hint> command to provide hints. The creator’s hints will be displayed at the bottom right corner for all participants. The participant with the highest score at the end of all the rounds will be crowned the final winner. Guild Castle Minigame – Sky Sovereigns Participation Method: Select <Sky Sovereigns> in the minigame UI to enter the Quiz Room. ※ You cannot enter if a game is already in progress or if the waiting room already has 20 players The first character that enters the waiting UI will be selected as that game’s leader. The leader can change the map. Participants can freely change teams by clicking on the empty slots. When all participants are ready, the leader can start the game. Gameplay Up to 20 players can enter a single game. ※ All buff effects will be removed upon entering. ※ If you are inactive for 10 seconds after entering, you will receive a warning. If you receive 2 warnings, you will be kicked from the game. Sky Sovereigns has a 3 minute time limit. Your character’s nametag, HP bar, and skill colours will be based on your team colour. When jumping, you can press the jump key again to do a Double Jump. You can use the ‘Hammer Swing!’ skill. You can hold the key down to charge the hammer. You can release it to swing the hammer, dealing damage, stunning, and knocking back multiple enemies. When the energy is filled, the stun duration and knockback distance are increased. Items will appear throughout the map, and you can acquire them to use item skills. Bomb Throw!: When used, throw a bomb in front of you to damage and stun multiple enemies. High Barrier: When used, you become immune to damage and stuns for a certain period of time. However, you cannot ignore death from the electricity currents. You can change hotkeys by clicking on the skill icons. During the game, you can press the ‘Tab’ key to check the current score for all characters. You can acquire individual points by killing enemies or destroying Golden Hammers. Enemy Kills: 1 point Golden Hammer Destroyed: 5 points At the end of the game, the RED and BLUE teams’ invidual scores are added up and the team with the higher score wins. [Back to Overview] Guild Reorganization Guild Attendance Check The attendance check will now be done automatically when you login to the game. ※ The guild attendance check may be slightly delayed if the date changes while you are logged in. ※ You can immediately try the guild attendance check by opening the Guild UI. Guild Skills The regular guild skills will be changed as follows. Guild Lv. 1 Guild’s Know-How I: 30 attack/magic attack, 10% damage reduction. Adventure Together: 5% additional experience from killing normal monsters that are level 101~200. Eyes on Small Change: Acquire an additional 8 mesos when you loot mesos while hunting. Guild Lv. 5 Guild’s Know-How II: 6 attack/magic attack. Special Power: 30 Arcane Force, 15 Star Force. I’ll Go To You: Teleport to a designated guild member’s location, 1 hour cooldown. Guild Lv. 10 Guild’s Know-How III: 12% normal monster damage. Merchant: 4% discount when buying items from a shop. However, this discount does not apply if the resell price is 70% or more of the selling price. Guild Lv. 15 Guild’s Know-How IV: 4 attack/magic attack. Master of Scroll Enhancements: When scroll enhancing using Spell Traces or special scrolls, increases success rate by +4%. Guild Lv. 20 Guild’s Know-How V: 40 all stats, 2000 HP. Not Afraid of Death: Death penalty’s duration is decreased by 30%. Guild Lv. 25 Guild’s Know-How VI: 5 attack/magic attack. Craftsman of Scroll Enhancements: When a scroll enhancement fails, there is a 4% chance to not consume a scroll slot. Guild Lv. 30 Sharenian’s Demon Riding: When you ride Sharenian’s Demon, 190 speed and 120 jump. You can no longer acquire G Potions. You can no longer acquire or use Guild’s Blessing/Guild’s Greater Blessing. If you have acquired the same or more Noblesse guild skill points as last week, your invested Noblesse skill points will be maintained. ※ The stats from the Guild’s Greater Blessing have been integrated into the regular guild skill ‘Guild’s Know-How I’. ※ If you acquire fewer Noblesse skill points than last week, your Noblesse skill point investments will be reset on Thursday at midnight like before. Guild Bulletin Board Post headings have been added. The default headings are ‘Notice’ and ‘Free’. You can add up to 3 additional headings, other than the default ones. You can set writing and reading permissions for each heading. You can now check the number of views of a post. A feature has been added to only show posts that have a certain number of likes or more. A feature has been added that lets board admins pin specific posts to the top. A voting feature has been added to posts. A reply feature has been added to comments. Flag Race Flag Race’s entry requirements have been removed. You will now receive 1000 Flag Race Points if you complete the Flag Race within the time limit, regardless of your record. [Back to Overview] Cash MapleStory x Frieren: Beyond Journey’s End items will be sold. Sale Duration: February 12, 2026 after the maintenance ~ March 18, 2026 Sale Items: Frieren: Beyond Journey’s End Outfit Collection Frieren: Beyond Journey’s End Goods Collection Special Luna Crystal 4 Special Luna Petite Pet Equipment ※ These items can only be purchased with NX. They can be traded 1 time. You can use the Frieren: Beyond Journey’s End Outfit Collection to acquire 1 item based on a set rate. You can see the rates here and the full item visuals here. [Back to Overview] Improvements Field The number of monsters required to be defeated to guarantee a Treasure Hunter chest to appear has been decreased. The Rune of Destruction has been changed. When the rune is activated, a powerful attack that hits enemies in a wide range will be activated 28 times consecutively. You will receive a damage boost effect for the duration. [Back to Overview] Items The following decorative items’ effects have been separated so that the effect display options can be applied. Water Bottle of Life ㅋ of Death The Santa Beard item tooltip will now include that it may look awkward when worn with certain items. The Entrancing Fantasy’s Snippets dropped by Radiant Malefic Star (Hard Mode) have been changed to drop 6 bundles of 3 instead of 18 bundles of 1. If you try to use a portal while a VIP Booster/HEXA Booster/Express Booster effect is being applied, a warning window will now be displayed. When extracting a ‘Proof of a Brilliant Hero’ item through the Mage Association NPC Owl, the extraction will now only be done after inputting a confirmation message. [Back to Overview] NPCs The Owl, Helena, and Cygnus Knight NPCs have been removed from the Azmoth Boundary map. The Mage Association’s Box NPC has been added. The World Archive details for certain characters from Azmoth Boundary have been changed. [Back to Overview] Monsters Radiant Malefic Star (Normal)’s HP has been decreased by about 30%. Developer Notes: On the February 5th Maple Now, many players provided feedback about Radiant Malefic Star (Normal)’s boss difficulty setting, so we conducted an internal review. After reviewing, we confirmed that even considering the differences in mechanics between Kaling and Radiant Malefic Star, Radiant Malefic Star (Normal)’s HP was excessively higher than Kaling’s. Therefore, we will adjust Radiant Malefic Star (Normal)’s HP to fit better in the official server update. We apologize again for not being able to sufficiently verify numerical consistency and we will do our best to provide you with better service in the future. The hitboxes for the monsters that appear when using VIP Boosters and Express Boosters have been increased by about 52%. [Back to Overview] Maps The following presets can now be changed in Epic Dungeon boss stages. Link Skills Hyper Stats Ability [Back to Overview] UI The item trade UI and storage UI have been improved so you can drag-and-drop or right-click to register, store, or take out items. The Buff Manager’s scrolling has been improved to be smoother. Erion’s Fragment Voucher acquisition information has been added to the Maple Guide’s Boss Contents section. Certain boss tiers have been changed in the Maple Guide’s Boss Contents section. In the Guild UI’s list of guild members, online guild members will now be displayed at the top, then for offline guild members, they will be listed in order of join date. [Back to Overview] Events You can now use the ‘Sol Janus’ skill in the <Mechaberry Farm: Strawberry Farm Battle> map. [Back to Overview] Maple Home The Maple Home preset feature has been added. This feature lets you save and load your currently decorated Maple Home theme, building design, furniture, and platforms. A total of 3 preset slots are provided. You can set a name for each preset. ※ Presets can be saved per world. ※ Names that violate game policies may result in sanctions. ※ When a preset is loaded, for items placed in a display case, they will be loaded based on the items that are currently displayed and not the items that were there at the time the preset was saved. ※ If a Maple Home theme that was saved in a preset has been extracted and you no longer have it, you cannot load that preset. [Back to Overview] Custom Illustrations For equipment that do not allow custom illustrations to be requested if they are equipped, a message has been added to the item tooltips so you can know ahead of time. Custom illustration requests will now be allowed when the following items are equipped. You can see the list of items here. The Moving Easel NPC’s chat bubble dialogue has been changed. [Back to Overview] Mac Version Beta Service Voice Chat is now available. [Back to Overview] Content Supplementary work is being done to alleviate lag issues in certain content. [Back to Overview] Error Fixes Quests Fixed an issue where the ‘[Maple Union] Champion’s Path’ quest could be started by characters in a Union raid lower than Grand Master I rank. [Back to Overview] Achievements Fixed an issue where the ‘Book of Luminous’ achievement would not be refreshed on characters that already met the requirements. [Back to Overview] Items Fixed an issue in Practice Mode where even if you selected the Mashoor’s Gift Weather Effect item, you could use it through the Buff Sequence. Fixed an issue where in certain situations, even if you used certain buff items, the durations would not be refreshed. Fixed an issue where the Snowflame Riding (Permanent) Voucher tooltip would display ‘unowned’ even if you had it. Fixed an issue where the Name Engraver and Name Remover items could not be used on Astra subweapons. Fixed an issue where emoticons would appear awkwardly if you had a chat bubble ring with animated effects equipped. Fixed an issue where the preview when naming the Malefic Staroid looked awkward. Fixed an issue where in specific situations, the Intense Power Crystals’ sale details’ number of players defeated with would be displayed awkwardly. [Back to Overview] Monsters Fixed an issue in the First Adversary (Easy/Normal/Hard/Extreme) phase 2 where character chat bubbles were covered by the element effects. Fixed an issue in Kai (Normal/Hard) where absolute bind effects were not applied during the Groggy pattern. Fixed an issue in Kai (Normal/Hard) where entering phase 2 would sometimes cause you to exit even if you had time remaining. Fixed an issue in Kai (Normal/Hard) where defeating the reward monster would sometimes cause you to exit even if you had time remaining. [Back to Overview] Maps Fixed an issue in the ‘Where Earth Sleeps’ map where certain portals’ locations were awkward. Fixed an issue in the ‘Labyrinth of Suffering Deepest Core 1’ and ‘Labyrinth of Suffering Interior 2’ maps where certain platforms were awkward. Fixed an issue in the ‘Monster Park Carcion Stage 3: Entangling Forest’ map where the portal images were awkward. Fixed an issue in the ‘Arteria Supply Depot’ map where certain objects were awkward. Fixed an issue in the ‘Beach Rocky Area 1’ map where certain ropes were awkward. Fixed an issue in Champion Raid: Dragon Island where if Mercedes linked skills, the character would not switch even if the control duration ran out. Fixed an issue in Champion Raid content maps where channel names were displayed awkwardly. [Back to Overview] UI Fixed an issue with the Maple Guide UI’s Special Content tab where clicking certain button images moved awkwardly when clicked. Fixed an issue with the Union Artifact’s artifact mission UI where the ‘Complete the Nightmare Paradise quests’ artifact special mission was displayed in an awkward order. Fixed an issue with the Soul Collection where Suu/Annihilation Weapon Suu’s normal illustrations’ display coordinates were awkward. Fixed an issue where if a character died with a Soul Weapon equipped, the Soul Weapon UI could not be opened until the Soul Gauge reset. Fixed an issue with the Buff Manager where if you had a 2x Experience Coupon item, the Union’s Experience item would be incorrectly displayed as owned. Fixed an issue where if you had the Union’s Wealth Stage 2 effect applied then used the Buff Sequence’s ‘use unapplied buffs’ feature, the Union’s Wealth Stage 3 would be applied. Fixed an issue where you could move intermittently while the minigame UI was open. Fixed an issue with the item trade UI where the decorative tab’s item list was displayed awkwardly. Fixed an issue in the cross world boss content <Dimensional Battlefield> where the cross world boss UI’s Find Party button did not work properly. Fixed an issue with the Fast Login UI where if the displayed character was deleted, the UI would display ‘retrieving information’ rather than ‘no character set for fast login’. Fixed an issue with Monster Collection where double-clicking Arma or Arma’s Subordinate monsters would not display the world map. Fixed an issue with item trades where in certain situations, the amount of mesos acquired through the trade was not displayed properly. Fixed an issue with the Maple Guide UI’s per-level content tab and quick move section where even if you dragged the scroll button to the bottom, there would still be a small amount of scroll space. Fixed an issue in the guid skill UI where Noblesse guild skills did not show that they could be registered in a Buff Sequence. Fixed an issue where achievements for cumulative number of daily quest completions did not count when completing Arcane River/Grandis daily quests with the Symbol Quick Pass UI. Fixed an issue where if Zero characters had an Alpha and Beta custom illustration applied then logged off as Alpha, the next time they logged in, it would look as though Beta’s custom illustration was removed. [Back to Overview] Events Fixed an issue with the [Ellanos Chronicle] ‘Ancient Power’ skill description where the number of Authentic Symbols increased was incorrect. Fixed an issue with the [Illusion’s Memories] event where already purchased ‘Heart Upgrade Module: Plasma’ item descriptttion displayed 1 remaining quantity. Fixed an issue with the [Burning Express] event where the message that appeared when failing to use a Booster was mispelled. Fixed an issue with the [Challengers Partner] event where the Partneroid cape appearance sometimes did not display the appearance when Chosen Seren (Hard) was defeated. Fixed an issue with the [Challengers EXP Duo] event where the message that stated you could use Duo Points to level up would be displayed even if the Duo Points usage period ended. [Back to Overview] Nexon Link Fixed an issue where if you played remotely using Nexon Link on a mobile network with an unstable environment, sometimes the controls would not work. [Back to Overview] Mac Version Beta Service Fixed an issue where certain items’ tooltip text looked blurry. Fixed an issue where the amount of space required to install the game was displayed incorrectly. [Back to Overview] Events Accelerated Hunting! Haste BEYOND Receive abundant experience benefits and items with the returning Haste event! Event Participation Requirements: A level 200 or higher character (or a Zero character that has completed story quest Chapter 2). Event Period: February 12, 2026 after the maintenance ~ March 18, 2026 at 11:59 PM. You can select the ‘Accelerated Hunting! Haste BEYOND’ event in the Event List and click the ‘Participate’ button to start the event. After starting the event, you can check the Haste BEYOND field benefits and special mission rewards through the Event List. ※ The field benefits and special mission rewards can be checked and claimed until March 18, 2026 at 11:59 PM. Event Period Special Benefits If you login during the event period, you will receive the ‘Haste BEYOND’ skill. ※ The ‘Haste BEYOND’ skill will be applied until March 18, 2026 at 11:59 PM. Every time you defeat a certain number of monsters within your level range, you will receive Haste BEYOND field benefit effects. ConditionBenefitAlwaysRune respawn and cooldown decreased to 10 minutes.Rune experience effect 2x.Defeat 10,000 monsters.Haste Treasure Boxes will be created in fields.Defeat 50,000 monsters.Elite Monsters/Champions will appear more frequently.Defeat 100,000 monsters.Treasure Box experience acquired +20%.Defeat 150,000 monsters.Additional experience acquired +20%.Defeat 200,000 monsters.Treasure Box experience acquired +30%.Defeat 250,000 monsters.Rune of Blessings respawn cycle decreased.Defeat 300,000 monsters.When hunting, 2x Sol Erda acquired. ※ Monsters within your level range refers to monsters -20~+20 of your character level. ※ The event will only progress if you defeat monsters within your level range while you are in a party of 1 or not in a party. ※ Haste Treasure Boxes will appear when you defeat 10,000 monsters within your level range on a level 200 or higher character in instanced maps. ※ Haste Treasure Boxes will give a gem that gives a lot of experience based on the Treasure Box’s rank. ※ Haste Treasure Boxes are not included in Treasure Hunter achievement counts. ※ You can discover up to 3 Haste Treasure Boxes per day per Maple ID. ※ The Haste BEYOND field benefits will be applied until March 18, 2026 at 11:59 PM. Haste Fever Time After participating in the event, you can use the Haste Fever Time through the special UI that appears when you are in a map with monsters within your level range or through the Haste UI. When you use the Haste Fever Time, Haste Inferno monsters that give lots of experience will be summoned for 5 minutes. When you defeat the Haste Inferno monsters, there is a low chance of acquiring the Cassandra’s Crystal Ball item that helps you defeat monsters within your level range. ※ The Haste Fever Time can be used 1 time per day per Maple ID. ※ The Haste Fever Time can only be used on level 200 or higher characters that have completed the ‘[5th Job] Another Power, Arcane Force’ quest. ※ Haste Inferno monsters are not affected by any additional experience effects. ※ When you acquire the Cassandra’s Crystal Ball item, the Cassandra’s Crystal Ball skill will be activated and attack nearby enemies. ※ The Cassandra’s Crystal Ball skill can stack up to 5 times, and the attack range will be increased/attack cycle will be decreased per stack. ※ When you acquire the Cassandra’s Crystal Ball item, the skill duration will be increased by 10 seconds, and can be increased by up to 90 seconds. Special Missions You can complete missions by defeating a certain number of monsters within your level range. ※ Monsters within your level range refers to monsters -20~+20 of your character level. ※ The event will only progress if you defeat monsters within your level range while you are in a party of 1 or not in a party. ※ The special mission progress record is shared between all characters within the Maple ID. ※ When you complete each mission, the cumulative number of monsters defeated will not be reset. You can receive rewards for each completed mission through the Haste UI. You can complete it up to 10 times during the event period. You can click the <Use> button in the top-right corner of the UI to use the Finale Pass to complete 1 attendance check you did not previously complete. Event Period: February 12, 2026 after the maintenance ~ March 18, 2026 at 11:59 PM. During the event period, you can leap your characters to your desired world through the NPC Isaga in Henesys. You can select the ‘Challengers World Early Leap’ event in the Event List and click the ‘Participate’ button to move to Henesys. ※ Worlds You can Leap To: All worlds. ※ You can only move to the first world you select through the Challengers World Early Leap and End Leap. ※ You can move a total of 5 characters to other worlds through the Challengers World Early Leap and End Leap. Dim Illusion Memories, Bright Illusion Memories, Complete Illusion Memories, Challengers Coins, Advanced Challengers Coins, and Premium Coins acquired in Challengers World will not be moved with the World Leap. Challengers World Bronze/Silver/Gold/Platinum rewards that can be acquired from Challengers World missions can only be claimed in Challengers World. Rewards that can be acquired from the Challengers Pass can only be claimed in Challengers World. The Challengers Pass: Special Support buff will be removed. The Challengers EXP Duo’s designated Challengers World character will be removed and you will no longer acquire Duo Points until the end of the usage period. The Tallahart Fantasia’s Illusion Magic Enhancement’s levels will not be moved. Your acquired Memory Traces and Reminiscense Traces will be maintained, so after the World Leap, you can set the levels again your destination world. If you World Leap without receiving Hyper Burning MAX level achievement rewards, you will receive them based on the world you move to. Please claim the enhanced level achievement rewards in Challengers World before you leap. The Mystic Seal event’s equipped Seal Core stats will be removed. Characters with active Meso Market transactions cannot proceed with World Leap. When World Leaping from Challengers World, items in the cash storage, storage, and Maple Auction will not be moved. Custom Backgrounds acquired using Voucher items will not be moved. ※ Custom Backgrounds acquired using Voucher items can be extracted into Vouchers through the NPC Isaga’s ‘Extract Custom Background’ option. The acquired Voucher can only be put into storage and is permanent. If you leap without checking the results of a custom illustration request, the request will be cancelled. Please confirm the request before leaping. The Tera Burning effect applied when a character is created in Challengers World will not be transferred through World Leap. ※ The Tera Burning effect will be removed when the character is transferred, and the ‘Tera Burning Gift Giveaway!’ quest will no longer be available. The Strange Cube, Karma Meister Cube, Karma Strange Additional Cube, and Karma Star Force 17 Star Enhancement Scroll (Level 160) purchased from the Challengers Shop are Challengers World-only items and you cannot World Leap while holding them. The Selective Challengers Level 4 Special Skill Ring Voucher and the Special Skill Ring acquired from using it are Challengers World-only items and you cannot World Leap while holding them. The Unstable Time Fragments are exclusive to Challengers World and you cannot leap while you have them in your inventory. If a character that has cleared an Epic Dungeon uses World Leap, you cannot claim the cleared Epic Dungeon’s bonus rewards. If there are no empty character slots in the world you are moving to, slots will be added before the Leap. However, if you already have 50 characters in the destination world, you cannot Leap to that world. [Back to Overview] Tell Me Your New Year’s Wish To celebrate New Year’s, people’s wishes have gathered in the Henesys Post Box. Check out the new year’s wish notes inside! Event Participation Requirements: Level 101 or higher characters (or a Zero character that has completed story quest Chapter 2). Event Period: February 12, 2026 after the maintenance ~ February 22, 2026 at 11:59 PM. You can select the ‘Tell Me Your New Year’s Wish’ event in the Event List and click the ‘Participate’ button or talk to the Post Box NPC to start the event. Check the new year’s wish notes, find their owners, and grant their wishes to complete the missions. When you complete all 3 missions, you can complete the quest and receive rewards through the Event List or Post Box NPC. items.title: KMS ver. 1.2.411 – MapleStory Crown: Sol Hecate, Astra Subweapon, & Radiant Malefic Star!
items.summary: The second patch of the MapleStory Crown update has been released! It contains the new 6th job common core, Sol Hecate, the new Astra subweapon system, and...

kmsBreakdown.summary: The second patch of the MapleStory Crown update has been released! It contains the new 6th job common core, Sol Hecate, the new Astra subweapon system, and a new boss, Radiant Malefic Star! There were also some skill balance adjustments and other game changes/QoL.
highlights: HEXA Skill – Common Core
A new common core that is shared between all classes has been added.

Sol Hecate A new skill that collects Sol Erda, drawn in by the Adversary’s resolve, to form a familiar. Sol Hecate will grow as its level increases. When it reaches certain levels, additional exclusive skills will be activated.
In summary, Sol Hecate is a skill that grows as you level it up.

Level 1 Sol Hecate – Automatically tracks and attacks enemies. Sol Hecate: Styx – Attacks and binds enemies for 4 seconds with 1 minute cooldown. This bind stacks with other bind durations (i.e. if you use it immediately after Erda Bind, the enemy will be bound for 10+4 = 14 seconds.) You can use Sol Hecate: Pactum to link this with one of your other skills (e.g. AB link) to have Sol Hecate automatically use Styx when that skill is used. Otherwise, you can cast Styx manually by hotkeying it separately.
[Back to Overview]

Sol Hecate A new skill that collects Sol Erda, drawn in by the Adversary’s resolve, to form a familiar. Sol Hecate will grow as its level increases. When it reaches certain levels, additional exclusive skills will be activated.
In summary, Sol Hecate is a skill that grows as you level it up.

Level 1 Sol Hecate – Automatically tracks and attacks enemies. Sol Hecate: Styx – Attacks and binds enemies for 4 seconds with 1 minute cooldown. This bind stacks with other bind durations (i.e. if you use it immediately after Erda Bind, the enemy will be bound for 10+4 = 14 seconds.) You can use Sol Hecate: Pactum to link this with one of your other skills (e.g. AB link) to have Sol Hecate automatically use Styx when that skill is used. Otherwise, you can cast Styx manually by hotkeying it separately.
[Back to Overview]

details.value: HEXA Skill – Common Core
A new common core that is shared between all classes has been added.

items: Sol Hecate A new skill that collects Sol Erda, drawn in by the Adversary’s resolve, to form a familiar. Sol Hecate will grow as its level increases. When it reaches certain levels, additional exclusive skills will be activated.
A new skill that collects Sol Erda, drawn in by the Adversary’s resolve, to form a familiar.
Sol Hecate will grow as its level increases. When it reaches certain levels, additional exclusive skills will be activated.
Sol Hecate: Styx A new skill that releases a wave of densely concentrated Sol Erda to lead enemies to their death.
A new skill that releases a wave of densely concentrated Sol Erda to lead enemies to their death.
Sol Hecate: Charon A new skill that protects the contract holder from their fate of death when hit by a fatal attack.
A new skill that protects the contract holder from their fate of death when hit by a fatal attack.
Sol Hecate: Phlegethon A new skill that disrupts the order of the Sol Erda that forms the world to temporarily break the barrier between the living and the underworld.
A new skill that disrupts the order of the Sol Erda that forms the world to temporarily break the barrier between the living and the underworld.
Sol Hecate: Pactum A new skill that aligns Sol Hecate’s will with the Adversary, controlling how Sol Hecate’s exclusive skills manifest.
A new skill that aligns Sol Hecate’s will with the Adversary, controlling how Sol Hecate’s exclusive skills manifest.

Sol Hecate, Sol Hecate: Styx, and Sol Hecate: Phlegethon will have different damage values for each class. Note: I won’t be listing every single class’ damage values since that’s crazy. If you want to see them all, you can check Namu Wiki.
Note: I won’t be listing every single class’ damage values since that’s crazy. If you want to see them all, you can check Namu Wiki.

details.value: In summary, Sol Hecate is a skill that grows as you level it up.

items: Level 1 Sol Hecate – Automatically tracks and attacks enemies. Sol Hecate: Styx – Attacks and binds enemies for 4 seconds with 1 minute cooldown. This bind stacks with other bind durations (i.e. if you use it immediately after Erda Bind, the enemy will be bound for 10+4 = 14 seconds.) You can use Sol Hecate: Pactum to link this with one of your other skills (e.g. AB link) to have Sol Hecate automatically use Styx when that skill is used. Otherwise, you can cast Styx manually by hotkeying it separately.
Sol Hecate – Automatically tracks and attacks enemies.

Sol Hecate: Styx – Attacks and binds enemies for 4 seconds with 1 minute cooldown. This bind stacks with other bind durations (i.e. if you use it immediately after Erda Bind, the enemy will be bound for 10+4 = 14 seconds.) You can use Sol Hecate: Pactum to link this with one of your other skills (e.g. AB link) to have Sol Hecate automatically use Styx when that skill is used. Otherwise, you can cast Styx manually by hotkeying it separately.

Level 20 Sol Hecate: Charon – If you are hit by an attack that would kill you, it will be ignored and you will be invincible for 10 seconds with a 10 minute cooldown. You can use Sol Hecate: Pactum to set a hotkey to end the invincibility effect early.

Sol Hecate: Charon – If you are hit by an attack that would kill you, it will be ignored and you will be invincible for 10 seconds with a 10 minute cooldown. You can use Sol Hecate: Pactum to set a hotkey to end the invincibility effect early.
Level 30 Sol Hecate: Phlegeton – Attacks enemies with a big burst of damage with a 2 minute cooldown. You can link this with another skill just like Styx or use it manually.
Sol Hecate: Phlegeton – Attacks enemies with a big burst of damage with a 2 minute cooldown. You can link this with another skill just like Styx or use it manually.

sections.summary: A new common core that is shared between all classes has been added.

items: Sol Hecate[Master Level: 30]The Sol Erda drawn in by the Adversary’s resolve is collected to form a familiar. Sol Hecate reacts to the Adversary’s will to automatically attack enemies and use its special skills.Sol Hecate will grow based on its level, and when it reaches certain levels, it will activate additional special skills that can be used.You can set which skills Sol Hecate will react to and use its special skills through Sol Hecate: Pactum. Sol Hecate will prioritize attacking the boss monster with the highest max HP. If it hits an enemy using Damage Reflect, you will not take damage.On/Off skill.Sol Hecate Translucency Applicaton On/Off: Right-click.Level 10: 20% defense ignore, 20% boss damage.Level 20: Activate the special skill Charon, 20% defense ignore, 20% boss damage.Level 30: Activate the special skill Phlegeton. [Level 30]Consumes 100 MP. Summon Sol Hecate.Sol Hecate will automatically track enemies and periodically create 4 high-density Sol Erda Particles.Sol Erda Particle: Deals X% damage X times.
[Level 30]Consumes 100 MP. Summon Sol Hecate.Sol Hecate will automatically track enemies and periodically create 4 high-density Sol Erda Particles.Sol Erda Particle: Deals X% damage X times.

Sol Hecate: Styx[Master Level: 30]Release a wave of densely concentrated Sol Erda that leads enemies to their deaths. Enemies caught in the wave will be bound and the Seeds of Death will be planted on them.The Seeds of Death will grow within them for a certain period of time to attack continuously, then take their lives when they fully bloom.You can set how the skill manifests with Sol Hecate: Pactum. [Level 30]Consumes 50 MP. Deals X% damage X times on up to 15 enemies.Enemies hit are bound for 4 seconds.Enemies hit will be inflicted with the Seeds of Death debuff for 3 seconds.Styx’s bind stacks with other binds and is applied additionally. After the bind is applied, there is a bind resistance of 47 seconds.Seeds of Death: When growing, deals X% damage X times. When they fully bloom, activate 7 life harvests that deal X% damage X times.Cooldown: 60 seconds.

[Level 30]Consumes 50 MP. Deals X% damage X times on up to 15 enemies.Enemies hit are bound for 4 seconds.Enemies hit will be inflicted with the Seeds of Death debuff for 3 seconds.Styx’s bind stacks with other binds and is applied additionally. After the bind is applied, there is a bind resistance of 47 seconds.Seeds of Death: When growing, deals X% damage X times. When they fully bloom, activate 7 life harvests that deal X% damage X times.Cooldown: 60 seconds.

Sol Hecate: Charon[Master Level: 30]Protect the contract holder from their fate of death when hit by a fatal attack.You can set the skill end method with Sol Hecate: Pactum. [Level 30]If your HP reaches 0, this skill will activate and ignore the death. Afterwards, you are invincible for 10 seconds.You can end the invincibility by using the command set through Sol Hecate: Pactum.Reactivation cooldown: 600 seconds.

[Level 30]If your HP reaches 0, this skill will activate and ignore the death. Afterwards, you are invincible for 10 seconds.You can end the invincibility by using the command set through Sol Hecate: Pactum.Reactivation cooldown: 600 seconds.

Sol Hecate: Phlegethon[Master Level: 30]Disrupt the order of the Sol Erda that forms the world to temporarily break the barrier between the world of the living and the underworld.The underworld’s torrents manifested in reality incinerate enemies. After the rift closes, the remaining unstable energy blooms in all directions to turn everything into nothingness. You can set how the skill manifests with Sol Hecate: Pactum.Illustration Cutscene On/Off: Right-click. [Level 30]Consumes 50 MP. Activate 8 torrents that deal X% damage X times on up to 15 enemies.After the rift closes, activate 10 full blooms that deal X% damage X times.Cooldown: 120 seconds.
[Level 30]Consumes 50 MP. Activate 8 torrents that deal X% damage X times on up to 15 enemies.After the rift closes, activate 10 full blooms that deal X% damage X times.Cooldown: 120 seconds.

Sol Hecate: Pactum[Master Level: 1]Align Sol Hecate’s will with the Adversary to set how Sol Hecate’s special skills are manifested. [Level 1]Set the skills that Styx and Phlegethon react to.Set the command that you can cancel Charon’s invincibility effect with.
[Level 1]Set the skills that Styx and Phlegethon react to.Set the command that you can cancel Charon’s invincibility effect with.

You can acquire the Astra subweapon after completing quests to collect Fierce Battle Traces and Erion’s Fragments. There are three stages of the Astra subweapon, with the following requirements.

details.value: You can acquire Fierce Battle Traces by defeating Grandis area bosses at Chosen Seren (Normal) and above.

You can acquire untradeable Erion’s Fragments by completing the following Grandis area daily quests. You will acquire the Fragments based on the highest level area daily quest you have completed that day.

If you receive the rewards from a lower level area’s daily quest then complete a higher level area’s daily quest, you will receive Erion’s Fragments equal to the difference in the reward amount between those two areas.

details.value: For Zero characters:

items: If a Zero character equips an Astra Hourglass, their subweapon slot’s Great Sword/Long Sword’s damage/boss damage/defense ignore boosts from potential/additional potential/base stats/bonus stats will not be applied. They will be replaced with the Astra Hourglass’ stats.

details.value: ※ If you unequip the Astra Hourglass, the subweapon slot’s Great Sword/Long Sword’s stats will be applied again.
Developer Notes:
Unlike existing subweapons that had varying utility across jobs, we aimed to ensure that the Astra subweapons offered as similar utility as possible.
To achieve this, we decided to adjust subweapons’ utility in cases where they differed from other jobs.

First, for Archmage (F/P), Archmage (I/L), Bishop, Flame Wizard, Evan, Battle Mage, Shadower, and Heroes/Paladins/Soul Masters that chose one-handed Genesis weapons, you can acquire additional stats equivalent to a 20 star Deimos Shield through special scroll enhancements.

For Dual Blades, since they have already Star Forced their subweapons, you can transfer Star Force enhancement levels by paying the difference in the costs of destruction recovery. In addition, you can acquire stats that other classes gain from Star Force enhancements on their Astra subweapons through special scroll enhancements.

For Demon Slayers and Demon Avengers, if you equip an Astra Force Shield that had the Ruin Force Shield transferred to it, you can use the ‘Astra Infusion’ on/off skill that increases final damage and damage taken, the same as a Ruin Force Shield.

For Zero, depending on if you were in Alpha/Beta, the subweapon’s base stats/bonus stats’ damage/boss damage/defense ignore effects and the potential/additional potential’s effects would be applied. However, if Zero equips the Astra subweapon, they will no longer acquire those stats from potential/additional potential or base stats/bonus stats. The Astra subweapon’s base stats will be increased equal to those values.
[Back to Overview]
Astra Subweapon Transfer Rules Per Class
Common

items: You can transfer your subweapon’s potential and additional potential to the Astra subweapon by paying 1b mesos.
You can also transfer again to an Astra subweapon that has already been transferred to or enhanced.
When you transfer a subweapon, the Star Force enhancements will be reset except for certain shields and Blades.
Items with the Mark of Binding applied cannot be transferred.
The list of subweapons that can be transferred to each Astra subweapon are as follows.

details.value: ※ Zero cannot transfer to an Astra subweapon.
Developer Notes:
The list of transferrable items is limited to the ‘subweapons for which top-tier items are difficult to identify clearly and exceptional shield items’.

For example, for Dual Blades, the Arcane Shade Blade is a completely superior item to the Absolabs Blade in terms of stats, and their tradeability is the same, so only Arcane Shade Blade transfer is supported.

In comparison, for Archmage (F/P), the Red Green Book <Final> is inferior to the Deimos Sage Shield in terms of stats but they are both valuable options due to the difference in tradeability, so both items transfer is supported.
Demon Avenger’s Extreme Force Shield and Ruin Force Shield both support transfer for the same reasons. However, the Astra Infusion skill can only be used if you transfer a Ruin Force Shield.
For Zero, transfer is not supported as they did not previously have an enhanceable subweapon.
Heroes, Paladins, and Soul Masters that have a Genesis Saber/Axe/Hammer or Destiny Saber/Axe/Hammer

items: For these jobs, only the characters that have chosen the one-handed Genesis weapon or Destiny weapon can acquire the Astra Sacred Aegis or Astra Topaz Aegis.

When transferring the Deimos Warrior Shield, the item’s Star Force enhancement levels will be transferred to the Astra Sacred Aegis or Astra Topaz Aegis, but there will be a different transfer cost applied.

details.value: Developer Notes:

When using shields that have already been Star Force enhanced, their Star Force enhancement stages will be transferred. However, an additional cost will be applied to account for the difference in cost due to level limit and cost of destruction recovery.

In addition, shields can have Miraculous Equipment Enhancement Scrolls applied to them, which makes those shields have higher value. Items with MEE scrolls applied are harder to determine scroll enhancement changes, and they can also be mixed with regular Equipment Enhancement Scrolls which can lead to significant differences in stats and value even with the same number of stars.

Therefore, the Star Force adjustments for these items will be based on a new standard called ‘item score’. ‘Item score’ is calculated by excluding the effects acquired from potential/additional potential, assigning 1 point per main stat and 3 points per attack/magic attack, then adding them together.

Items that exceed the minimum item score will have their Star Force upgraded to a level appropriate to their value when transferred. Items with insufficient item scores will not receive Star Force adjustments. As of now, only 10 out of 276 items will not receive adjustments. The majority of equipment will be adjusted as intended.

The transfer rules related to MEE Scrolls will only apply to the current end-game equipment (Deimos Sage Shield, Deimos Darkness Shield, Fearless Prelude, Fearless Wrist). Deimos Warrior Shield and Fearless Kite Shield, which are not considered end-game equipment and have relatively low value are not included in the Star Force adjustments, and they will be transferred with the same rules as regular subweapons.
