details.value: Radiant Malefic Star is similar to Jin Hilla in that while it only has one form, as its HP is lowered, it unlocks more attacks. In addition, there are two ‘dimensions’ you can move between, illusion and reality, each with different attacks and mechanics.

items: Phase 1: 100%~80% HP
Phase 2: 40%~79% HP
Phase 3: 0%~39% HP

details.value: When you enter the boss, in the preparation room, there will be a special altar that you can press Harvest/NPC Chat on. Only one person in the party can do so (in a solo, it will always be yourself), and the person that does will become Awakened, allowing them to swap between illusion/reality in the boss and interact with the Illusion’s Essence cores that spawn in the fight (will explain a little more later).

The preparation room also has the Unfinished Journal NPC which you can talk to to read the thoughts of an unlucky sailor that previously faced off against Radiant Malefic Star. It serves as a quick guide to the boss’ mechanics.

The boss has a regular Death Count system (5 per person) but also the Predation Value. If this value reaches 1000, you will instantly fail the boss regardless of your remaining Death Count. The value will increase from being hit by certain attacks and can be decreased by interacting with some of the mechanics.
The timer underneath is the countdown to when the boss will forcefully swap you between illusion and reality. You will begin the fight in the illusion map.

The main mechanic of this boss is the Illusion Magic/Illusion Detection gauge. The gold circle is Illusion Magic, and it starts from 1000. The higher the Illusion Magic gauge, the boss’ attacks will be stronger and additional patterns will be unlocked.
The center orb is the Illusion Detection gauge, and it can go up to 1000. If you hover over the Illusion Detection, you can see the boss’ Illusion Magic cooldown.

When you are hit by the boss’ attacks, the Illusion Detection cooldown will go up, delaying it. If the cooldown reaches 0, the boss will cast a special attack that creates an Illusion’s Essence. The awakened party member can interact with it to reduce the Illusion Magic gauge by 200.
The awakened party member can also press Harvest/NPC Chat to use the Illusion Projection/Reality Alert skill, which lets you freely swap between illusion and reality.

In the reality map, the boss will have more attacks and will attack more frequently. However, based on your Illusion Magic gauge, you can increase your final damage by up to 30% at the cost of making the boss stronger. In the reality map, when the boss casts a special attack that creates an Illusion’s Essence, you can choose to interact with one of two cores. The left core will decrease the Illusion Magic gauge (lowering your final damage) while the right core will increase the gauge (increasing your final damage)

Therefore, Radiant Malefic Star is a boss that is focused on risk/reward. You can choose to stay in the illusion map as long as possible for fairly simple boss patterns with no increased damage, or stay in the reality map to increase your final damage at the cost of making the boss stronger. Of course, there’s some more complexity but I didn’t want to write a full guide. You can read more details on the Namu Wiki page.

details.value: You can select the ‘[Boss Clear Event] Call of Deception’ event in the Event List and click the ‘Participate’ button to start the event.
※ If you are logged in to another account in the same Nexon ID at the same time, you cannot participate in the Clear event.
※ If you participate in the event in an abormal way, your game or content usage may be restricted and you will be excluded from the event participation and rewards.
Regular Clear Mission

You can receive clear rewards through the Event List by defeating Radiant Malefic Star at least 1 time during the event period. The rewards are different based on the Radiant Malefic Star mode that you clear, and the clear record is shared between characters within the same world.
Defeating Radiant Malefic Star on a higher difficulty will also grant you the rewards given for defeating the lower difficulties.
※ The items acquired from using the vouchers are permanent and untradable.
※ You cannot complete the missions by clearing in Practice Mode or Story Mode.
※ After clearing Radiant Malefic Star (Normal/Hard), you must enter the reward map to satisfy the clear mission requirements.
※ You can participate in the event by clearing the boss in a party of 2 or more as well.
Solo First Clear Mission
You can complete the Solo First Clear Mission by being one of the first 10 players that defeat Hard Radiant Malefic Star during the event period.
If you complete the Solo First Clear mission, you can acquire the following rewards through the Event List during the separate reward claiming period.
※ After defeating Radiant Malefic Star (Hard), you must defeat the reward monster ‘Tilted Scales’ to be recognized as a clear.
※ Your clear will be acknowledged even if you clear in Cross World Boss Content.
※ You cannot participate in the Solo First Clear Mission if you are in Practice Mode or not on a Green PC. Even if you defeat the reward monster, it will not be recognized as a First Clear.
※ If you enter Radiant Malefic Star before January 22 but clear after January 22, you will not be able to participate in the solo first clear mission.
※ You cannot enter Radiant Malefic Star (Hard) in a party of 1 on January 22 from 12:00 AM until 7:00 PM.
※ You will only be acknowledged as participating in the solo first clear mission if you enter as a party of 1 after January 22 at 7:00 PM.
※ The Solo First Clear mission rewards can be claimed after the February 12, 2026 maintenance 1 time per Nexon ID, and can only be claimed on the character that completed the mission.
※ If your game usage is restricted due to violations of the operating policy during the event period, you may be excluded from receiving the First Clear mission rewards.

sections.title: Burning Express

details.value: The appearance of new Express Boosters! Complete the daily attendance check and grow even faster!
Event Participation Requirements:

items: A level 260 or higher character (or a Zero character that has completed story quest Chapter 2).

details.value: Event Period:

items: January 15, 2026 after the maintenance ~ February 11, 2026 at 11:59 PM.

details.value: You can select the ‘Burning Express’ event in the Event List and click the ‘Participate’ button to start the event.
Daily Attendance Check
Once per day, you can defeat 1,000 monsters within your level range to complete the attendance check and receive rewards.

You can press the [Use] button at the bottom left of the Burning Express UI to use the Attendance Check Pass to complete previously missed attendance checks. You can use it for the number of participations you missed as of the previous day. Each Pass costs 3,000 Maple Points.
※ The participation records are shared between all characters within the Maple ID.
※ The monsters defeated record is shared between all characters within the Maple ID and is reset every day at 12:00 AM.
※ The event will only progress if you defeat monsters within your level range while you are in a party of 1 or not in a party.
※ The number of monsters within your level range defeated will not increase in the cross world boss content <Dimensional Battlefield>.
Base Attendance Check Rewards
You can acquire the following rewards based on your number of attendance check completions.
※ All reward items can be claimed until February 11, 2026 at 11:59 PM and will expire on February 12, 2026 at 2:00 AM.
※ The Advanced EXP Vouchers and Express Boosters can only be used by level 260 or higher characters. They can only be used after completing the ‘Another Power, Authentic Force’ quest.
Express Pass Attendance Check Rewards
You can buy the Express Pass in the Cash Shop for 30,000 NX. When you use it, you can acquire the following rewards based on your number of completed attendance checks.
However, even if you buy the Express Pass, you cannot acquire the rewards for attendance checks you have not completed.
※ All reward items can be claimed until February 11, 2026 at 11:59 PM and will expire on February 12, 2026 at 2:00 AM.
※ The Express Boosters can only be used by level 260 or higher characters. They can only be used after completing the ‘Another Power, Authentic Force’ quest.
Express Boosters
You can use the Express Booster in a map with monsters within your level range to summon Growing Flame monsters that give a large amount of experience for 100 seconds.
Express Boosters do not receive any additional experience effects.
You can also purchase Express Boosters in the <Night of Falling Illusions> meso shop.

items: Express Booster (30m mesos) [limit of 10 per week per world]

sections.title: Momentum Pass

details.value: Cassandra’s support is speeding up! Level up the Momentum Pass and receive rewards!
Event Participation Requirements:

items: A level 280 or higher character (or a Zero character that has completed story quest Chapter 2).

details.value: Event Period:

items: January 15, 2026 after the maintenance ~ February 11, 2026 at 11:59 PM.

details.value: You can select the ‘Momentum Pass’ event in the Event List and click the ‘Participate’ button to start the event.
※ You can only participate in this event on one character per person.
※ The character and Maple ID participating in this event cannot be changed afterwards.
※ Even if you delete the character that participates in the Momentum Pass event, you cannot participate in the event on another character.
Momentum Pass Levels and Weekly Missions
The Momentum Pass levels range from level 0~10. You can reach the next level by acquiring 720 Momentum Pass Points.
You can acquire Momentum Pass Points by completing weekly missions.
You can acquire up to 2,000 Momentum Pass Points per week.
When you have acquired all of the Momentum Pass Points you can for thet week, all remaining uncompleted missions will be completed.
※ The missions can only be progressed on the character that is participating in the event.
※ The weekly Momentum Pass point acquisition and weekly mission progress are reset every Thursday at midnight.
※ Symbol daily quests, Monster Park, Monster Park Extreme, and Epic Dungeon missions completed before participating in the event are not included in the weekly mission progress.
You can buy Momentum Pass Points based on the amount of Points you did not acquire from the previous week.
※ 100 Momentum Pass Points costs 1,000 Maple Points.
Pass Level Achievement Rewards
If you buy the Prime Momentum Pass, you can claim the Prime Momentum Rewards.
※ All rewards can be claimed during the event period until February 11, 2026 at 11:59 PM.
※ All rewards will expire on February 12, 2026 at 2:00 AM.
※ All rewards except for the VIP Sauna Entry Tickets are untradeable.
※ The VIP Sauna Entry Tickets can only be put into storage.
Prime Momentum Pass
The Prime Momentum Pass will be available for purchase.

items: Sale Period: January 15, 2026 after the maintenance ~ February 11, 2026 at 11:00 PM
Price: 49,800 NX (cannot be purchased with Maple Points or Mileage)
When you use the ‘Prime Momentum Pass’, you can claim the Prime Momentum rewards.

details.value: ※ The ‘Prime Momentum Pass’ cannot be refunded after purchase.
※ The ‘Prime Momentum Pass’ will expire on February 11, 2026 at 11:59 PM.
※ Regardless of the purchase date, the Prime Pass effects will be applied until February 11, 2026 at 11:59 PM.
※ The ‘Prime Momentum Pass’ can only be purchased 1 time per person.
※ You cannot purchase the ‘Prime Momentum Pass’ if you have no characters participating in the event.

details.value: HEXA Skill – Common Core
A new common core that is shared between all classes has been added.

items: Sol Hecate A new skill that collects Sol Erda, drawn in by the Adversary’s resolve, to form a familiar. Sol Hecate will grow as its level increases. When it reaches certain levels, additional exclusive skills will be activated.
A new skill that collects Sol Erda, drawn in by the Adversary’s resolve, to form a familiar.
Sol Hecate will grow as its level increases. When it reaches certain levels, additional exclusive skills will be activated.
Sol Hecate: Styx A new skill that releases a wave of densely concentrated Sol Erda to lead enemies to their death.
A new skill that releases a wave of densely concentrated Sol Erda to lead enemies to their death.
Sol Hecate: Charon A new skill that protects the contract holder from their fate of death when hit by a fatal attack.
A new skill that protects the contract holder from their fate of death when hit by a fatal attack.
Sol Hecate: Phlegethon A new skill that disrupts the order of the Sol Erda that forms the world to temporarily break the barrier between the living and the underworld.
A new skill that disrupts the order of the Sol Erda that forms the world to temporarily break the barrier between the living and the underworld.
Sol Hecate: Pactum A new skill that aligns Sol Hecate’s will with the Adversary, controlling how Sol Hecate’s exclusive skills manifest.
A new skill that aligns Sol Hecate’s will with the Adversary, controlling how Sol Hecate’s exclusive skills manifest.

Sol Hecate, Sol Hecate: Styx, and Sol Hecate: Phlegethon will have different damage values for each class. Note: I won’t be listing every single class’ damage values since that’s crazy. If you want to see them all, you can check Namu Wiki.

details.value: In summary, Sol Hecate is a skill that grows as you level it up.

items: Level 1 Sol Hecate – Automatically tracks and attacks enemies. Sol Hecate: Styx – Attacks and binds enemies for 4 seconds with 1 minute cooldown. This bind stacks with other bind durations (i.e. if you use it immediately after Erda Bind, the enemy will be bound for 10+4 = 14 seconds.) You can use Sol Hecate: Pactum to link this with one of your other skills (e.g. AB link) to have Sol Hecate automatically use Styx when that skill is used. Otherwise, you can cast Styx manually by hotkeying it separately.
Sol Hecate – Automatically tracks and attacks enemies.

Sol Hecate: Styx – Attacks and binds enemies for 4 seconds with 1 minute cooldown. This bind stacks with other bind durations (i.e. if you use it immediately after Erda Bind, the enemy will be bound for 10+4 = 14 seconds.) You can use Sol Hecate: Pactum to link this with one of your other skills (e.g. AB link) to have Sol Hecate automatically use Styx when that skill is used. Otherwise, you can cast Styx manually by hotkeying it separately.

Level 20 Sol Hecate: Charon – If you are hit by an attack that would kill you, it will be ignored and you will be invincible for 10 seconds with a 10 minute cooldown. You can use Sol Hecate: Pactum to set a hotkey to end the invincibility effect early.

Sol Hecate: Charon – If you are hit by an attack that would kill you, it will be ignored and you will be invincible for 10 seconds with a 10 minute cooldown. You can use Sol Hecate: Pactum to set a hotkey to end the invincibility effect early.
Level 30 Sol Hecate: Phlegeton – Attacks enemies with a big burst of damage with a 2 minute cooldown. You can link this with another skill just like Styx or use it manually.
Sol Hecate: Phlegeton – Attacks enemies with a big burst of damage with a 2 minute cooldown. You can link this with another skill just like Styx or use it manually.

Developer Notes:
Unlike existing subweapons that had varying utility across jobs, we aimed to ensure that the Astra subweapons offered as similar utility as possible.
To achieve this, we decided to adjust subweapons’ utility in cases where they differed from other jobs.

First, for Archmage (F/P), Archmage (I/L), Bishop, Flame Wizard, Evan, Battle Mage, Shadower, and Heroes/Paladins/Soul Masters that chose one-handed Genesis weapons, you can acquire additional stats equivalent to a 20 star Deimos Shield through special scroll enhancements.

For Dual Blades, since they have already Star Forced their subweapons, you can transfer Star Force enhancement levels by paying the difference in the costs of destruction recovery. In addition, you can acquire stats that other classes gain from Star Force enhancements on their Astra subweapons through special scroll enhancements.

For Demon Slayers and Demon Avengers, if you equip an Astra Force Shield that had the Ruin Force Shield transferred to it, you can use the ‘Astra Infusion’ on/off skill that increases final damage and damage taken, the same as a Ruin Force Shield.

For Zero, depending on if you were in Alpha/Beta, the subweapon’s base stats/bonus stats’ damage/boss damage/defense ignore effects and the potential/additional potential’s effects would be applied. However, if Zero equips the Astra subweapon, they will no longer acquire those stats from potential/additional potential or base stats/bonus stats. The Astra subweapon’s base stats will be increased equal to those values.

Developer Notes:
The list of transferrable items is limited to the ‘subweapons for which top-tier items are difficult to identify clearly and exceptional shield items’.

For example, for Dual Blades, the Arcane Shade Blade is a completely superior item to the Absolabs Blade in terms of stats, and their tradeability is the same, so only Arcane Shade Blade transfer is supported.

In comparison, for Archmage (F/P), the Red Green Book <Final> is inferior to the Deimos Sage Shield in terms of stats but they are both valuable options due to the difference in tradeability, so both items transfer is supported.
Demon Avenger’s Extreme Force Shield and Ruin Force Shield both support transfer for the same reasons. However, the Astra Infusion skill can only be used if you transfer a Ruin Force Shield.
For Zero, transfer is not supported as they did not previously have an enhanceable subweapon.

details.value: Developer Notes:

When using shields that have already been Star Force enhanced, their Star Force enhancement stages will be transferred. However, an additional cost will be applied to account for the difference in cost due to level limit and cost of destruction recovery.

In addition, shields can have Miraculous Equipment Enhancement Scrolls applied to them, which makes those shields have higher value. Items with MEE scrolls applied are harder to determine scroll enhancement changes, and they can also be mixed with regular Equipment Enhancement Scrolls which can lead to significant differences in stats and value even with the same number of stars.

Therefore, the Star Force adjustments for these items will be based on a new standard called ‘item score’. ‘Item score’ is calculated by excluding the effects acquired from potential/additional potential, assigning 1 point per main stat and 3 points per attack/magic attack, then adding them together.

Items that exceed the minimum item score will have their Star Force upgraded to a level appropriate to their value when transferred. Items with insufficient item scores will not receive Star Force adjustments. As of now, only 10 out of 276 items will not receive adjustments. The majority of equipment will be adjusted as intended.

The transfer rules related to MEE Scrolls will only apply to the current end-game equipment (Deimos Sage Shield, Deimos Darkness Shield, Fearless Prelude, Fearless Wrist). Deimos Warrior Shield and Fearless Kite Shield, which are not considered end-game equipment and have relatively low value are not included in the Star Force adjustments, and they will be transferred with the same rules as regular subweapons.

details.value: Radiant Malefic Star is similar to Jin Hilla in that while it only has one form, as its HP is lowered, it unlocks more attacks. In addition, there are two ‘dimensions’ you can move between, illusion and reality, each with different attacks and mechanics.

items: Phase 1: 100%~80% HP
Phase 2: 40%~79% HP
Phase 3: 0%~39% HP

details.value: When you enter the boss, in the preparation room, there will be a special altar that you can press Harvest/NPC Chat on. Only one person in the party can do so (in a solo, it will always be yourself), and the person that does will become Awakened, allowing them to swap between illusion/reality in the boss and interact with the Illusion’s Essence cores that spawn in the fight (will explain a little more later).

The preparation room also has the Unfinished Journal NPC which you can talk to to read the thoughts of an unlucky sailor that previously faced off against Radiant Malefic Star. It serves as a quick guide to the boss’ mechanics.

The boss has a regular Death Count system (5 per person) but also the Predation Value. If this value reaches 1000, you will instantly fail the boss regardless of your remaining Death Count. The value will increase from being hit by certain attacks and can be decreased by interacting with some of the mechanics.
The timer underneath is the countdown to when the boss will forcefully swap you between illusion and reality. You will begin the fight in the illusion map.

The main mechanic of this boss is the Illusion Magic/Illusion Detection gauge. The gold circle is Illusion Magic, and it starts from 1000. The higher the Illusion Magic gauge, the boss’ attacks will be stronger and additional patterns will be unlocked.
The center orb is the Illusion Detection gauge, and it can go up to 1000. If you hover over the Illusion Detection, you can see the boss’ Illusion Magic cooldown.

When you are hit by the boss’ attacks, the Illusion Detection cooldown will go up, delaying it. If the cooldown reaches 0, the boss will cast a special attack that creates an Illusion’s Essence. The awakened party member can interact with it to reduce the Illusion Magic gauge by 200.
The awakened party member can also press Harvest/NPC Chat to use the Illusion Projection/Reality Alert skill, which lets you freely swap between illusion and reality.

In the reality map, the boss will have more attacks and will attack more frequently. However, based on your Illusion Magic gauge, you can increase your final damage by up to 30% at the cost of making the boss stronger. In the reality map, when the boss casts a special attack that creates an Illusion’s Essence, you can choose to interact with one of two cores. The left core will decrease the Illusion Magic gauge (lowering your final damage) while the right core will increase the gauge (increasing your final damage)

Therefore, Radiant Malefic Star is a boss that is focused on risk/reward. You can choose to stay in the illusion map as long as possible for fairly simple boss patterns with no increased damage, or stay in the reality map to increase your final damage at the cost of making the boss stronger. Of course, there’s some more complexity but I didn’t want to write a full guide. You can read more details on the Namu Wiki page.

※ If you participate in the event in an abormal way, your game or content usage may be restricted and you will be excluded from the event participation and rewards.
Regular Clear Mission

You can receive clear rewards through the Event List by defeating Radiant Malefic Star at least 1 time during the event period. The rewards are different based on the Radiant Malefic Star mode that you clear, and the clear record is shared between characters within the same world.
Defeating Radiant Malefic Star on a higher difficulty will also grant you the rewards given for defeating the lower difficulties.
※ The items acquired from using the vouchers are permanent and untradable.
※ You cannot complete the missions by clearing in Practice Mode or Story Mode.
※ After clearing Radiant Malefic Star (Normal/Hard), you must enter the reward map to satisfy the clear mission requirements.
※ You can participate in the event by clearing the boss in a party of 2 or more as well.
Solo First Clear Mission
You can complete the Solo First Clear Mission by being one of the first 10 players that defeat Hard Radiant Malefic Star during the event period.
If you complete the Solo First Clear mission, you can acquire the following rewards through the Event List during the separate reward claiming period.
※ After defeating Radiant Malefic Star (Hard), you must defeat the reward monster ‘Tilted Scales’ to be recognized as a clear.
※ Your clear will be acknowledged even if you clear in Cross World Boss Content.
※ You cannot participate in the Solo First Clear Mission if you are in Practice Mode or not on a Green PC. Even if you defeat the reward monster, it will not be recognized as a First Clear.
※ If you enter Radiant Malefic Star before January 22 but clear after January 22, you will not be able to participate in the solo first clear mission.
※ You cannot enter Radiant Malefic Star (Hard) in a party of 1 on January 22 from 12:00 AM until 7:00 PM.
※ You will only be acknowledged as participating in the solo first clear mission if you enter as a party of 1 after January 22 at 7:00 PM.
※ The Solo First Clear mission rewards can be claimed after the February 12, 2026 maintenance 1 time per Nexon ID, and can only be claimed on the character that completed the mission.
※ If your game usage is restricted due to violations of the operating policy during the event period, you may be excluded from receiving the First Clear mission rewards.

Azmoth Canyon related achievements will be moved to the Memories tab and can no longer be completed.
You can now purchase Azmoth Canyon’s Monster Egg from the Monster Park entry map’s general merchant Raku to register Azmoth Canyon related monsters in Monster Collection.

items: You can buy Azmoth Canyon’s Monster Eggs for 150 Monster Park Coins each.
You can buy up to 5 per week per world.
The Monster Collection rates when using the Azmoth Canyon’s Monster Egg are as follows.

details.value: Your existing Azmoth Coins will be maintained. You can exchange them until June 30, 2026 at 11:59 PM by talking to the NPC Owl in the Azmoth Border or the Maple Admin NPC in major towns.
[Back to Overview]
Daily Gift
Starting from February 2026, the Daily Gift’s completion rewards will be changed as follows.

items: Day 5/10/19/24: 1 3x Experience Coupon (30 Minutes)
Day 9/23: 10 Special Medals of Honor
Day 14/21: 1 Karma Black Cube
Day 18: 10 Karma Eternal Rebirth Flames
Day 25: 10 Karma Black Rebirth Flames
Day 28: 1 Karma White Additional Cube

details.value: ※ The additional reward ratio based on MVP rank will be applied the same as it currently is.
[Back to Overview]
Cash
The Sol Erda (5) product will be sold.

items: Sale Period: January 15, 2026 after the maintenance ~ February 11 at 11:59 PM
Price: 10,000 NX

details.value: ※ You can buy this product up to 4 times per Maple ID during the sale period. It can only be purchased with NX.
※ Only level 260 or higher characters that have completed the 6th job advancement can use this product.
※ You cannot use this product if you are already holding 20 Sol Erda.
The Gender Change Coupon will be sold.

items: Sale Period: January 15, 2026 after the maintenance ~ always
Sale Location: Cash Shop > Beauty > Etc.
Price: 15,000 NX
When you use a Gender Change Coupon, you must first unequip all gender-restricted equipment and cosmetic items.
When you use a Gender Change Coupon, your character’s current hair and face will be reset. You can choose one of the available hair and face options from character creation.
A feature has been added where if you try to change to a different gendered hair or face stored in the Beauty Room, you can consume a Freestyle Coupon to change it to a unisex hair or face.

details.value: ※ The Gender Change Coupon cannot be used on Mikhail, Angelic Buster, Zero, or married characters.
※ Demon Slayer and Demon Avenger characters must complete the 4th job advancement before they can use this item.
※ You can only use the Gender Change Coupon in towns.
※ You cannot use Mileage when buying a Gender Change Coupon. If you buy it with NX, you can trade it 1 time.
※ The Gender Change Coupon will expire 30 days after purchase.
Gender Change Coupon related achievements have been added.

items: From Today, I’m a Male Character
From Today, I’m a Female Character
An Oldie but a Goodie

details.value: The MapleStory Boss Collection products have been updated.

items: Sale Duration: January 15, 2026 after the maintenance ~ March 18 at 11:59 PM
The ‘Radiant Malefic Star Package (M/F)’ will be sold.
The ‘Radiant Malefic Star Hair/Face’ have been added to the Boss Hair/Face Coupon lists.

details.value: The maximum number of hair and face slots in the Beauty Room have been increased from 42 to 48.
Non-Zero jobs will now display their gender in the hair/face Beauty Rooms.
A feature has been added where if you search for a hair or face in the Cash Shop, you can go directly to the sale page.
New products have been added to the Mileage Shop.

items: 1 Karma Black Cube Voucher: 7 day expiry, 1500 Mileage, can be purchased 7 times per month per Nexon ID, the item from the Voucher will expire after 7 days.
1 Karma White Additional Cube Voucher: 7 day expiry, 3000 Mileage, can be purchased 7 times per month per Nexon ID, the item from the Voucher will expire after 7 days.

details.value: The Maple Gold Coin Voucher sold in the Mileage Shop will be discontinued.
※ The Mileage Shop changes will be applied on February 1, 2026 at 6:00 AM.
When using the House Wedding Ticket for a wedding, the ‘Wedding Fanfare’ and ‘Special Wedding Fanfare’ buff effects will no longer be provided.
[Back to Overview]
Improvements
A Buff Manager hotkey has been added.
The Buff Favourites tab and feature have been added to the Buff Manager.

items: You can add a buff to your favourites by clicking the star button in the top right corner of the icon.
You can view your favourited buffs in the Favourites tab at the top of the Buff Manager.

details.value: You can now enter the Maple Auction from boss entry maps.
Certain job selection UIs have been changed.
In the Maple Auction, you can now double click items to register them for sale.
The Quest Postbox UI will now display gray text for boss pre-requisite quests above the recommended levels for bosses you can already enter.
The Soul Collection’s monster illustration quality has been improved.
Fixed an issue where the notification text when acquiring Soul Shards was awkward. When you acquire or use Soul Shards, the results will now be displayed in the chat window.
The Event List will now remember the open/closed event folder status when it was last closed.
When checking custom illustration request results, you can now provide feedback on the results. The feedback will be used to improve future models.
Certain custom illustration system related UI have been improved.
[Back to Overview]
Error Fixes

items: Fixed an issue where certain boss monsters sometimes did not perform their death animations when defeated.
Fixed an issue where sometimes boss monsters would move awkwardly.
Alleviated an issue where sometimes the connection to the game server would disconnect during boss content.

details.value: Kai (Normal/Hard)

items: Fixed an issue if you exited, specific sounds would play repeatedly.
Fixed an issue certain skills would be activated in the opposite direction of the character after using Chrono Step.
Fixed an issue where sometimes pattern sounds would stop playing during map pattern activations.
Fixed an issue where sometimes Chrono Break could not be used in the time distortion.

details.value: Limbo (Normal/Hard)

items: Fixed an issue where rewards could not be acquired properly in certain situations if you defeated Limbo in Phase 3 at the same time as the Thought Frenzy pattern.

details.value: [Back to Overview]

items: Fixed an issue in Epic Dungeon: Angler Company stage 4 where characters could go outside of the map when using Teleport.

details.value: [Back to Overview]

items: Fixed an issue in the Character Creation UI where if you returned to the job select UI, certain UI would not be displayed.
Fixed an issue in the minimap’s NPC list where if you clicked certain NPCs, the arrow pointing to the selected NPC would be displayed awkwardly.
Fixed an issue where if you used the item link and emoticons at the same time, the chat window’s emoticon would be displayed awkwardly.
Fixed an issue in the V Matrix where unopened Crest of the Solar/Spider in Mirror cores’ descriptions were displayed awkwardly.
Fixed an issue in the V Matrix where the Ethereal Form core diescription displayed the pre-improved effect.
Fixed an issue where if you had an expired Special Core equipped and extended it, it would not activate even if you met the conditions.
Fixed an issue in boss content where if you had an Illusion Ring equipped, your character’s silhouette’s location would be displayed incorrectly.
Fixed an issue where the NPC Chat/Harvest key did not work while using the Chew Chew Island area’s Symbol Quick Pass.
Fixed an issue in the equipment window where the character’s display location was awkward.
Fixed an issue where Lie Detectors could not be removed while the Special Core expiration notification was activated.

Fixed an issue where if you used the Soul Collection to craft Souls while the Soul Bag’s auto store option was off and you did not have available inventory space, an abnormal crafting failure would occur.

Fixed an issue in Foggy Forest Training Centre where if you applied the Champion’s Protection through the training centre only buff feature then used the cooldown reset feature, the Champion’s Protection would be reset.
Fixed an issue where the Arcs recommended level was displayed incorrectly when using the Monster Park Quick Pass.

Fixed an issue where the loading screen would be displayed repeatedly if you clicked the Fast Login button consecutively on an account with the ‘skip entering 2nd password on a designated PC’ setting enabled.
Fixed an issue where Angelic Buster’s Dress Up Mode’s applied custom illustration would be removed when you relogged in or changed characters.
Fixed an issue where if you closed the window quickly when changing your current illustration, certain UI elements would close too late and look awkward.
Fixed an issue where the Colouring Prism/Colouring Prism Pro’s colour palette were not applied correctly for certain items.

details.value: [Back to Overview]

items: Fixed an issue where the Soul Shard Combination number achievement was not displayed properly on accounts with characters that crafted Souls through the Soul Collection.

Fixed an issue where if the first soul acquired through Soul crafting was a Magnificent Soul, only the ‘[Soul] Magnificent Soul Maker’ achievement would be completed and not the ‘[Soul] Soul Maker’ achievement.

details.value: [Back to Overview]
Events
Your chance to request multiple custom illustrations! Try to create your own custom illustrations with Illustration Request Tickets, available for purchase weekly.
Event Participation Requirements:

items: Level 10 or higher character.

details.value: Event Period:

items: January 15, 2026 after the maintenance ~ March 18, 2026 at 11:59 PM.

details.value: You can select the ‘Weekly Illustration Request Ticket Sale’ event in the Event List‘s Coin Shop menu or talk to the NPC Moving Easel in major towns to access the custom illustration shop.
You can use mesos to purchase Illustration Request Tickets for 300m mesos each.

※ The Illustration Request Ticket purchase limit is per Maple ID. It will reset every Thursday at 12:00 AM. Please understand that right after reset, custom illustration requests may surge, resulting in delays or temporary unavailability.
[Back to Overview]
Event Participation Requirements:

items: Level 101 or higher characters created in Challengers, Challengers 2, Challengers 3, or Challengers 4 worlds.

details.value: Event Period:

items: January 15, 2026 after the maintenance ~ February 11, 2026 at 11:59 PM.

details.value: During the event period, you can leap your characters to your desired world through the NPC Isaga in Henesys. You can select the ‘Challengers World Early Leap’ event in the Event List and click the ‘Participate’ button to move to Henesys.
※ Worlds You can Leap To: All worlds.
※ You can only move to the first world you select through the Challengers World Early Leap and End Leap.
※ You can move a total of 5 characters to other worlds through the Challengers World Early Leap and End Leap.

items: Dim Illusion Memories, Bright Illusion Memories, Complete Illusion Memories, Challengers Coins, Advanced Challengers Coins, and Premium Coins acquired in Challengers World will not be moved with the World Leap.
Challengers World Bronze/Silver/Gold/Platinum rewards that can be acquired from Challengers World missions can only be claimed in Challengers World.
Rewards that can be acquired from the Challengers Pass can only be claimed in Challengers World.
The Challengers Pass: Special Support buff will be removed.
The Challengers EXP Duo’s designated Challengers World character will be removed and you will no longer acquire Duo Points until the end of the usage period.

The Tallahart Fantasia’s Illusion Magic Enhancement’s levels will not be moved. Your acquired Memory Traces and Reminiscense Traces will be maintained, so after the World Leap, you can set the levels again your destination world.

If you World Leap without receiving Hyper Burning MAX level achievement rewards, you will receive them based on the world you move to. Please claim the enhanced level achievement rewards in Challengers World before you leap.
The Mystic Seal event’s equipped Seal Core stats will be removed.
Characters with active Meso Market transactions cannot proceed with World Leap.
When World Leaping from Challengers World, items in the cash storage, storage, and Maple Auction will not be moved.
Custom Backgrounds acquired using Voucher items will not be moved.

details.value: ※ Custom Backgrounds acquired using Voucher items can be extracted into Vouchers through the NPC Isaga’s ‘Extract Custom Background’ option. The acquired Voucher can only be put into storage and is permanent.

items: If you leap without checking the results of a custom illustration request, the request will be cancelled. Please confirm the request before leaping.
The Tera Burning effect applied when a character is created in Challengers World will not be transferred through World Leap.

details.value: ※ The Tera Burning effect will be removed when the character is transferred, and the ‘Tera Burning Gift Giveaway!’ quest will no longer be available.

items: The Strange Cube, Karma Meister Cube, Karma Strange Additional Cube, and Karma Star Force 17 Star Enhancement Scroll (Level 160) purchased from the Challengers Shop are Challengers World-only items and you cannot World Leap while holding them.
The Selective Challengers Level 4 Special Skill Ring Voucher and the Special Skill Ring acquired from using it are Challengers World-only items and you cannot World Leap while holding them.
The Unstable Time Fragments are exclusive to Challengers World and you cannot leap while you have them in your inventory.
If a character that has cleared an Epic Dungeon uses World Leap, youc annot claim the cleared Epic Dungeon’s bonus rewards.
If there are no empty character slots in the world you are moving to, slots will be added before the Leap.
However, if you already have 50 characters in the destination world, you cannot Leap to that world.

details.value: [Back to Overview]
The appearance of new Express Boosters! Complete the daily attendance check and grow even faster!
Event Participation Requirements:

items: A level 260 or higher character (or a Zero character that has completed story quest Chapter 2).

details.value: Event Period:

items: January 15, 2026 after the maintenance ~ February 11, 2026 at 11:59 PM.

details.value: You can select the ‘Burning Express’ event in the Event List and click the ‘Participate’ button to start the event.
Daily Attendance Check
Once per day, you can defeat 1,000 monsters within your level range to complete the attendance check and receive rewards.

You can press the [Use] button at the bottom left of the Burning Express UI to use the Attendance Check Pass to complete previously missed attendance checks. You can use it for the number of participations you missed as of the previous day. Each Pass costs 3,000 Maple Points.
※ The participation records are shared between all characters within the Maple ID.
※ The monsters defeated record is shared between all characters within the Maple ID and is reset every day at 12:00 AM.
※ The event will only progress if you defeat monsters within your level range while you are in a party of 1 or not in a party.
※ The number of monsters within your level range defeated will not increase in the cross world boss content <Dimensional Battlefield>.
Base Attendance Check Rewards
You can acquire the following rewards based on your number of attendance check completions.
※ All reward items can be claimed until February 11, 2026 at 11:59 PM and will expire on February 12, 2026 at 2:00 AM.
※ The Advanced EXP Vouchers and Express Boosters can only be used by level 260 or higher characters. They can only be used after completing the ‘Another Power, Authentic Force’ quest.
Express Pass Attendance Check Rewards
You can buy the Express Pass in the Cash Shop for 30,000 NX. When you use it, you can acquire the following rewards based on your number of completed attendance checks.
However, even if you buy the Express Pass, you cannot acquire the rewards for attendance checks you have not completed.
※ All reward items can be claimed until February 11, 2026 at 11:59 PM and will expire on February 12, 2026 at 2:00 AM.
※ The Express Boosters can only be used by level 260 or higher characters. They can only be used after completing the ‘Another Power, Authentic Force’ quest.
Express Boosters
You can use the Express Booster in a map with monsters within your level range to summon Growing Flame monsters that give a large amount of experience for 100 seconds.
Express Boosters do not receive any additional experience effects.
You can also purchase Express Boosters in the <Night of Falling Illusions> meso shop.

items: Express Booster (30m mesos) [limit of 10 per week per world]

details.value: ※ Express Boosters can only be used in Authentic Force maps.
※ Using an Express Booster in a map with level 295 or higher monsters will grant the same experience as level 294 monsters.
[Back to Overview]
Cassandra’s support is speeding up! Level up the Momentum Pass and receive rewards!
Event Participation Requirements:

items: A level 280 or higher character (or a Zero character that has completed story quest Chapter 2).

details.value: Event Period:

items: January 15, 2026 after the maintenance ~ February 11, 2026 at 11:59 PM.

details.value: You can select the ‘Momentum Pass’ event in the Event List and click the ‘Participate’ button to start the event.
※ You can only participate in this event on one character per person.
※ The character and Maple ID participating in this event cannot be changed afterwards.
※ Even if you delete the character that participates in the Momentum Pass event, you cannot participate in the event on another character.
Momentum Pass Levels and Weekly Missions
The Momentum Pass levels range from level 0~10. You can reach the next level by acquiring 720 Momentum Pass Points.
You can acquire Momentum Pass Points by completing weekly missions.
You can acquire up to 2,000 Momentum Pass Points per week.
When you have acquired all of the Momentum Pass Points you can for thet week, all remaining uncompleted missions will be completed.
※ The missions can only be progressed on the character that is participating in the event.
※ The weekly Momentum Pass point acquisition and weekly mission progress are reset every Thursday at midnight.
※ Symbol daily quests, Monster Park, Monster Park Extreme, and Epic Dungeon missions completed before participating in the event are not included in the weekly mission progress.
You can buy Momentum Pass Points based on the amount of Points you did not acquire from the previous week.
※ 100 Momentum Pass Points costs 1,000 Maple Points.
Pass Level Achievement Rewards
If you buy the Prime Momentum Pass, you can claim the Prime Momentum Rewards.
※ All rewards can be claimed during the event period until February 11, 2026 at 11:59 PM.
※ All rewards will expire on February 12, 2026 at 2:00 AM.
※ All rewards except for the VIP Sauna Entry Tickets are untradeable.
※ The VIP Sauna Entry Tickets can only be put into storage.
Prime Momentum Pass
The Prime Momentum Pass will be available for purchase.

items: Sale Period: January 15, 2026 after the maintenance ~ February 11, 2026 at 11:00 PM
Price: 49,800 NX (cannot be purchased with Maple Points or Mileage)
When you use the ‘Prime Momentum Pass’, you can claim the Prime Momentum rewards.

details.value: ※ The ‘Prime Momentum Pass’ cannot be refunded after purchase.
※ The ‘Prime Momentum Pass’ will expire on February 11, 2026 at 11:59 PM.
※ Regardless of the purchase date, the Prime Pass effects will be applied until February 11, 2026 at 11:59 PM.
※ The ‘Prime Momentum Pass’ can only be purchased 1 time per person.
※ You cannot purchase the ‘Prime Momentum Pass’ if you have no characters participating in the event.
[Back to Overview]

details.value: You can now equip Cash effect items and Finish Attack effect items at the same time.

kmsBreakdown.fullText: The second patch of the MapleStory Crown update has been released! It contains the new 6th job common core, Sol Hecate, the new Astra subweapon system, and a new boss, Radiant Malefic Star! There were also some skill balance adjustments and other game changes/QoL. Overview HEXA Skill – Common Core Skill Changes Common Hero Paladin Dark Knight Archmage (F/P) Archmage (I/L) Bishop Bowmaster Marksman Night Lord Shadower Dual Blade Viper Captain Cannon Shooter Mikhail Soul Master Flame Wizard Wind Breaker Night Walker Striker Aran Evan Luminous Mercedes Phantom Eunwol Demon Slayer Blaster Wild Hunter Xenon Mechanic Kaiser Cadena Angelic Buster Zero Kinesis Adele Illium Khali Ark Len Lara Hoyoung Astra Subweapon Astra Subweapon Transfer Rules Per Class New Boss: Radiant Malefic Star Information [Boss Clear Event] Call of Deception Epic Dungeon Azmoth Canyon Daily Gift Cash Improvements Items UI Error Fixes Items Monsters Maps UI Events Maple Home Achievements Events Weekly Illustration Request Ticket Sale Challengers World 1st Early Leap Burning Express Momentum Pass [Boss Clear] Kai First Clear Event Rewards Sunday Maple HEXA Skill – Common Core A new common core that is shared between all classes has been added. Sol Hecate A new skill that collects Sol Erda, drawn in by the Adversary’s resolve, to form a familiar. Sol Hecate will grow as its level increases. When it reaches certain levels, additional exclusive skills will be activated. Sol Hecate: Styx A new skill that releases a wave of densely concentrated Sol Erda to lead enemies to their death. Sol Hecate: Charon A new skill that protects the contract holder from their fate of death when hit by a fatal attack. Sol Hecate: Phlegethon A new skill that disrupts the order of the Sol Erda that forms the world to temporarily break the barrier between the living and the underworld. Sol Hecate: Pactum A new skill that aligns Sol Hecate’s will with the Adversary, controlling how Sol Hecate’s exclusive skills manifest. Sol Hecate, Sol Hecate: Styx, and Sol Hecate: Phlegethon will have different damage values for each class. Note: I won’t be listing every single class’ damage values since that’s crazy. If you want to see them all, you can check Namu Wiki. In summary, Sol Hecate is a skill that grows as you level it up. Level 1 Sol Hecate – Automatically tracks and attacks enemies. Sol Hecate: Styx – Attacks and binds enemies for 4 seconds with 1 minute cooldown. This bind stacks with other bind durations (i.e. if you use it immediately after Erda Bind, the enemy will be bound for 10+4 = 14 seconds.) You can use Sol Hecate: Pactum to link this with one of your other skills (e.g. AB link) to have Sol Hecate automatically use Styx when that skill is used. Otherwise, you can cast Styx manually by hotkeying it separately. Level 20 Sol Hecate: Charon – If you are hit by an attack that would kill you, it will be ignored and you will be invincible for 10 seconds with a 10 minute cooldown. You can use Sol Hecate: Pactum to set a hotkey to end the invincibility effect early. Level 30 Sol Hecate: Phlegeton – Attacks enemies with a big burst of damage with a 2 minute cooldown. You can link this with another skill just like Styx or use it manually. [Back to Overview] Skill Details Sol Hecate[Master Level: 30]The Sol Erda drawn in by the Adversary’s resolve is collected to form a familiar. Sol Hecate reacts to the Adversary’s will to automatically attack enemies and use its special skills.Sol Hecate will grow based on its level, and when it reaches certain levels, it will activate additional special skills that can be used.You can set which skills Sol Hecate will react to and use its special skills through Sol Hecate: Pactum. Sol Hecate will prioritize attacking the boss monster with the highest max HP. If it hits an enemy using Damage Reflect, you will not take damage.On/Off skill.Sol Hecate Translucency Applicaton On/Off: Right-click.Level 10: 20% defense ignore, 20% boss damage.Level 20: Activate the special skill Charon, 20% defense ignore, 20% boss damage.Level 30: Activate the special skill Phlegeton. [Level 30]Consumes 100 MP. Summon Sol Hecate.Sol Hecate will automatically track enemies and periodically create 4 high-density Sol Erda Particles.Sol Erda Particle: Deals X% damage X times. Sol Hecate: Styx[Master Level: 30]Release a wave of densely concentrated Sol Erda that leads enemies to their deaths. Enemies caught in the wave will be bound and the Seeds of Death will be planted on them.The Seeds of Death will grow within them for a certain period of time to attack continuously, then take their lives when they fully bloom.You can set how the skill manifests with Sol Hecate: Pactum. [Level 30]Consumes 50 MP. Deals X% damage X times on up to 15 enemies.Enemies hit are bound for 4 seconds.Enemies hit will be inflicted with the Seeds of Death debuff for 3 seconds.Styx’s bind stacks with other binds and is applied additionally. After the bind is applied, there is a bind resistance of 47 seconds.Seeds of Death: When growing, deals X% damage X times. When they fully bloom, activate 7 life harvests that deal X% damage X times.Cooldown: 60 seconds. Sol Hecate: Charon[Master Level: 30]Protect the contract holder from their fate of death when hit by a fatal attack.You can set the skill end method with Sol Hecate: Pactum. [Level 30]If your HP reaches 0, this skill will activate and ignore the death. Afterwards, you are invincible for 10 seconds.You can end the invincibility by using the command set through Sol Hecate: Pactum.Reactivation cooldown: 600 seconds. Sol Hecate: Phlegethon[Master Level: 30]Disrupt the order of the Sol Erda that forms the world to temporarily break the barrier between the world of the living and the underworld.The underworld’s torrents manifested in reality incinerate enemies. After the rift closes, the remaining unstable energy blooms in all directions to turn everything into nothingness. You can set how the skill manifests with Sol Hecate: Pactum.Illustration Cutscene On/Off: Right-click. [Level 30]Consumes 50 MP. Activate 8 torrents that deal X% damage X times on up to 15 enemies.After the rift closes, activate 10 full blooms that deal X% damage X times.Cooldown: 120 seconds. Sol Hecate: Pactum[Master Level: 1]Align Sol Hecate’s will with the Adversary to set how Sol Hecate’s special skills are manifested. [Level 1]Set the skills that Styx and Phlegethon react to.Set the command that you can cancel Charon’s invincibility effect with. The Astra Sacred Aegis and Astra Topaz Aegis cannot be equipped with two-handed weapons. You can acquire the Astra subweapon after completing quests to collect Fierce Battle Traces and Erion’s Fragments. There are three stages of the Astra subweapon, with the following requirements. 1st Mission: 600 Fierce Battle Traces and 3,000 Erion’s Fragments 2nd Mission: 600 Fierce Battle Traces and 3,000 Erion’s Fragments 3rd Mission: 800 Fierce Battle Traces and 4,000 Erion’s Fragments You can acquire Fierce Battle Traces by defeating Grandis area bosses at Chosen Seren (Normal) and above. You will acquire the Fragments based on the highest level area daily quest you have completed that day. If you receive the rewards from a lower level area’s daily quest then complete a higher level area’s daily quest, you will receive Erion’s Fragments equal to the difference in the reward amount between those two areas. Astra subweapons all have the following properties. Untradeable. Level 200 or higher characters can equip them. All Astra subweapons can be enhanced up to 15/20/30 stars based on the quest progress (except for Astra Blades). Astra Blades can be enhanced up to 30 stars regardless of the quest progress, but they have 3/6/10 scroll enhancement slots based on the quest progress. All Astra subweapons’ Star Force enhancement stat boosts are the same as level 200 armor (except for Astra Blades). Astra Blades’ Star Force enhancement stat boosts apply the same rules as Arcane Shade Blades. If an Astra subweapon is destroyed through Star Force enhancements, you can purchase a replacement for 1b mesos. If you have completed all of the Astra subweapon quests, you can reset the quests through Nineheart in Ereb to do them again. You can enhance the Astra Sacred Aegis and Astra Topaz Aegis using a special scroll. Astra Aegis Only Attack Scroll: 100% success rate, STR +30, DEX +10, Attack +10 You can buy the Astra Aegis Only Attack Scroll for 500m mesos each. You can use up to 5. You can enhance the Astra Arcane Shield, Astra Ruby Shield, Astra Prominent Shield, and Astra Umbral Shield using a special scroll. Astra Shield Only Magic Attack Scroll: 100% success rate, INT +30, LUK +10, Magic Attack +10 You can buy the Astra Shield Only Magic Attack Scroll for 500m mesos each. You can use up to 5. You can enhance the Astra Bane Shield using a special scroll. Astra Bane Shield Only Attack Scroll: 100% success rate, LUK +30, DEX +10, Attack +10 You can buy the Astra Bane Shield Only Attack Scroll for 500m mesos each. You can use up to 5. You can enhance the Astra Blade using a special scroll. Astra Blade Only Attack Scroll: 100% success rate, LUK +15, DEX +15, Attack +9 You can buy the Astra Shield Only Attack Scroll for 1b mesos each. You can use up to 10. ※ You can buy the Astra Subweapon Only Scrolls through the NPC Erion. If Demon Slayers and Demon Avengers equip an Astra Force Shield that had the Ruin Force Shield transferred to it, they can use the Astra Infusion skill. Astra Infusion is an on/off skill that increases final damage by 10% and damage taken by 8% (for Demon Slayers)/25% (for Demon Avengers). For Zero characters: If a Zero character equips an Astra Hourglass, their subweapon slot’s Great Sword/Long Sword’s damage/boss damage/defense ignore boosts from potential/additional potential/base stats/bonus stats will not be applied. They will be replaced with the Astra Hourglass’ stats. ※ If you unequip the Astra Hourglass, the subweapon slot’s Great Sword/Long Sword’s stats will be applied again. Developer Notes: Unlike existing subweapons that had varying utility across jobs, we aimed to ensure that the Astra subweapons offered as similar utility as possible. To achieve this, we decided to adjust subweapons’ utility in cases where they differed from other jobs. First, for Archmage (F/P), Archmage (I/L), Bishop, Flame Wizard, Evan, Battle Mage, Shadower, and Heroes/Paladins/Soul Masters that chose one-handed Genesis weapons, you can acquire additional stats equivalent to a 20 star Deimos Shield through special scroll enhancements. For Dual Blades, since they have already Star Forced their subweapons, you can transfer Star Force enhancement levels by paying the difference in the costs of destruction recovery. In addition, you can acquire stats that other classes gain from Star Force enhancements on their Astra subweapons through special scroll enhancements. For Demon Slayers and Demon Avengers, if you equip an Astra Force Shield that had the Ruin Force Shield transferred to it, you can use the ‘Astra Infusion’ on/off skill that increases final damage and damage taken, the same as a Ruin Force Shield. For Zero, depending on if you were in Alpha/Beta, the subweapon’s base stats/bonus stats’ damage/boss damage/defense ignore effects and the potential/additional potential’s effects would be applied. However, if Zero equips the Astra subweapon, they will no longer acquire those stats from potential/additional potential or base stats/bonus stats. The Astra subweapon’s base stats will be increased equal to those values. [Back to Overview] Astra Subweapon Transfer Rules Per Class Common You can transfer your subweapon’s potential and additional potential to the Astra subweapon by paying 1b mesos. You can also transfer again to an Astra subweapon that has already been transferred to or enhanced. When you transfer a subweapon, the Star Force enhancements will be reset except for certain shields and Blades. Items with the Mark of Binding applied cannot be transferred. Developer Notes: The list of transferrable items is limited to the ‘subweapons for which top-tier items are difficult to identify clearly and exceptional shield items’. For example, for Dual Blades, the Arcane Shade Blade is a completely superior item to the Absolabs Blade in terms of stats, and their tradeability is the same, so only Arcane Shade Blade transfer is supported. In comparison, for Archmage (F/P), the Red Green Book <Final> is inferior to the Deimos Sage Shield in terms of stats but they are both valuable options due to the difference in tradeability, so both items transfer is supported. Demon Avenger’s Extreme Force Shield and Ruin Force Shield both support transfer for the same reasons. However, the Astra Infusion skill can only be used if you transfer a Ruin Force Shield. For Zero, transfer is not supported as they did not previously have an enhanceable subweapon. Heroes, Paladins, and Soul Masters that have a Genesis Saber/Axe/Hammer or Destiny Saber/Axe/Hammer For these jobs, only the characters that have chosen the one-handed Genesis weapon or Destiny weapon can acquire the Astra Sacred Aegis or Astra Topaz Aegis. For regular subweapons, Fearless Kite Shield, Blue Hoplon, Violet Hoplon, Green Hoplon, Blue Dragon Shield, and the Timeless Kite Shield, you can transfer the potential and additional potential to the Astra Sacred Aegis or Astra Topaz Aegis for 1b mesos, the same as regular jobs. When transferring the Deimos Warrior Shield, the item’s Star Force enhancement levels will be transferred to the Astra Sacred Aegis or Astra Topaz Aegis, but there will be a different transfer cost applied. Developer Notes: When using shields that have already been Star Force enhanced, their Star Force enhancement stages will be transferred. However, an additional cost will be applied to account for the difference in cost due to level limit and cost of destruction recovery. In addition, shields can have Miraculous Equipment Enhancement Scrolls applied to them, which makes those shields have higher value. Items with MEE scrolls applied are harder to determine scroll enhancement changes, and they can also be mixed with regular Equipment Enhancement Scrolls which can lead to significant differences in stats and value even with the same number of stars. Therefore, the Star Force adjustments for these items will be based on a new standard called ‘item score’. ‘Item score’ is calculated by excluding the effects acquired from potential/additional potential, assigning 1 point per main stat and 3 points per attack/magic attack, then adding them together. Items that exceed the minimum item score will have their Star Force upgraded to a level appropriate to their value when transferred. Items with insufficient item scores will not receive Star Force adjustments. As of now, only 10 out of 276 items will not receive adjustments. The majority of equipment will be adjusted as intended. The transfer rules related to MEE Scrolls will only apply to the current end-game equipment (Deimos Sage Shield, Deimos Darkness Shield, Fearless Prelude, Fearless Wrist). Deimos Warrior Shield and Fearless Kite Shield, which are not considered end-game equipment and have relatively low value are not included in the Star Force adjustments, and they will be transferred with the same rules as regular subweapons. When transferring the Arcane Shade Blade, the item’s Star Force enhancement levels will be transferred to the Astra Blade, but there will be a different transfer cost applied. [Back to Overview] Information Pristine, Tender Illusion Unveiled Deformity Rest of the Broken Truth Radiant Malefic Star can only be entered in a party of 1~3 players. Normal Mode: Level: 280 HP: 4.698Q Authentic Force: 400 Power Crystal Price: 658m Hard Mode: Level: 280 HP: 14.74Q Authentic Force: 550 Power Crystal Price: 2.819b Radiant Malefic Star is similar to Jin Hilla in that while it only has one form, as its HP is lowered, it unlocks more attacks. In addition, there are two ‘dimensions’ you can move between, illusion and reality, each with different attacks and mechanics. Phase 1: 100%~80% HP Phase 2: 40%~79% HP Phase 3: 0%~39% HP When you enter the boss, in the preparation room, there will be a special altar that you can press Harvest/NPC Chat on. Only one person in the party can do so (in a solo, it will always be yourself), and the person that does will become Awakened, allowing them to swap between illusion/reality in the boss and interact with the Illusion’s Essence cores that spawn in the fight (will explain a little more later). The preparation room also has the Unfinished Journal NPC which you can talk to to read the thoughts of an unlucky sailor that previously faced off against Radiant Malefic Star. It serves as a quick guide to the boss’ mechanics. The boss has a regular Death Count system (5 per person) but also the Predation Value. If this value reaches 1000, you will instantly fail the boss regardless of your remaining Death Count. The value will increase from being hit by certain attacks and can be decreased by interacting with some of the mechanics. The timer underneath is the countdown to when the boss will forcefully swap you between illusion and reality. You will begin the fight in the illusion map. The main mechanic of this boss is the Illusion Magic/Illusion Detection gauge. The gold circle is Illusion Magic, and it starts from 1000. The higher the Illusion Magic gauge, the boss’ attacks will be stronger and additional patterns will be unlocked. The center orb is the Illusion Detection gauge, and it can go up to 1000. If you hover over the Illusion Detection, you can see the boss’ Illusion Magic cooldown. When you are hit by the boss’ attacks, the Illusion Detection cooldown will go up, delaying it. If the cooldown reaches 0, the boss will cast a special attack that creates an Illusion’s Essence. The awakened party member can interact with it to reduce the Illusion Magic gauge by 200. The awakened party member can also press Harvest/NPC Chat to use the Illusion Projection/Reality Alert skill, which lets you freely swap between illusion and reality. In the reality map, the boss will have more attacks and will attack more frequently. However, based on your Illusion Magic gauge, you can increase your final damage by up to 30% at the cost of making the boss stronger. In the reality map, when the boss casts a special attack that creates an Illusion’s Essence, you can choose to interact with one of two cores. The left core will decrease the Illusion Magic gauge (lowering your final damage) while the right core will increase the gauge (increasing your final damage) Therefore, Radiant Malefic Star is a boss that is focused on risk/reward. You can choose to stay in the illusion map as long as possible for fairly simple boss patterns with no increased damage, or stay in the reality map to increase your final damage at the cost of making the boss stronger. Of course, there’s some more complexity but I didn’t want to write a full guide. You can read more details on the Namu Wiki page. You can use mesos to purchase Illustration Request Tickets for 300m mesos each. ItemQuantityTradeabilityDurationIllustration Request Ticket7Can only be put into storage.Expires after 30 days. ※ The Illustration Request Ticket purchase limit is per Maple ID. It will reset every Thursday at 12:00 AM. Please understand that right after reset, custom illustration requests may surge, resulting in delays or temporary unavailability. [Back to Overview] Challengers World 1st Early Leap Event Participation Requirements: Level 101 or higher characters created in Challengers, Challengers 2, Challengers 3, or Challengers 4 worlds. Event Period: January 15, 2026 after the maintenance ~ February 11, 2026 at 11:59 PM. During the event period, you can leap your characters to your desired world through the NPC Isaga in Henesys. You can select the ‘Challengers World Early Leap’ event in the Event List and click the ‘Participate’ button to move to Henesys. ※ Worlds You can Leap To: All worlds. ※ You can only move to the first world you select through the Challengers World Early Leap and End Leap. ※ You can move a total of 5 characters to other worlds through the Challengers World Early Leap and End Leap. Dim Illusion Memories, Bright Illusion Memories, Complete Illusion Memories, Challengers Coins, Advanced Challengers Coins, and Premium Coins acquired in Challengers World will not be moved with the World Leap. Challengers World Bronze/Silver/Gold/Platinum rewards that can be acquired from Challengers World missions can only be claimed in Challengers World. Rewards that can be acquired from the Challengers Pass can only be claimed in Challengers World. The Challengers Pass: Special Support buff will be removed. The Challengers EXP Duo’s designated Challengers World character will be removed and you will no longer acquire Duo Points until the end of the usage period. The Tallahart Fantasia’s Illusion Magic Enhancement’s levels will not be moved. Your acquired Memory Traces and Reminiscense Traces will be maintained, so after the World Leap, you can set the levels again your destination world. If you World Leap without receiving Hyper Burning MAX level achievement rewards, you will receive them based on the world you move to. Please claim the enhanced level achievement rewards in Challengers World before you leap. The Mystic Seal event’s equipped Seal Core stats will be removed. Characters with active Meso Market transactions cannot proceed with World Leap. When World Leaping from Challengers World, items in the cash storage, storage, and Maple Auction will not be moved. Custom Backgrounds acquired using Voucher items will not be moved. ※ Custom Backgrounds acquired using Voucher items can be extracted into Vouchers through the NPC Isaga’s ‘Extract Custom Background’ option. The acquired Voucher can only be put into storage and is permanent. If you leap without checking the results of a custom illustration request, the request will be cancelled. Please confirm the request before leaping. The Tera Burning effect applied when a character is created in Challengers World will not be transferred through World Leap. ※ The Tera Burning effect will be removed when the character is transferred, and the ‘Tera Burning Gift Giveaway!’ quest will no longer be available. The Strange Cube, Karma Meister Cube, Karma Strange Additional Cube, and Karma Star Force 17 Star Enhancement Scroll (Level 160) purchased from the Challengers Shop are Challengers World-only items and you cannot World Leap while holding them. The Selective Challengers Level 4 Special Skill Ring Voucher and the Special Skill Ring acquired from using it are Challengers World-only items and you cannot World Leap while holding them. The Unstable Time Fragments are exclusive to Challengers World and you cannot leap while you have them in your inventory. If a character that has cleared an Epic Dungeon uses World Leap, youc annot claim the cleared Epic Dungeon’s bonus rewards. If there are no empty character slots in the world you are moving to, slots will be added before the Leap. However, if you already have 50 characters in the destination world, you cannot Leap to that world. [Back to Overview] Burning Express The appearance of new Express Boosters! Complete the daily attendance check and grow even faster! Event Participation Requirements: A level 260 or higher character (or a Zero character that has completed story quest Chapter 2). Event Period: January 15, 2026 after the maintenance ~ February 11, 2026 at 11:59 PM. You can select the ‘Burning Express’ event in the Event List and click the ‘Participate’ button to start the event. Daily Attendance Check Once per day, you can defeat 1,000 monsters within your level range to complete the attendance check and receive rewards. You can press the [Use] button at the bottom left of the Burning Express UI to use the Attendance Check Pass to complete previously missed attendance checks. You can use it for the number of participations you missed as of the previous day. Each Pass costs 3,000 Maple Points. ※ The participation records are shared between all characters within the Maple ID. ※ The monsters defeated record is shared between all characters within the Maple ID and is reset every day at 12:00 AM. ※ The event will only progress if you defeat monsters within your level range while you are in a party of 1 or not in a party. ※ The number of monsters within your level range defeated will not increase in the cross world boss content <Dimensional Battlefield>. Base Attendance Check Rewards You can acquire the following rewards based on your number of attendance check completions. Number RewardQuantityTradeability1Express Booster5Can only be put into storage.2Advanced EXP Voucher10003Express Booster54Express Booster55Advanced EXP Voucher10006Express Booster57Advanced EXP Voucher10008Express Booster59Advanced EXP Voucher100010Express Booster511Express Booster512Advanced EXP Voucher100013Express Booster514Advanced EXP Voucher100015Express Booster516Advanced EXP Voucher100017Express Booster518Express Booster519Advanced EXP Voucher100020Express Booster521Advanced EXP Voucher1000 ※ All reward items can be claimed until February 11, 2026 at 11:59 PM and will expire on February 12, 2026 at 2:00 AM. ※ The Advanced EXP Vouchers and Express Boosters can only be used by level 260 or higher characters. They can only be used after completing the ‘Another Power, Authentic Force’ quest. Express Pass Attendance Check Rewards You can buy the Express Pass in the Cash Shop for 30,000 NX. When you use it, you can acquire the following rewards based on your number of completed attendance checks. However, even if you buy the Express Pass, you cannot acquire the rewards for attendance checks you have not completed. NumberRewardQuantityTradeability1Express Booster5Can only be put into storage.2535410556575859510511101251351451551651751810195205215 ※ All reward items can be claimed until February 11, 2026 at 11:59 PM and will expire on February 12, 2026 at 2:00 AM. ※ The Express Boosters can only be used by level 260 or higher characters. They can only be used after completing the ‘Another Power, Authentic Force’ quest. Express Boosters You can use the Express Booster in a map with monsters within your level range to summon Growing Flame monsters that give a large amount of experience for 100 seconds. Express Boosters do not receive any additional experience effects. You can also purchase Express Boosters in the <Night of Falling Illusions> meso shop. Express Booster (30m mesos) [limit of 10 per week per world] ※ Express Boosters can only be used in Authentic Force maps. ※ Using an Express Booster in a map with level 295 or higher monsters will grant the same experience as level 294 monsters. [Back to Overview] Momentum Pass Cassandra’s support is speeding up! Level up the Momentum Pass and receive rewards! Event Participation Requirements: A level 280 or higher character (or a Zero character that has completed story quest Chapter 2). Event Period: January 15, 2026 after the maintenance ~ February 11, 2026 at 11:59 PM. You can select the ‘Momentum Pass’ event in the Event List and click the ‘Participate’ button to start the event. ※ You can only participate in this event on one character per person. ※ The character and Maple ID participating in this event cannot be changed afterwards. ※ Even if you delete the character that participates in the Momentum Pass event, you cannot participate in the event on another character. Momentum Pass Levels and Weekly Missions The Momentum Pass levels range from level 0~10. You can reach the next level by acquiring 720 Momentum Pass Points. You can acquire Momentum Pass Points by completing weekly missions. Weekly MissionChallengers Pass Points Complete Grandis daily quests 3 times.100Complete Grandis daily quests 6 times.100Complete Grandis daily quests 9 times.100Complete Grandis daily quests 12 times.100Complete Grandis daily quests 15 times.100Complete Monster Park 2 times.100Complete Monster Park 4 times.100Complete Monster Park 6 times.100Complete Monster Park 8 times.100Complete Monster Park 10 times.100Complete Monster Park 12 times.100Complete Monster Park Extreme 1 time.200Complete an Epic Dungeon 1 time.200Defeat 10,000 monsters within your level range.200Defeat 20,000 monsters within your level range.200Defeat 30,000 monsters within your level range.200Defeat 40,000 monsters within your level range.200Defeat 50,000 monsters within your level range.200Defeat 60,000 monsters within your level range.200Defeat 70,000 monsters within your level range.200Defeat 80,000 monsters within your level range.200Defeat 90,000 monsters within your level range.200Defeat 100,000 monsters within your level range.200 You can acquire up to 2,000 Momentum Pass Points per week. When you have acquired all of the Momentum Pass Points you can for thet week, all remaining uncompleted missions will be completed. ※ The missions can only be progressed on the character that is participating in the event. ※ The weekly Momentum Pass point acquisition and weekly mission progress are reset every Thursday at midnight. ※ Symbol daily quests, Monster Park, Monster Park Extreme, and Epic Dungeon missions completed before participating in the event are not included in the weekly mission progress. You can buy Momentum Pass Points based on the amount of Points you did not acquire from the previous week. ※ 100 Momentum Pass Points costs 1,000 Maple Points. Pass Level Achievement Rewards If you buy the Prime Momentum Pass, you can claim the Prime Momentum Rewards. Pass LevelMomentum RewardPrime Momentum Reward1Mechaberry Farm Entry Ticket2 4x Experience Coupons (30 Minutes)2VIP Sauna Entry TicketMechaberry Farm Entry Ticket31 Sol Erda3,000 Advanced EXP Vouchers4100 Advanced EXP Vouchers2 4x Experience Coupons (30 Minutes)5VIP Sauna Entry Ticket2 Mechaberry Farm Entry Tickets61 Sol Erda3,000 Advanced EXP Vouchers7100 Advanced EXP Vouchers2 4x Experience Coupons (30 Minutes)8VIP Sauna Entry Ticket3 Mechaberry Farm Entry Tickets91 Sol Erda3,000 Advanced EXP Vouchers10100 Advanced EXP Vouchers4 Mechaberry Farm Entry Tickets ※ All rewards can be claimed during the event period until February 11, 2026 at 11:59 PM. ※ All rewards will expire on February 12, 2026 at 2:00 AM. ※ All rewards except for the VIP Sauna Entry Tickets are untradeable. ※ The VIP Sauna Entry Tickets can only be put into storage. Prime Momentum Pass The Prime Momentum Pass will be available for purchase. Sale Period: January 15, 2026 after the maintenance ~ February 11, 2026 at 11:00 PM Price: 49,800 NX (cannot be purchased with Maple Points or Mileage) When you use the ‘Prime Momentum Pass’, you can claim the Prime Momentum rewards. ※ The ‘Prime Momentum Pass’ cannot be refunded after purchase. ※ The ‘Prime Momentum Pass’ will expire on February 11, 2026 at 11:59 PM. ※ Regardless of the purchase date, the Prime Pass effects will be applied until February 11, 2026 at 11:59 PM. ※ The ‘Prime Momentum Pass’ can only be purchased 1 time per person. ※ You cannot purchase the ‘Prime Momentum Pass’ if you have no characters participating in the event. [Back to Overview] [Boss Clear] Kai First Clear Event Rewards Gifts for the first 10 players that defeated Hard Kai in Challengers World! receive the first clear mission rewards! Event Participation Requirements: Level 101 or higher characters that have completed the ‘[Kai First Clear Event] First Clear Mission’ within the Nexon ID. Event Period: January 15, 2026 after the maintenance ~ January 21, 2026 at 11:59 PM. You can select the ‘Kai First Clear Mission Rewards’ event in the Event List and click the ‘Participate’ button to receive the following rewards. They can be claimed in regular worlds too. ※ The rewards can only be claimed on the 1st Maple ID that claims a reward. [Back to Overview] Sunday Maple Event Period: January 18, 2025 January 25, 2025 February 1, 2026 February 8, 2026 On January 18, the following benefits will take place: Shining Star Force Time 30% discount on Star Force enhancement costs 30% reduction on destruction rate when Star Forcing at 21 stars or lower On January 25, the following benefits will take place: Sol Erda Time For every 2 minutes you are logged in, accumulate 1 Sol Erda Fragment Voucher (up to 90 vouchers). When you have been logged in for a total of 2 hours, receive an additional 10 Sol Erda Fragment Vouchers. When you have been logged in for a total of 3 hours, receive 1 Sol Erda. 3x Sol Erda acquired through hunting. items.title: KMS ver. 1.2.410 – MapleStory Crown: Kinesis Remaster!
items.summary: The first patch of the MapleStory Crown update has been released! The main features include the Kinesis remaster, tons various game improvements and qualit...

kmsBreakdown.summary: The first patch of the MapleStory Crown update has been released! The main features include the Kinesis remaster, tons various game improvements and quality of life changes, and Challenge World season 3. This time, there’s an extra Challenge World-exclusive season t, Kai!
highlights: Sorry for the post being late, this patch came out a week and a half ago but since there were only a few days inbetween the KMST and KMS release, I didn’t have enough time to do a KMST post (as I mentioned before) so I had to translate everything all at once (and there was a lot in these patch notes). Then I was busy playing the actual game and other assorted holiday stuff lol.
Wishing everyone a Merry Christmas, happy holidays, and happy new year!
Kinesis Remaster Common Quests Skills Beginner 1st Job 2nd Job 3rd Job 4th Job Hyper Skills 5th Job 6th Job V Core Changes HEXA Core Changes Character Settings Change Support
Psychic Points have been removed.
[Back to Overview]
The ‘People in Need of a Hero’ quests have been streamlined into a single location in Riena Strait.
