sections.title: [Halloween Event] Night of the Yetis

details.value: A hunt between monster Yetis and hunters! Survive the spooky night in El Nath and acquire various rewards!
Event Participation Requirements:

items: Level 101 or higher characters (Zero characters that have completed Chapter 2).

details.value: Event Period:

items: October 23, 2025 after the maintenance ~ November 5, 2025 at 11:59 PM.

details.value: ※ During the event period, Henesys, Nameless Town, Cernium Square, and City of Researchers will be decorated for Halloween.
You can select ‘[Halloween Event] Night of the Yetis’ in the Event List and click the ‘Participate’ button to enter the <Monster Yeti Hunter Temporary Outpost>.
You can participate in <Night of the Yetis> through Quick Start or Party Play by talking to Scadur.
If you choose Quick Start, you can choose your desired role between hunter and Yeti to start matching.
※ If you choose a role and matching is not complete within 30 seconds, you may be matched as the other role.
※ You can proceed in Quick Start without a party, and you will play with 4 other players that applied through Quick Start.
If you enter as a party, the party leader will always be the hunter.
※ You can proceed in Party Play in a party of 2-5 players.
※ Hunting Points and number of wins will not be accumulated in Party Play.
※ The Monster Yeti Hunter Temporary Outpost is a cross-world waiting room, so you can form parties with players from other worlds.
Night of the Yetis

items: Night of the Yetis is a survival game where the roles of surviving and hunting are reversed based on the time of day.
5 players, matched cross-world, are assigned the roles of 1 hunter and 4 monster Yetis.
You can use WASD to move, SPACE to jump, and the mouse/keyboard to use skills.
The game is divided into daytime and nighttime, and will begin in daytime.
Night Energy: The game begins at 0 Night Energy. When it reaches 300, it will become nighttime.

details.value: ※ Night Energy will increase periodically.
※ The hunter cannot use skills during nighttime.
※ At nighttime, the monster Yetis transform into giant Yetis, and their skills will change.
※ When the hunter uses the Shoot skill, it will increase if they miss a monster Yeti and decrease if they successfully hunt a monster Yeti.

items: The hunter can win by finding and hunting all of the monster Yetis that are mimicking the Dark Junior Yetis during the daytime, or by reaching the hideout during the nighttime.
The monster Yetis can win by surviving until nighttime and either hunting the hunter or if the hunter fails to reach the hideout within the time limit.

details.value: ※ At nighttime, the hunter’s hideout located furthest from the hunter’s current location will be activated. The hunter can win if they reach the hideout within the time limit.
Monster Yetis

items: The monster Yetis can win by surviving until nighttime and either hunting the hunter or if the hunter fails to reach the hideout within the time limit.

The monster Yetis can mimic Dark Junior Yetis by using the Head Scratch and Hide Legs skills. Head Scratch: This skill mimics the Dark Junior Yeti’s actions. Hide Legs: This skill hides the monster Yeti’s legs. While they are hidden, your speed and jump are decreased. You can use the skill again to unhide your legs. If your Hunger reaches 100, your legs will be revealed.
Head Scratch: This skill mimics the Dark Junior Yeti’s actions.

Hide Legs: This skill hides the monster Yeti’s legs. While they are hidden, your speed and jump are decreased. You can use the skill again to unhide your legs. If your Hunger reaches 100, your legs will be revealed.

details.value: ※ Head Scratch can only be used while your legs are hidden.
※ Hide Legs can only be used when you have 1 or higher hunger.
※ The usable skills will change based on the monster Yeti’s legs.

items: Hunger: You start at 100 hunger, and it decreases over time. If your hunger reaches 0, the monster Yeti’s legs will be revealed and you cannot use the Hide Legs skill.
Eat Food: When used near food, you can eet it to replenish your hunger.
Dash: This skill lets you jump a set distance forward.

details.value: ※ When you use Eat Food, your current location will be briefly displayed to the hunter.
※ Dash can only be used when your legs are not hidden.
Giant Monster Yetis

items: The monster Yetis will transform into Giant Monster Yetis when it is nighttime. Their usable skills will change.
You can win by either hunting the hunter or if the hunter fails to reach the hideout within the time limit.

details.value: Hunter

items: The hunter can win by finding and hunting all of the monster Yetis that are mimicking the Dark Junior Yetis during the daytime, or by reaching the hideout during the nighttime.
The hunter can use their Shoot and Surveillance Camera skills to find and hunt the monster Yetis.

details.value: ※ For the Shoot skills, you can aim with the mouse cursor and attack the targeted location with the left mouse button.

items: The Surveillance Camera skill briefly reveals the locations of monster Yetis that pass by their installed range without their legs hidden.

details.value: ※ You can install up to 5 surveillance cameras per game. After installing them, you cannot retrieve them.
※ Once a surveillance camera is installed, it will remain for the rest of the daytime.
Rewards
If you win a game, you will receive 500 Hunting Points and accumulate your number of wins. If you lose, you will receive 250 Hunting Points.

※ If you don’t play for a certain period of time or are the first person to leave an in-progress game, you will receive a penalty preventing you from being able to participate in matchmaking for a set period of time.
You can acquire up to 1,000 Hunting Points per day and up to 3,000 Hunting Points per week.
※ The weekly cap will reset every Thursday at midnight.
You can acquire various rewards based on your accumulated Hunting Points and number of wins.
※ The event progress is shared between all characters within the maple ID.
※ The Monster Yetiroid Voucher includes a Lidium Heart.
※ The items acquired from the Selective Hallowcat’s Hat Voucher, Selective Magic Academy Purple School Uniform Box, and Selective Mystic Rose Outfit Box are permanent and untradeable items.
※ The Lidium Heart from the Monster Yetiroid Voucher is permanent and untradeable, the Monster Yetiroid is permanent and can only be put into storage.
※ The Riding acquired from the Giant Monster Yeti Riding Voucher is permanent and can only be used on the character that you use it on.
※ All rewards can only be claimed once per Maple ID.
※ All rewards can only be claimed during the event period.
※ All rewards can be claimed during the event period by selecting the ‘Night of the Yetis’ in the Event List and clicking the ‘<Night of the Yetis> Receive Rewards’ option.
[Back to Overview]

sections.summary: A hunt between monster Yetis and hunters! Survive the spooky night in El Nath and acquire various rewards!

details.value: Event Participation Requirements:

items: Level 130 or higher characters created in Challengers, Challengers 2, Challengers 3, or Challengers 4 worlds.

details.value: ※ Zero characters that are level 130 or higher and have completed story quest Chapter 2 can Early Leap.
Event Period:

items: October 23, 2025 after the maintenance ~ November 19, 2025 at 11:59 PM.

details.value: During the event period, you can leap your characters to your desired world through the NPC Isaga in Henesys. You can select the ‘Challengers World Early Leap’ event in the Event List and click the ‘Participate’ button to move to Henesys.
※ Worlds You can Leap To: All worlds.
※ You can move a total of 5 characters to other worlds through the Challengers World Early Leap and End Leap.

items: Basic Hideout Tokens, Intermediate Hideout Tokens, Advanced Hideout Tokens, Challengers Coins, and Advanced Challengers Coins acquired in Challengers World will not be moved with the World Leap.
Challengers World Bronze/Silver/Gold/Platinum rewards that can be acquired from Challengers World missions can only be claimed in Challengers World.
The Mystic Seal event’s equipped Seal Core stats will be removed.
Maple Hideout Special Menu’s skill levels will be applied based on the world you arrive in.

If a character designated as the Manager or Crew in Hideout Duo leaps to a world that wasn’t selected, the character designation information will be removed. In addition, Hideout Duo events cannot be progressed in that world.
However, you can continue the event by re-designating a character in the selected world or the world where the character was created.
Characters with active Meso Market transactions cannot proceed with World Leap.
When World Leaping from Challengers World, items in the cash storage, storage, and Maple Auction will not be moved.
Custom Backgrounds acquired using Voucher items will not be moved.

details.value: ※ Custom Backgrounds acquired using Voucher items can be extracted into Vouchers through the NPC Isaga’s ‘Extract Custom Background’ option. The acquired Voucher can only be put into storage and is permanent.

items: The Tera Burning effect applied when a character is created in Challengers World will not be transferred through World Leap.

details.value: ※ The Tera Burning effect will be removed when the character is transferred, and the ‘Tera Burning Gift Giveaway!’ quest will no longer be available.

items: The Strange Cube, Karma Meister Cube, Karma Strange Additional Cube, and Karma Star Force 17 Star Enhancement Scroll (Level 160) purchased from the Challengers Shop are Challengers World-only items and you cannot World Leap while holding them.
The Selective Challengers Level 4 Special Skill Ring Voucher and the Special Skill Ring acquired from using it are Challengers World-only items and you cannot World Leap while holding them.
If a character that has cleared an Epic Dungeon uses World Leap, you cannot claim the cleared Epic Dungeon’s bonus rewards.
If there are no empty character slots in the world you are moving to, slots will be added before the Leap.
However, if you already have 50 characters in the destination world, you cannot Leap to that world.

details.value: [Back to Overview]
World Archive
A new expedition has begun in the Dimensional Library. Help Thales investigate monsters and NPCs around the world to complete the encyclopedia.
The <World Archive> has been added.

items: The World Archive is an account-based collection system, collected on a per-Maple ID basis.
You can add information on Maple World’s monsters and NPCs to the encyclopedia. Defeating monsters or talking to NPCs will automatically register their information in the World Archive.

details.value: ※ Grandis and Arcane River areas will be added in a future update.
How to Use the System

You can begin the World Archive by accepting the ‘[Dimensional Library] World Archive Project 1, 2, 3’ quest. When you complete the related quests, you will be able to access the World Archive UI through the full menu.
※ Characters level 100 or higher that have completed the ‘[Dimensional Library] Please Visit the Dimensional Library’ quest can do this through the NPC Librarian Thales.
※ The quest progress is shared between all worlds, and you can only complete it 1 time per Maple ID.
You can check your encyclopedia collection status and search for each area’s monsters and NPC information through the World Archive UI.

items: World Select Screen You can select from Maple World, Grandis, Arcane River, or the Appendix. Clicking each button will take you to the corresponding world’s region select screen.
You can select from Maple World, Grandis, Arcane River, or the Appendix.
Clicking each button will take you to the corresponding world’s region select screen.

Region Select Screen A list of regions within the selected world will be displayed. You can check each region’s collection progress. The filter feature lets you search by ‘All’, ‘Incomplete’, or ‘Completed’ status. You can search by region name or collection record name. Clicking each region button will take you to the region’s detailed collection screen.
A list of regions within the selected world will be displayed. You can check each region’s collection progress.
The filter feature lets you search by ‘All’, ‘Incomplete’, or ‘Completed’ status. You can search by region name or collection record name.
Clicking each region button will take you to the region’s detailed collection screen.
Detailed Region Collection Screen You can use the Tab key and arrow keys to switch between the Category and Collection information.
You can use the Tab key and arrow keys to switch between the Category and Collection information.

details.value: Collection Registration

items: Monsters: Registered upon defeating. In parties, you must deal damage at least 1 time for it to be registered. ※ They will not be registered in Practice Mode.
Registered upon defeating. In parties, you must deal damage at least 1 time for it to be registered.
※ They will not be registered in Practice Mode.
NPCs: Registered upon clicking or interacting with the NPC Chat/Harvest key. Certain NPCs offer special hidden research information through additional interactions.
Registered upon clicking or interacting with the NPC Chat/Harvest key. Certain NPCs offer special hidden research information through additional interactions.

details.value: You can click the notification displayed when you register something in the collection to immediately check the collected information.
World Archive Rewards
When you collect a certain number of collection records, you can acquire the following rewards based on the cumulative number of registered records.
※ The rewards can only be received 1 time per Maple ID, regardless of world.
The <Special Appendix> has been added.

items: This will be unlocked automatically when you unlock the <World Archive>.
This allows you to check your known settings.

details.value: ※ Sections other than Maple World will be added in future updates.
[Back to Overview]
Improvements

items: The effect displayed when Resistance classes job advance has been changed.
Certain Resistance-only quests’ job conditions have been changed to other jobs.
Certain Edelstein quests’ headers have been changed to [Black Wings].
The ‘Variety Hair Changer’ quest description has been changed.

details.value: [Back to Overview]
The Potential Stamp system has been removed.

items: All items that have potential or additional potential granted to them (except for certain cases with fixed potential or additional potential) will now have 3 lines by default.
Existing items with 2 lines of potential or additional potential will become 3 lines when they are reset.
When you use Potential Stamps, you will now acquire Power Elixirs based on the success rate (100% success rate will give 100 Power Elixirs).
Potential Stamp items will no longer drop from Elite Monsters.
Potential Stamp items can no longer be acquired from the ‘Rare Treasure Boxes’ dropped by Elite Bosses.
Potential Stamp items can no longer be acquired from various Exploration Boxes.
Potential Stamp items can no longer be acquired by trading in various Cube Fragments.
Silver Additional Potential Stamps can no longer be acquired from Gold Apples.
Gold Potential Stamps have been removed from the Union Coin Shop.
Gold Potential Stamps and Special Additional Potential Stamps have been removed from the Hideout Coin Shop and Challengers Shop.

details.value: Some changes have been made to the Enhancement UI.

items: When the Enhancement UI is opened, it will now open in the location where it was previously closed.
When the Enhancement UI is opened, it will no longer adjust the locations and status of the Equip UI and Inventory UI.
The left tab size has been decreased.
The held mesos display method has been abbreviated. You can hover over the heading area to check more accurately.
The Star Catcher and destruction prevention options selection method when Star Force enhancing have been changed from a checkbox to a switch.
When a scroll that randomly increases values succeeds, the increased values will now be displayed temporarily during the enhancement success cutscene.
The default display option for bonus stat values has been changed to disable the detailed information.
When resetting bonus stats, potential, or additional potential, you can now preview the results at the start of the enhancement success cutscene.
A cube-only inventory has been added when resetting potential and additional potential.
The notification formats when resetting potential and additional potential have been unified.
The ‘Special Skill Ring Polishing Level Exchange’ title has been changed to ‘Ring Polishing Level Exchange’.

details.value: [Back to Overview]

It has been improved so that if an item with the Binding Mark applied has Epic additional potential and you try to reset it, a pop-up will appear to inform you that the tier up rate and guaranteed tier up count will not be applied.
A Quick Move shortcut key has been added. The Quick Move button’s size has been made slightly larger.

If the Maple Guide minimized UI is closed and you log out and log back in, the minimized Maple Guide UI will no longer be displayed automatically. However, if you can start or are in progress of a main quest or job advancement quest, the minimized Maple Guide UI will be displayed when you log in.
The Profession Skill UI’s craft rate text has been hcanged to acquisition rate upon success.
You can no longer use Sequences while Violetta’s Lie Detector is activating.
You can now click the Delivery Arrived notification in the Unified Notifications window to open the delivery claim UI.
In the Maple Auction, if an item has had its appearance changed with an Anvil, an Anvil icon will now be displayed on the item.
A Close button has been added to the Hideout Stamp UI. You can activate the Hideout Stamp UI again by logging into the game or by talking to Hoon.
In the Return Scroll stat selection UI, a notification has been added for when no stat changes occur.

It has been improved so that when you use the VIP Sauna then exit on a Red PC environment, the UI that displays the usage time and experience gained will now display a notification that the experience acquired was reduced due to the Red PC environment.
In the Character Info window’s item drop rate tooltip, the description related to certain penalties has been improved.
[Back to Overview]
The guide displayed after using the Golden Strawberry Farm has been improved.
Hideout Duo

items: When exchanging Managing Points on characters that haven’t completed the 6th job advancement, you can now only exchange for experience.
If you remove a Manager designation, you can now use the Hideout Break Room again after registering a Manager.

details.value: [Back to Overview]
The Character Info/Union Info/Guild Info searches have been improved to make it easier to check each detailed search and the item’s search availability date.
The Character Info search has been improved so that the Ring Exchange Skill registered equipment search now includes the registered equipment’s description and image.
[Back to Overview]
Error Fixes

items: Fixed an error where certain skills’ hit effects were affected by the skill effect transparency option.

details.value: [Back to Overview]

items: Fixed an error where the ‘[Ghost Park] Korean Folk Town Ghost Park Opening!’ quest incorrectly stated that you could travel to Ghost Park through the Dimensional Mirror.
Fixed an issue where the ‘Variety Hair Changer’ quest progress would not update when changing hairs with certain hair coupons.
Fixed an error where certain characters could not play ‘Maple Chronicle: Odium’.
Fixed an issue with the ‘[Lacheln] Dreaming’ quest where pets appeared awkwardly during the cutscene.

details.value: [Back to Overview]

items: Fixed an issue where the Ring of Sum’s usable skills were displayed awkwardly.
Fixed an issue where equipment provided by default when creating a character had the incorrect number of scroll upgrades.
Fixed an issue where Superior Protect Shields could not be used on Superior equipment items.
Fixed an issue where stat increases from equipment growth levels sometimes did not affect Star Force stats.
Fixed an issue where titles with moving images would sometimes get darkened when moving.
Fixed an issue where cash weapon effects were not displayed for specific jobs’ actions.
Fixed an issue where you could not use a space between characters when using the Pet Name Tag or Android Name Tag Coupon.

details.value: [Back to Overview]

items: Fixed an issue in the Medal UI where the scrollable area was awkward.
Fixed an issue in the Character Select window where certain weapon effects were displayed awkwardly behind the character’s class insignia.

Fixed an issue in the Character Select window’s Job Growth Status UI where if you had multiple characters of the same job, the level of the character would be displayed as the level of the character that was the earliest in the character select window.

Fixed an issue where the private login’s tooltip in the login process incorrectly stated that the most recent login date would be displayed in the Buddy UI even if you logged in privately during server maintenance.
Fixed an issue if you tried to register a skill that can’t be registered in a Buff Sequence, it would display the message ‘This skill cannot be registered in a Skill Sequence’.
Fixed an issue where if you selected Hyper skills that can’t be registered in Buff Favourites or Sequences, the skill icons would look awkward.
Fixed an error where in certain situations, the hit effect transparency option was incorrectly applied as the skill effect transparency option value.
You can no longer use the Android shop if you don’t have a Machine Heart equipped.
Fixed an issue where sometimes the UI would look awkward when playing the Epic Dungeon Nightmare Paradise on an Ark.
Fixed an issue where coins you held looked awkward in the Traveling Salesman shop UI.
Fixed an issue where guild member ranks would change in bulk if a guild member’s account was deleted.
Fixed an error where if you acquired a new item identical to a currently equipped decorative item, the new item effect would not be displayed in the inventory.
Fixed an issue where sometimes kicking a party member would cause the client to close.
Fixed an issue where sometimes deleting a friend would cause the client to close.
Fixed an error where sometimes the previous week’s guild hunting point or boss point records were not reset.
Fixed an issue where sometimes closing certain confirmation windows would cause the client to close.
Fixed an issue where if you were using Smart AI Correction and changed maps, the screen would look awkward for a short time.
Fixed an issue where if you registered a navigation route then logged into another character, the destination registered on the previous character would be set as the navigation destination.
Fixed an error where in certain situations, the VIP Sauna related UI appeared in the Maple Auction preview window.
Fixed an issue in the Equip UI’s pet tab where if the auto pot usage option was set to 9% or 0%, sometimes it would not be saved.
Fixed an issue where sometimes resizing the window with the mouse was awkward.
Fixed an issue in the VIP Sauna room where if you acquired experience, the VIP Sauna UI’s achievable level would flicker.
Fixed an issue where if you opened the VIP Sauna UI in specific situations, the client would freeze.
Fixed an issue where characters would not blink in the Cash Shop preview window and the Maple Auction preview window.
Fixed an issue where the Hideout Stamp UI was not visible in the Kritias map.
Fixed an issue where you could perform other actions while enhancing items in the Enhancement UI.
Fixed an issue where a guide for random portals that can no longer be used appeared when you reached a certain level.
Fixed an issue with the Boss UI’s weekly defeat limit where if you hovered over it, the tooltip was sometimes displayed behind the UI.
Fixed an issue where after going to the login, Cash Shop, or Maple Auction then returned to your map and the resolution changed, the UI locations would be set incorrectly.

details.value: [Back to Overview]
Cassandra has arrived after hearing the news about the Wild Hunter remaster! Participate in the SAVAGE attendance check and receive gifts!
Event Participation Requirements:

items: All Wild Hunter characters.

details.value: Event Period:

items: October 23, 2025 after the maintenance ~ November 19, 2025 at 11:59 PM.

details.value: You can select the ‘[Remaster Event] SAVAGE’ in the Event List and click the ‘Participate’ button to start this event.
Once per day, you can defeat 1,000 monsters within your level range on a Wild Hunter character to complete the SAVAGE attendance check and receive rewards.
The SAVAGE attendance check can be completed up to 10 times over the event period.

You can press the <Use> button in the top right corver of the SAVAGE UI to use the <SAVAGE Pass> which completes a SAVAGE check-in that you did not complete previously. It costs 3,000 Maple Points per 1 Pass.
※ The participation record is shared between all characters within the Maple ID.
※ The monsters defeated record is shared between all characters within the Maple ID, and is reset every day at midnight.
※ This event will only progress when a Wild Hunter character that is in a party of 1 or not in a party defeats monsters with their level range.
※ The number of monsters defeated will not increase in the Cross World Boss Content <Dimensional Battlefield>.
SAVAGE Base Reward
During the event period, you can receive a Hyper Burning Booster from the SAVAGE UI.
※ This can only be received by Wild Hunter characters level 258 or lower. You cannot receive if it the Hyper Burning effect is already being applied.
※ The Hyper Burning Booster can be used until November 20, 2025 at 2:00 AM. The applied effects will expire on December 18, 2025 at 2:00 AM.
SAVAGE Attendance Check Rewards
※ The growth/enhancement rewards can only be claimed on a level 260 or higher Wild Hunter character. The decoration rewards can be claimed on all characters.
※ During the event period, you can also buy Petite Asili and Petite Asili’s equipment in the Cash Shop.
[Back to Overview]
Camila has returned with pretty outfits for autumn. Fill Camila’s store with autumn colours and receive pretty gifts!
Event Participation Requirements:

items: Level 101 or higher characters (Zero characters that have completed Chapter 2).

details.value: Event Period:

items: October 23, 2025 after the maintenance ~ November 19, 2025 at 11:59 PM.

details.value: You can select the ‘Camila’s Autumn Coloured Store’ in the Event List and click the ‘Participate’ button to start this event.
Weekly Mission
Every time you defeat 2,000 monsters within your level range, you can click the ‘Fill’ button in the UI to fill the store with autumn colours and accumulate the following rewards.
※ The monsters defeated record is shared between all characters within the Maple ID, and is reset every day at midnight.
※ This event will only progress when a character that is in a party of 1 or not in a party defeats monsters with their level range.
※ The number of monsters defeated will not increase in the Cross World Boss Content <Dimensional Battlefield>.
You can defeat up to 10,000 monsters within your level range per week. If you defeat all 10,000 monsters, you will also accumulate that week’s bonus rewards.
You can claim the accumulated VIP Boosters and VIP Sauna Entry Tickets during the event period by clicking the ‘Receive’ button in the UI.
※ The VIP Booster and VIP Sauna Entry Ticket accumulation and claim status are shared between all characters within the Maple ID. The claimed items can only be put into storage.
※ VIP Sauna Entry Tickets can only be used during the ‘VIP Sauna’ event period. After the event period ends, the effects will no longer apply even if you have remaining Sauna Entry Ticket time.
※ If you have claimed the weekly bonus rewards 3 times or more, you can click the ‘Receive’ button that appears on the right side of the UI to acquire the ‘Autumn Coloured Display Case’.
※ The Autumn Coloured Display Case can be claimed after creating a Maple Home. Upon claiming it, the furniture will be instantly added to your Maple Home.

※ Maple Home requires a level 260 or higher character that has completed ‘The Day After’ story within the world. You can then complete the ‘[Maple Home] Spiegelmann’s Super Gift’ quest through the lightbulb icon to create a Maple Home.
※ Maple Home is shared between all characters within the world. The furniture acquired from the Voucher can be used by all characters within the Maple ID.
Autumn Flower Bookmarks
You can use your accumulated Autumn Flower Bookmarks by clicking the ‘Open Store’ button in the UI or through the ‘Trendy Store’, ‘Steady Store’, and ‘Accessory Store’ located in Henesys.
You can also accumulate additional Autumn Flower Bookmarks by buying Autumn Flower Bookmark Vouchers for 100m mesos each through the ‘Buy’ button in the UI.
※ You can buy up to 4,000 Autumn Flower Bookmark Vouchers per Maple ID. They are untradeable.
※ Unused Autumn Flower Bookmark Vouchers can be resold for 100m mesos each through the Shop UI.

details.value: [Back to Overview]
A hunt between monster Yetis and hunters! Survive the spooky night in El Nath and acquire various rewards!
Event Participation Requirements:

items: Level 101 or higher characters (Zero characters that have completed Chapter 2).

details.value: Event Period:

items: October 23, 2025 after the maintenance ~ November 5, 2025 at 11:59 PM.

details.value: ※ During the event period, Henesys, Nameless Town, Cernium Square, and City of Researchers will be decorated for Halloween.
You can select ‘[Halloween Event] Night of the Yetis’ in the Event List and click the ‘Participate’ button to enter the <Monster Yeti Hunter Temporary Outpost>.
You can participate in <Night of the Yetis> through Quick Start or Party Play by talking to Scadur.
If you choose Quick Start, you can choose your desired role between hunter and Yeti to start matching.
※ If you choose a role and matching is not complete within 30 seconds, you may be matched as the other role.
※ You can proceed in Quick Start without a party, and you will play with 4 other players that applied through Quick Start.
If you enter as a party, the party leader will always be the hunter.
※ You can proceed in Party Play in a party of 2-5 players.
※ Hunting Points and number of wins will not be accumulated in Party Play.
※ The Monster Yeti Hunter Temporary Outpost is a cross-world waiting room, so you can form parties with players from other worlds.
Night of the Yetis

items: Night of the Yetis is a survival game where the roles of surviving and hunting are reversed based on the time of day.
5 players, matched cross-world, are assigned the roles of 1 hunter and 4 monster Yetis.
You can use WASD to move, SPACE to jump, and the mouse/keyboard to use skills.
The game is divided into daytime and nighttime, and will begin in daytime.
Night Energy: The game begins at 0 Night Energy. When it reaches 300, it will become nighttime.

details.value: ※ Night Energy will increase periodically.
※ The hunter cannot use skills during nighttime.
※ At nighttime, the monster Yetis transform into giant Yetis, and their skills will change.
※ When the hunter uses the Shoot skill, it will increase if they miss a monster Yeti and decrease if they successfully hunt a monster Yeti.

items: The hunter can win by finding and hunting all of the monster Yetis that are mimicking the Dark Junior Yetis during the daytime, or by reaching the hideout during the nighttime.
The monster Yetis can win by surviving until nighttime and either hunting the hunter or if the hunter fails to reach the hideout within the time limit.

details.value: ※ At nighttime, the hunter’s hideout located furthest from the hunter’s current location will be activated. The hunter can win if they reach the hideout within the time limit.
Monster Yetis

items: The monster Yetis can win by surviving until nighttime and either hunting the hunter or if the hunter fails to reach the hideout within the time limit.

The monster Yetis can mimic Dark Junior Yetis by using the Head Scratch and Hide Legs skills. Head Scratch: This skill mimics the Dark Junior Yeti’s actions. Hide Legs: This skill hides the monster Yeti’s legs. While they are hidden, your speed and jump are decreased. You can use the skill again to unhide your legs. If your Hunger reaches 100, your legs will be revealed.
Head Scratch: This skill mimics the Dark Junior Yeti’s actions.

Hide Legs: This skill hides the monster Yeti’s legs. While they are hidden, your speed and jump are decreased. You can use the skill again to unhide your legs. If your Hunger reaches 100, your legs will be revealed.

details.value: ※ Head Scratch can only be used while your legs are hidden.
※ Hide Legs can only be used when you have 1 or higher hunger.
※ The usable skills will change based on the monster Yeti’s legs.

items: Hunger: You start at 100 hunger, and it decreases over time. If your hunger reaches 0, the monster Yeti’s legs will be revealed and you cannot use the Hide Legs skill.
Eat Food: When used near food, you can eet it to replenish your hunger.
Dash: This skill lets you jump a set distance forward.

details.value: ※ When you use Eat Food, your current location will be briefly displayed to the hunter.
※ Dash can only be used when your legs are not hidden.
Giant Monster Yetis

items: The monster Yetis will transform into Giant Monster Yetis when it is nighttime. Their usable skills will change.
You can win by either hunting the hunter or if the hunter fails to reach the hideout within the time limit.

details.value: Hunter

items: The hunter can win by finding and hunting all of the monster Yetis that are mimicking the Dark Junior Yetis during the daytime, or by reaching the hideout during the nighttime.
The hunter can use their Shoot and Surveillance Camera skills to find and hunt the monster Yetis.

details.value: ※ For the Shoot skills, you can aim with the mouse cursor and attack the targeted location with the left mouse button.

items: The Surveillance Camera skill briefly reveals the locations of monster Yetis that pass by their installed range without their legs hidden.

details.value: ※ You can install up to 5 surveillance cameras per game. After installing them, you cannot retrieve them.
※ Once a surveillance camera is installed, it will remain for the rest of the daytime.
Rewards
If you win a game, you will receive 500 Hunting Points and accumulate your number of wins. If you lose, you will receive 250 Hunting Points.

※ If you don’t play for a certain period of time or are the first person to leave an in-progress game, you will receive a penalty preventing you from being able to participate in matchmaking for a set period of time.
You can acquire up to 1,000 Hunting Points per day and up to 3,000 Hunting Points per week.
※ The weekly cap will reset every Thursday at midnight.
You can acquire various rewards based on your accumulated Hunting Points and number of wins.
※ The event progress is shared between all characters within the maple ID.
※ The Monster Yetiroid Voucher includes a Lidium Heart.
※ The items acquired from the Selective Hallowcat’s Hat Voucher, Selective Magic Academy Purple School Uniform Box, and Selective Mystic Rose Outfit Box are permanent and untradeable items.
※ The Lidium Heart from the Monster Yetiroid Voucher is permanent and untradeable, the Monster Yetiroid is permanent and can only be put into storage.
※ The Riding acquired from the Giant Monster Yeti Riding Voucher is permanent and can only be used on the character that you use it on.
※ All rewards can only be claimed once per Maple ID.
※ All rewards can only be claimed during the event period.
※ All rewards can be claimed during the event period by selecting the ‘Night of the Yetis’ in the Event List and clicking the ‘<Night of the Yetis> Receive Rewards’ option.
[Back to Overview]
Event Participation Requirements:

items: Level 130 or higher characters created in Challengers, Challengers 2, Challengers 3, or Challengers 4 worlds.

details.value: ※ Zero characters that are level 130 or higher and have completed story quest Chapter 2 can Early Leap.
Event Period:

items: October 23, 2025 after the maintenance ~ November 19, 2025 at 11:59 PM.

details.value: During the event period, you can leap your characters to your desired world through the NPC Isaga in Henesys. You can select the ‘Challengers World Early Leap’ event in the Event List and click the ‘Participate’ button to move to Henesys.
※ Worlds You can Leap To: All worlds.
※ You can move a total of 5 characters to other worlds through the Challengers World Early Leap and End Leap.

items: Basic Hideout Tokens, Intermediate Hideout Tokens, Advanced Hideout Tokens, Challengers Coins, and Advanced Challengers Coins acquired in Challengers World will not be moved with the World Leap.
Challengers World Bronze/Silver/Gold/Platinum rewards that can be acquired from Challengers World missions can only be claimed in Challengers World.
The Mystic Seal event’s equipped Seal Core stats will be removed.
Maple Hideout Special Menu’s skill levels will be applied based on the world you arrive in.

If a character designated as the Manager or Crew in Hideout Duo leaps to a world that wasn’t selected, the character designation information will be removed. In addition, Hideout Duo events cannot be progressed in that world.
However, you can continue the event by re-designating a character in the selected world or the world where the character was created.
Characters with active Meso Market transactions cannot proceed with World Leap.
When World Leaping from Challengers World, items in the cash storage, storage, and Maple Auction will not be moved.
Custom Backgrounds acquired using Voucher items will not be moved.

details.value: ※ Custom Backgrounds acquired using Voucher items can be extracted into Vouchers through the NPC Isaga’s ‘Extract Custom Background’ option. The acquired Voucher can only be put into storage and is permanent.

items: The Tera Burning effect applied when a character is created in Challengers World will not be transferred through World Leap.

details.value: ※ The Tera Burning effect will be removed when the character is transferred, and the ‘Tera Burning Gift Giveaway!’ quest will no longer be available.

items: The Strange Cube, Karma Meister Cube, Karma Strange Additional Cube, and Karma Star Force 17 Star Enhancement Scroll (Level 160) purchased from the Challengers Shop are Challengers World-only items and you cannot World Leap while holding them.
The Selective Challengers Level 4 Special Skill Ring Voucher and the Special Skill Ring acquired from using it are Challengers World-only items and you cannot World Leap while holding them.
If a character that has cleared an Epic Dungeon uses World Leap, you cannot claim the cleared Epic Dungeon’s bonus rewards.
If there are no empty character slots in the world you are moving to, slots will be added before the Leap.
However, if you already have 50 characters in the destination world, you cannot Leap to that world.

kmsBreakdown.fullText: The official server has been updated with the Wild Hunter remaster, as well as skill changes to other jobs, and the new World Archive system! Like I mentioned before, this post was late because I was in LA for MapleCon last week. I think everyone knows how it went lol, kinda crazy how bad it was considering how much more expensive tickets were this year. Hopefully, Nexon can learn from their (many) mistakes and improve next time…? Overview Wild Hunter Remaster Skills Beginner 1st Job 2nd Job 3rd Job 4th Job Hyper Skills 5th Job 6th Job V Core Changes HEXA Core Changes Story Commemorative Gifts Skill Changes Common Adventurer Warrior Hero Paladin Bowmaster Marksman Night Lord Shadower Dual Blade Viper Captain Cannon Shooter Mikhail Soul Master Flame Wizard Wind Breaker Night Walker Evan Mercedes Luminous Phantom Blaster Battle Mage Xenon Mechanic Demon Slayer Demon Avenger Kain Angelic Buster Illium Ark Len Hoyoung V Core Changes Azmoth Canyon Intense Power Crystals Monster Collection World Archive Improvements Quests Enhancement Items Monsters Maps UI Events Login Open API Error Fixes Skills Quests Items NPCs Monsters Maps UI Sounds Cash Open API Events [Remaster Event] SAVAGE Camila’s Autumn Coloured Store [Halloween Event] Night of the Yetis Challengers World 4th Early Leap Sunday Maple Wild Hunter Remaster The Character Creation UI’s illustration has been changed. The Wild Hunter Face has been added. The Nomad Breeze Hair/Nomad Whisper Hair has been added. Skills Beginner Capture This skill has been removed. Hunter’s Call This skill has been removed. Final Damage Increase This skill has been removed. 1st Job Wild Vulcan: Proto[Master Level: 20][Crossbow Skill] Use an improved prototype of a crossbow. The fired arrows pierce through enemies. [Level 20]Consumes 10 MP. Deals 130% damage 3 times on up to 5 enemies. Jaguar Riding[Master Level: 1]By communicating with Asili, you can ride them in combat. While riding, your speed and Stance rate are increased. If you are not riding Asili, you cannot use skills other than movement skills. [Level 1]Consumes 20 MP. Ride Asili.While riding, increases speed by 40 and Stance rate by 50%.Cooldown: 1 second. Savage Leap[Master Level: 5]You can jump once more while jumping to move a short distance. You can use this skill up to 2 times. You can use this skill with the up arrow key to move upwards. [Level 5]Consumes 10 MP. Jump a certain distance. Predator’s Leap[Master Level: 5]Asili marks the nearest enemy with the Mark of Prey. You can use the skill again to teleport to the marked enemy and pounce on them. After teleporting, Predator’s Leap: Running Start will be activated using the power from pouncing on the enemy.If you use this skill with the down arrow key, you can change the enemy marked with the Mark of Prey.In addition, permanently increases speed and jump. [Level 5]Consumes 10 MP. Mark the nearest enemy within a certain range with the Mark of Prey for 600 seconds.If you use the skill again, teleport to the prey’s location.Change mark cooldown: 0.5 seconds.[Passive Effect: 20 speed, 20 jump] Predator’s Leap: Running Start[Master Level: 5]Pounce on your prey and jump once more. This skill is only activated after Predator’s Leap’s teleport, and will be deactivated after being used. This skill shares SP with Predator’s Leap. [Level 5]Consumes 10 MP. Jump a certain distance. Hunter’s Gait[Master Level: 15]Demonstrate a hunter’s nimble yet stealthy movements. [Level 15]35 speed, 20 max speed, 53% defense, 50% Stance rate, 20% damage reduction. Nature’s Wrath[Master Level: 20]Resonate with the wrath of nature and increase your stats. [Level 20]30% critical rate, 30 attack, 20% max HP. [Back to Overview] 2nd Job Wild Vulcan: Ignite[Master Level: 20][Crossbow Skill] Further improve the crossbow. Arrows with ignition devices attached fly a certain distance then explode. [Level 20]Consumes 20 MP. Fire explosing arrows that deal 200% damage 4 times on up to 5 enemies. Assist: Rend Claw[Master Level: 10][Assist Skill] When Crossbow skills hit an enemy, the power of nature flowing through Asili manifests automatically in the form of a Spirit Jaguar.The Spirit Jaguar’s rough claws slash enemies.If this skill hits an enemy using Damage Reflect, you will not take damage. Assist skills are not affected by cooldown reset effects. [Back to Overview] V Core Changes Drill Container Cores have been removed. Existing Enhancement Cores that have Drill Container as a skill will have them replaced with the following skills in the given order. Wild Vulcan: Apex Command: Titan Pounce Command: Alpha Surge Jaguar Soul Cores have been removed. Existing Enhancement Cores that have Jaguar Soul as a skill will have them replaced with the following skills in the given order. Wild Vulcan: Apex Command: Titan Pounce Command: Alpha Surge Flash Rain/Rampage as One Cores have been removed. Existing Enhancement Cores that have Flash Rain/Rampage as One as a skill will have them replaced with the following skills in the given order. Wild Vulcan: Apex Command: Titan Pounce Command: Alpha Surge Double Shot/Triple Shot/Wild Shot Enhancement Cores have been changed into Wild Vulcan: Proto/Wild Vulcan: Ignite/Wild Vulcan: Shatterline Enhancement Cores. Summon Jaguar Enhancement Cores have been changed into Assist: Rend Claw Enhancement Cores. Claw Cut Enhancement Cores have been changed into Assist: Fang Snap Enhancement Cores. Final Attack Enhancement Cores have been changed into Final Attack/Another Bite Enhancement Cores. Cross Road/White Heat Rush Enhancement Cores have been changed into White Heat Rush Enhancement Cores. Assistant Hunting Unit Enhancement Cores have been changed into Assist: Skull Bash Enhancement Cores. Sonic Boom/Jaguar Soul Enhancement Cores have been changed into Trap Seeder/Volt Snare Enhancement Cores. Wild Vulcan/Wild Vulcan Type-P Enhancement Cores have been changed into Wild Vulcan: Apex Enhancement Cores. Drill Container Enhancement Cores have been changed into Command: Alpha Surge Enhancement Cores. Flash Rain/Rampage as One Enhancement Cores have been changed into Command: Titan Pounce Enhancement Cores. HEXA Core Changes The Nature’s Belief Core has been changed into the Linked Impact Core. The Wild Vulcan VI Core has been changed into the Wild Vulcan: Apex VI Core. The Jaguar Skill VI Core has been changed into the Assist VI Core. The Summon Jaguar VI Core has been changed into the Command VI Core. The Final Attack VI/Assistant Hunting Unit VI/Drill Container VI Core has been changed into the Another Bite VI/Final Attack VI/Trap Seeder VI Core. The Jaguar Storm Enhancement Core has been changed into the Command: Predator’s Eye Enhancement Core. Command: Predator’s Eye’s final damage is increased by 60%. The Jaguar Maximum Enhancement Core has been changed into the Overbite Enhancement Core. Overbite’s final damage is increased by 60%. The Wild Grenade Enhancement Core has been changed into the Primal Bloom Enhancement Core. During Primal Bloom, Assist and Command skills’ damage are increased. Assist: Rend Claw VI’s damage is increased by 155%p. Assist: Fang Snap VI’s damage is increased by 180%p. Assist: Skull Bash VI’s damage is increased by 190%p. Command: Alpha Surge VI’s damage is increased by 130%p. Command: Titan Pounce VI’s damage is increased by 95%p. Command: Predator’s Eye’s damage is increased by 80%p. The Wild Vulcan: Type X Enhancement Core has been changed into the Wild Vulcan: Overdrive Enhancement Core. Wild Vulcan: Overdrive’s final damage is increased by 60%. [Back to Overview] Story Wild Hunter, Battle Mage, Mechanic, and Blaster job quests have been changed. Certain quests’ titles, dialogue, and cutscenes have been changed. Illustrations have been added. Certain quests’ start and completion conditions, and rewards have been changed. Resistance-related Chronicles will be suspended temporarily. Certain Wild Hunter, Battle Mage, Mechanic, and Blaster job quests have been removed. [Resistance] Resistance’s Trainee [Resistance] Test Subject’s Recovery [Resistance] Gift for the Test Subject 1 Certain Wild Hunter, Battle Mage, Mechanic, and Blaster job quests have been added. [Back to Overview] Monster Collection New monsters have been added to Monster Collection. Minar Forest Grandis Special New Cube Chairs have been added. Baekyeon Cube Chair First Adversary Cube Chair Baldrix Cube Chair Helena Cube Chair Owl Cube Chair Jade Warmaster Cube Chair A new Monster Collection related achievement has been added. [Monster Collection] Towards the Canyon, Towards the Continent [Back to Overview] World Archive A new expedition has begun in the Dimensional Library. Help Thales investigate monsters and NPCs around the world to complete the encyclopedia. The <World Archive> has been added. The World Archive is an account-based collection system, collected on a per-Maple ID basis. You can add information on Maple World’s monsters and NPCs to the encyclopedia. Defeating monsters or talking to NPCs will automatically register their information in the World Archive. ※ Grandis and Arcane River areas will be added in a future update. How to Use the System You can begin the World Archive by accepting the ‘[Dimensional Library] World Archive Project 1, 2, 3’ quest. When you complete the related quests, you will be able to access the World Archive UI through the full menu. ※ Characters level 100 or higher that have completed the ‘[Dimensional Library] Please Visit the Dimensional Library’ quest can do this through the NPC Librarian Thales. ※ The quest progress is shared between all worlds, and you can only complete it 1 time per Maple ID. You can check your encyclopedia collection status and search for each area’s monsters and NPC information through the World Archive UI. World Select Screen You can select from Maple World, Grandis, Arcane River, or the Appendix. Clicking each button will take you to the corresponding world’s region select screen. Region Select Screen A list of regions within the selected world will be displayed. You can check each region’s collection progress. The filter feature lets you search by ‘All’, ‘Incomplete’, or ‘Completed’ status. You can search by region name or collection record name. Clicking each region button will take you to the region’s detailed collection screen. Detailed Region Collection Screen You can use the Tab key and arrow keys to switch between the Category and Collection information. Collection Registration Monsters: Registered upon defeating. In parties, you must deal damage at least 1 time for it to be registered. ※ They will not be registered in Practice Mode. NPCs: Registered upon clicking or interacting with the NPC Chat/Harvest key. Certain NPCs offer special hidden research information through additional interactions. You can click the notification displayed when you register something in the collection to immediately check the collected information. World Archive Rewards When you collect a certain number of collection records, you can acquire the following rewards based on the cumulative number of registered records. Cumulative RegistrationsRewardDurationTradeability250Appreciator of Knowledge ChairPermanentCan only be put into storage.500Maple World Map Voucher30 Days750Pioneer of Knowledge ChairPermanent1000Maple World Map Custom Background Voucher30 Days1250Character Select Screen Theme Voucher – World Archive30 Days ※ The rewards can only be received 1 time per Maple ID, regardless of world. The <Special Appendix> has been added. This will be unlocked automatically when you unlock the <World Archive>. This allows you to check your known settings. ※ Sections other than Maple World will be added in future updates. [Back to Overview] Improvements Quests The effect displayed when Resistance classes job advance has been changed. Certain Resistance-only quests’ job conditions have been changed to other jobs. Certain Edelstein quests’ headers have been changed to [Black Wings]. The ‘Variety Hair Changer’ quest description has been changed. [Back to Overview] Enhancement The Potential Stamp system has been removed. All items that have potential or additional potential granted to them (except for certain cases with fixed potential or additional potential) will now have 3 lines by default. Existing items with 2 lines of potential or additional potential will become 3 lines when they are reset. When you use Potential Stamps, you will now acquire Power Elixirs based on the success rate (100% success rate will give 100 Power Elixirs). Potential Stamp items will no longer drop from Elite Monsters. Potential Stamp items can no longer be acquired from the ‘Rare Treasure Boxes’ dropped by Elite Bosses. Potential Stamp items can no longer be acquired from various Exploration Boxes. Potential Stamp items can no longer be acquired by trading in various Cube Fragments. Silver Additional Potential Stamps can no longer be acquired from Gold Apples. Gold Potential Stamps have been removed from the Union Coin Shop. Gold Potential Stamps and Special Additional Potential Stamps have been removed from the Hideout Coin Shop and Challengers Shop. Some changes have been made to the Enhancement UI. When the Enhancement UI is opened, it will now open in the location where it was previously closed. When the Enhancement UI is opened, it will no longer adjust the locations and status of the Equip UI and Inventory UI. The left tab size has been decreased. The held mesos display method has been abbreviated. You can hover over the heading area to check more accurately. The Star Catcher and destruction prevention options selection method when Star Force enhancing have been changed from a checkbox to a switch. When a scroll that randomly increases values succeeds, the increased values will now be displayed temporarily during the enhancement success cutscene. The default display option for bonus stat values has been changed to disable the detailed information. When resetting bonus stats, potential, or additional potential, you can now preview the results at the start of the enhancement success cutscene. A cube-only inventory has been added when resetting potential and additional potential. The notification formats when resetting potential and additional potential have been unified. The ‘Special Skill Ring Polishing Level Exchange’ title has been changed to ‘Ring Polishing Level Exchange’. [Back to Overview] Items A new BGM has been added to the Hideout Maplaylist Chair. Play With Me All Day The Ruin Force Shield’s increased damage taken from all attacks (including % HP attacks) for Demon Slayers has been decreased from 15% to 8%. [Back to Overview] UI It has been improved so that if an item with the Binding Mark applied has Epic additional potential and you try to reset it, a pop-up will appear to inform you that the tier up rate and guaranteed tier up count will not be applied. A Quick Move shortcut key has been added. The Quick Move button’s size has been made slightly larger. If the Maple Guide minimized UI is closed and you log out and log back in, the minimized Maple Guide UI will no longer be displayed automatically. However, if you can start or are in progress of a main quest or job advancement quest, the minimized Maple Guide UI will be displayed when you log in. The Profession Skill UI’s craft rate text has been hcanged to acquisition rate upon success. You can no longer use Sequences while Violetta’s Lie Detector is activating. You can now click the Delivery Arrived notification in the Unified Notifications window to open the delivery claim UI. In the Maple Auction, if an item has had its appearance changed with an Anvil, an Anvil icon will now be displayed on the item. A Close button has been added to the Hideout Stamp UI. You can activate the Hideout Stamp UI again by logging into the game or by talking to Hoon. In the Return Scroll stat selection UI, a notification has been added for when no stat changes occur. It has been improved so that when you use the VIP Sauna then exit on a Red PC environment, the UI that displays the usage time and experience gained will now display a notification that the experience acquired was reduced due to the Red PC environment. In the Character Info window’s item drop rate tooltip, the description related to certain penalties has been improved. [Back to Overview] Events The guide displayed after using the Golden Strawberry Farm has been improved. Hideout Duo When exchanging Managing Points on characters that haven’t completed the 6th job advancement, you can now only exchange for experience. If you remove a Manager designation, you can now use the Hideout Break Room again after registering a Manager. [Back to Overview] Login The game data loading speed has been improved so that you can login to the game faster after starting the client. [Back to Overview] Open API The Character Info/Union Info/Guild Info searches have been improved to make it easier to check each detailed search and the item’s search availability date. The Character Info search has been improved so that the Ring Exchange Skill registered equipment search now includes the registered equipment’s description and image. Fixed an issue where the ‘Variety Hair Changer’ quest progress would not update when changing hairs with certain hair coupons. Fixed an error where certain characters could not play ‘Maple Chronicle: Odium’. Fixed an issue with the ‘[Lacheln] Dreaming’ quest where pets appeared awkwardly during the cutscene. Fixed an issue where equipment provided by default when creating a character had the incorrect number of scroll upgrades. Fixed an issue where Superior Protect Shields could not be used on Superior equipment items. Fixed an issue where cash weapon effects were not displayed for specific jobs’ actions. Fixed an issue where you could not use a space between characters when using the Pet Name Tag or Android Name Tag Coupon. [Back to Overview] NPCs Fixed an issue where NPC Moss’ dialogue was awkward. Fixed an issue where if Mechanics were transformed into a different appearance by certain monsters’ patterns, sometimes the client would freeze. Fixed an issue in Epic Dungeon Nightmare Paradise where Contaminated Jagang would sometimes cast the Plague Cloud pattern late when bound. Fixed an issue where sometimes if you were playing on Nexon Link Remote and you reached the daily monster limit, the penalty would not be applied immediately. Limbo Alleviated an issue in Phase 1 Specter B where sometimes it would teleport when casting Nightmare. Alleviated an issue in Phase 1 Specter B where sometimes it would teleport when casting Shadow Hide. Fixed an issue where players who successfully explored Azmoth Canyon but were moved to the Azmoth Boundary without receiving rewards due to things like the client closing would be unable to re-enter Azmoth Canyon. Fixed an issue in Epic Dungeon Nightmare Paradise’s Path to the Temple map where if you fell to the bottom, sometimes you wuold not return to your original location. Fixed an issue in Epic Dungeon Nightmare Paradise’s Burning Tower where if Mechanic used Open Gate: GX-9, the client would crash intermittently. Fixed an issue in the Character Select window where certain weapon effects were displayed awkwardly behind the character’s class insignia. Fixed an issue in the Character Select window’s Job Growth Status UI where if you had multiple characters of the same job, the level of the character would be displayed as the level of the character that was the earliest in the character select window. Fixed an issue where the private login’s tooltip in the login process incorrectly stated that the most recent login date would be displayed in the Buddy UI even if you logged in privately during server maintenance. Fixed an issue if you tried to register a skill that can’t be registered in a Buff Sequence, it would display the message ‘This skill cannot be registered in a Skill Sequence’. Fixed an issue where if you selected Hyper skills that can’t be registered in Buff Favourites or Sequences, the skill icons would look awkward. Fixed an error where in certain situations, the hit effect transparency option was incorrectly applied as the skill effect transparency option value. You can no longer use the Android shop if you don’t have a Machine Heart equipped. Fixed an issue where sometimes the UI would look awkward when playing the Epic Dungeon Nightmare Paradise on an Ark. Fixed an issue where coins you held looked awkward in the Traveling Salesman shop UI. Fixed an issue where guild member ranks would change in bulk if a guild member’s account was deleted. Fixed an error where if you acquired a new item identical to a currently equipped decorative item, the new item effect would not be displayed in the inventory. Fixed an issue where sometimes kicking a party member would cause the client to close. Fixed an issue where sometimes deleting a friend would cause the client to close. Fixed an error where sometimes the previous week’s guild hunting point or boss point records were not reset. Fixed an issue where sometimes closing certain confirmation windows would cause the client to close. Fixed an issue where if you were using Smart AI Correction and changed maps, the screen would look awkward for a short time. Fixed an issue where if you registered a navigation route then logged into another character, the destination registered on the previous character would be set as the navigation destination. Fixed an error where in certain situations, the VIP Sauna related UI appeared in the Maple Auction preview window. Fixed an issue in the Equip UI’s pet tab where if the auto pot usage option was set to 9% or 0%, sometimes it would not be saved. Fixed an issue where sometimes resizing the window with the mouse was awkward. Fixed an issue in the VIP Sauna room where if you acquired experience, the VIP Sauna UI’s achievable level would flicker. Fixed an issue where if you opened the VIP Sauna UI in specific situations, the client would freeze. Fixed an issue with the Boss UI’s weekly defeat limit where if you hovered over it, the tooltip was sometimes displayed behind the UI. Fixed an issue where after going to the login, Cash Shop, or Maple Auction then returned to your map and the resolution changed, the UI locations would be set incorrectly. [Back to Overview] Events [Remaster Event] SAVAGE Cassandra has arrived after hearing the news about the Wild Hunter remaster! Participate in the SAVAGE attendance check and receive gifts! Event Participation Requirements: All Wild Hunter characters. Event Period: October 23, 2025 after the maintenance ~ November 19, 2025 at 11:59 PM. You can select the ‘[Remaster Event] SAVAGE’ in the Event List and click the ‘Participate’ button to start this event. Once per day, you can defeat 1,000 monsters within your level range on a Wild Hunter character to complete the SAVAGE attendance check and receive rewards. The SAVAGE attendance check can be completed up to 10 times over the event period. You can press the <Use> button in the top right corver of the SAVAGE UI to use the <SAVAGE Pass> which completes a SAVAGE check-in that you did not complete previously. It costs 3,000 Maple Points per 1 Pass. ※ The participation record is shared between all characters within the Maple ID. ※ The monsters defeated record is shared between all characters within the Maple ID, and is reset every day at midnight. ※ This event will only progress when a Wild Hunter character that is in a party of 1 or not in a party defeats monsters with their level range. ※ The number of monsters defeated will not increase in the Cross World Boss Content <Dimensional Battlefield>. SAVAGE Base Reward During the event period, you can receive a Hyper Burning Booster from the SAVAGE UI. RewardQuantityTradeabilityHyper Burning Booster1Untradeable ※ This can only be received by Wild Hunter characters level 258 or lower. You cannot receive if it the Hyper Burning effect is already being applied. ※ The Hyper Burning Booster can be used until November 20, 2025 at 2:00 AM. The applied effects will expire on December 18, 2025 at 2:00 AM. [Back to Overview] Camila’s Autumn Coloured Store Camila has returned with pretty outfits for autumn. Fill Camila’s store with autumn colours and receive pretty gifts! Event Participation Requirements: Level 101 or higher characters (Zero characters that have completed Chapter 2). Event Period: October 23, 2025 after the maintenance ~ November 19, 2025 at 11:59 PM. You can select the ‘Camila’s Autumn Coloured Store’ in the Event List and click the ‘Participate’ button to start this event. Weekly Mission Every time you defeat 2,000 monsters within your level range, you can click the ‘Fill’ button in the UI to fill the store with autumn colours and accumulate the following rewards. Fill RewardQuantityVIP Booster1Autumn Flower Bookmark1 ※ The monsters defeated record is shared between all characters within the Maple ID, and is reset every day at midnight. ※ This event will only progress when a character that is in a party of 1 or not in a party defeats monsters with their level range. ※ The number of monsters defeated will not increase in the Cross World Boss Content <Dimensional Battlefield>. You can defeat up to 10,000 monsters within your level range per week. If you defeat all 10,000 monsters, you will also accumulate that week’s bonus rewards. Bonus RewardQuantityVIP Booster1Autumn Flower Bookmark5 You can claim the accumulated VIP Boosters and VIP Sauna Entry Tickets during the event period by clicking the ‘Receive’ button in the UI. ※ The VIP Booster and VIP Sauna Entry Ticket accumulation and claim status are shared between all characters within the Maple ID. The claimed items can only be put into storage. ※ VIP Sauna Entry Tickets can only be used during the ‘VIP Sauna’ event period. After the event period ends, the effects will no longer apply even if you have remaining Sauna Entry Ticket time. ※ If you have claimed the weekly bonus rewards 3 times or more, you can click the ‘Receive’ button that appears on the right side of the UI to acquire the ‘Autumn Coloured Display Case’. ※ The Autumn Coloured Display Case can be claimed after creating a Maple Home. Upon claiming it, the furniture will be instantly added to your Maple Home. ※ Maple Home requires a level 260 or higher character that has completed ‘The Day After’ story within the world. You can then complete the ‘[Maple Home] Spiegelmann’s Super Gift’ quest through the lightbulb icon to create a Maple Home. ※ Maple Home is shared between all characters within the world. The furniture acquired from the Voucher can be used by all characters within the Maple ID. Autumn Flower Bookmarks You can use your accumulated Autumn Flower Bookmarks by clicking the ‘Open Store’ button in the UI or through the ‘Trendy Store’, ‘Steady Store’, and ‘Accessory Store’ located in Henesys. You can also accumulate additional Autumn Flower Bookmarks by buying Autumn Flower Bookmark Vouchers for 100m mesos each through the ‘Buy’ button in the UI. ※ You can buy up to 4,000 Autumn Flower Bookmark Vouchers per Maple ID. They are untradeable. ※ Unused Autumn Flower Bookmark Vouchers can be resold for 100m mesos each through the Shop UI. (Permanent) Voucher (1 coin) [limit of 20] Chairs Piping Hot Steamed Bun Chair (1 coin) [limit of 20] Soap Bubble Chair (1 coin) [limit of 20] Spring Train Station Chair (1 coin) [limit of 20] Exploration Rocket Chair (1 coin) [limit of 20] Discovery Campfire Chair (1 coin) [limit of 20] Newtro Henesys Chair (1 coin) [limit of 20] Real Time Search Chair (1 coin) [limit of 20] Yeti Game Gear Chair (1 coin) [limit of 20] I’m The Main Character Chair (1 coin) [limit of 20] Duel of Light and Lightning (1 coin) [limit of 20] Maple Warrior’s Throne (1 coin) [limit of 20] Special Teddy Family Voucher (1 coin) [limit of 20] Wanna Be Maple Idol! Voucher (1 coin) [limit of 20] Glorion Gold Insignia Voucher (1 coin) [limit of 20] Warm Wave Voucher (2 coins) [limit of 20] Maple Leaf’s Blessing Voucher (2 coins) [limit of 20] Lucid Chatting Emoticons (2 coins) [limit of 20] Rock Spirit Chatting Emoticons (2 coins) [limit of 20] Small Spirit Chatting Emoticons (2 coins) [limit of 20] [Back to Overview] [Halloween Event] Night of the Yetis A hunt between monster Yetis and hunters! Survive the spooky night in El Nath and acquire various rewards! Event Participation Requirements: Level 101 or higher characters (Zero characters that have completed Chapter 2). Event Period: October 23, 2025 after the maintenance ~ November 5, 2025 at 11:59 PM. ※ During the event period, Henesys, Nameless Town, Cernium Square, and City of Researchers will be decorated for Halloween. You can select ‘[Halloween Event] Night of the Yetis’ in the Event List and click the ‘Participate’ button to enter the <Monster Yeti Hunter Temporary Outpost>. You can participate in <Night of the Yetis> through Quick Start or Party Play by talking to Scadur. If you choose Quick Start, you can choose your desired role between hunter and Yeti to start matching. ※ If you choose a role and matching is not complete within 30 seconds, you may be matched as the other role. ※ You can proceed in Quick Start without a party, and you will play with 4 other players that applied through Quick Start. If you enter as a party, the party leader will always be the hunter. ※ You can proceed in Party Play in a party of 2-5 players. ※ Hunting Points and number of wins will not be accumulated in Party Play. ※ The Monster Yeti Hunter Temporary Outpost is a cross-world waiting room, so you can form parties with players from other worlds. Night of the Yetis Night of the Yetis is a survival game where the roles of surviving and hunting are reversed based on the time of day. 5 players, matched cross-world, are assigned the roles of 1 hunter and 4 monster Yetis. You can use WASD to move, SPACE to jump, and the mouse/keyboard to use skills. The game is divided into daytime and nighttime, and will begin in daytime. Night Energy: The game begins at 0 Night Energy. When it reaches 300, it will become nighttime. ※ Night Energy will increase periodically. ※ The hunter cannot use skills during nighttime. ※ At nighttime, the monster Yetis transform into giant Yetis, and their skills will change. ※ When the hunter uses the Shoot skill, it will increase if they miss a monster Yeti and decrease if they successfully hunt a monster Yeti. The hunter can win by finding and hunting all of the monster Yetis that are mimicking the Dark Junior Yetis during the daytime, or by reaching the hideout during the nighttime. The monster Yetis can win by surviving until nighttime and either hunting the hunter or if the hunter fails to reach the hideout within the time limit. ※ At nighttime, the hunter’s hideout located furthest from the hunter’s current location will be activated. The hunter can win if they reach the hideout within the time limit. Monster Yetis The monster Yetis can win by surviving until nighttime and either hunting the hunter or if the hunter fails to reach the hideout within the time limit. The monster Yetis can mimic Dark Junior Yetis by using the Head Scratch and Hide Legs skills. Head Scratch: This skill mimics the Dark Junior Yeti’s actions. Hide Legs: This skill hides the monster Yeti’s legs. While they are hidden, your speed and jump are decreased. You can use the skill again to unhide your legs. If your Hunger reaches 100, your legs will be revealed. ※ Head Scratch can only be used while your legs are hidden. ※ Hide Legs can only be used when you have 1 or higher hunger. ※ The usable skills will change based on the monster Yeti’s legs. Hunger: You start at 100 hunger, and it decreases over time. If your hunger reaches 0, the monster Yeti’s legs will be revealed and you cannot use the Hide Legs skill. Eat Food: When used near food, you can eet it to replenish your hunger. Dash: This skill lets you jump a set distance forward. ※ When you use Eat Food, your current location will be briefly displayed to the hunter. ※ Dash can only be used when your legs are not hidden. Skill NameKeyCooldownDescriptionMoveWASDNone–JumpSPACENone–Head ScratchLeft-click1 secondScratch your head. This skill can only be used when your legs are hidden.Hide LegsRight-click1 secondHide your legs. If you do so, your speed is decreased. If you use the skill again or your hunger reaches 0, your legs will be revealed.Eat FoodE5 secondsWhen used near food, you can eat it to replenish your hunger. When you eat food, your location is revealed to the hunter.DashSHIFT5 secondsThis skill can only be used if your legs are not hidden. Consumes hunger when used.Fart!Q3 secondsRelease a fart sound. Giant Monster Yetis The monster Yetis will transform into Giant Monster Yetis when it is nighttime. Their usable skills will change. You can win by either hunting the hunter or if the hunter fails to reach the hideout within the time limit. Skill NameKeyCooldownDescriptionMoveWASDNone–JumpSPACENone–ScratchLeft-click1 secondScratch in front of you.ChaseSHIFT5 secondsIncrease your speed for a certain period of time.RoarQ3 secondsLet out a roar. Hunter The hunter can win by finding and hunting all of the monster Yetis that are mimicking the Dark Junior Yetis during the daytime, or by reaching the hideout during the nighttime. The hunter can use their Shoot and Surveillance Camera skills to find and hunt the monster Yetis. ※ For the Shoot skills, you can aim with the mouse cursor and attack the targeted location with the left mouse button. The Surveillance Camera skill briefly reveals the locations of monster Yetis that pass by their installed range without their legs hidden. ※ You can install up to 5 surveillance cameras per game. After installing them, you cannot retrieve them. ※ Once a surveillance camera is installed, it will remain for the rest of the daytime. Skill NameKeyCooldownDescriptionMoveWASDNone–JumpSPACENone–ShootMouse left clickNoneAttack at the location of the mouse cursor.Surveillance CameraSHIFT1 secondInstall a camera that reveals monster Yetis that have not hidden their legs. (5) Rewards If you win a game, you will receive 500 Hunting Points and accumulate your number of wins. If you lose, you will receive 250 Hunting Points. ※ If you don’t play for a certain period of time or are the first person to leave an in-progress game, you will receive a penalty preventing you from being able to participate in matchmaking for a set period of time. You can acquire up to 1,000 Hunting Points per day and up to 3,000 Hunting Points per week. ※ The weekly cap will reset every Thursday at midnight. You can acquire various rewards based on your accumulated Hunting Points and number of wins. ※ The event progress is shared between all characters within the maple ID. ConditionRewardQuantityTradeabilityDurationReach 1,000 Hunting PointsSelective Hallowcat’s Hat Voucher1Can only be put into storage.7 DaysReach 2,000 Hunting PointsSelective Magic Academy Purple School Uniform Box17 DaysReach 3,000 Hunting PointsNight Cat Damage Skin17 DaysReach 4,000 Hunting PointsSelective Mystic Rose Outfit Box17 DaysReach 5,000 Hunting PointsMonster Yetiroid Voucher17 DaysReach 6,000 Hunting PointsGiant Monster Yeti Riding Voucher17 DaysReach 10 wins as the hunterHunter’s Hideout Chair1Permanent ※ The Monster Yetiroid Voucher includes a Lidium Heart. ※ The items acquired from the Selective Hallowcat’s Hat Voucher, Selective Magic Academy Purple School Uniform Box, and Selective Mystic Rose Outfit Box are permanent and untradeable items. ※ The Lidium Heart from the Monster Yetiroid Voucher is permanent and untradeable, the Monster Yetiroid is permanent and can only be put into storage. ※ The Riding acquired from the Giant Monster Yeti Riding Voucher is permanent and can only be used on the character that you use it on. ※ All rewards can only be claimed once per Maple ID. ※ All rewards can only be claimed during the event period. ※ All rewards can be claimed during the event period by selecting the ‘Night of the Yetis’ in the Event List and clicking the ‘<Night of the Yetis> Receive Rewards’ option. [Back to Overview] Challengers World 4th Early Leap Event Participation Requirements: Level 130 or higher characters created in Challengers, Challengers 2, Challengers 3, or Challengers 4 worlds. ※ Zero characters that are level 130 or higher and have completed story quest Chapter 2 can Early Leap. Event Period: October 23, 2025 after the maintenance ~ November 19, 2025 at 11:59 PM. During the event period, you can leap your characters to your desired world through the NPC Isaga in Henesys. You can select the ‘Challengers World Early Leap’ event in the Event List and click the ‘Participate’ button to move to Henesys. ※ Worlds You can Leap To: All worlds. ※ You can move a total of 5 characters to other worlds through the Challengers World Early Leap and End Leap. Basic Hideout Tokens, Intermediate Hideout Tokens, Advanced Hideout Tokens, Challengers Coins, and Advanced Challengers Coins acquired in Challengers World will not be moved with the World Leap. Challengers World Bronze/Silver/Gold/Platinum rewards that can be acquired from Challengers World missions can only be claimed in Challengers World. The Mystic Seal event’s equipped Seal Core stats will be removed. Maple Hideout Special Menu’s skill levels will be applied based on the world you arrive in. If a character designated as the Manager or Crew in Hideout Duo leaps to a world that wasn’t selected, the character designation information will be removed. In addition, Hideout Duo events cannot be progressed in that world. However, you can continue the event by re-designating a character in the selected world or the world where the character was created. Characters with active Meso Market transactions cannot proceed with World Leap. When World Leaping from Challengers World, items in the cash storage, storage, and Maple Auction will not be moved. Custom Backgrounds acquired using Voucher items will not be moved. ※ Custom Backgrounds acquired using Voucher items can be extracted into Vouchers through the NPC Isaga’s ‘Extract Custom Background’ option. The acquired Voucher can only be put into storage and is permanent. The Tera Burning effect applied when a character is created in Challengers World will not be transferred through World Leap. ※ The Tera Burning effect will be removed when the character is transferred, and the ‘Tera Burning Gift Giveaway!’ quest will no longer be available. The Strange Cube, Karma Meister Cube, Karma Strange Additional Cube, and Karma Star Force 17 Star Enhancement Scroll (Level 160) purchased from the Challengers Shop are Challengers World-only items and you cannot World Leap while holding them. The Selective Challengers Level 4 Special Skill Ring Voucher and the Special Skill Ring acquired from using it are Challengers World-only items and you cannot World Leap while holding them. If a character that has cleared an Epic Dungeon uses World Leap, you cannot claim the cleared Epic Dungeon’s bonus rewards. If there are no empty character slots in the world you are moving to, slots will be added before the Leap. However, if you already have 50 characters in the destination world, you cannot Leap to that world. [Back to Overview] Sunday Maple Event Period: October 26, 2025 November 2, 2025 November 9, 2025 November 16, 2025 On October 26, the following benefits will take place: When your [HEXA Stat] Main Stat is level 5 or higher, the Main Stat’s enhancement rate is increased by 20%. Rune respawn and cooldown decreased to 10 minutes. Rune experience buff effect +100% Treasure Hunter experience 3x Combo Kill experience acquired +300% On November 2, the following benefits will take place: 30% discount on Star Force enhancement costs 50% discount on Spell Trace enhancement costs On November 9, the following benefits will take place: Monster Park clear experience +250% 50% discount on Ability reset costs Monster Collection new monster registration chance +100% Receive 3 Mystery Monster Eggs Number of Elite Monsters summoned +2 On November 16, the following benefits will take place: Shining Star Force Time 30% discount on Star Force enhancement costs When Star Forcing at 21 stars or less, destruction chance -30% [Back to Overview]
